item.title: plusOne Javascript 中的整数数组 | plusOne Array of Integers in Javascript
item.description:
发表于将大整数加一并返回结果数字数组。

例子：

输入：digits = [1,2,3] 输出：[1,2,4] 解释：数组表示整数 123。加一得到 123 + 1 = 124。

因此，结果应该是 [1,2,4]。

输入：digits = [9] 输出：[1,0] 解释：数组表示整数 9。加一得到 9 + 1 = 10。

因此，结果应该是 [1,0]。

这是我的实现：

 function plusOne(digits: number[]): number[] { let n = digits.length; for(let i=n-1; i>=0; i--) { if(digits[i] < 9) { digits[i]++; return digits; } digits[i] = 0; } let newArr = Array.from({length: n+1}, () => 0); newArr[0] = 1; return newArr; };

如果您有更简化和更好的解决方案，请提供。

谢谢。

皮埃尔-亨利·索里亚✨ - Apr 8 4月8日



原文:

Vishwak ❤️ JS

Posted on Apr 30

Increment the large integer by one and return the resulting array of digits.

Example:

Input: digits = [1,2,3] Output: [1,2,4] Explanation: The array represents the integer 123. Incrementing by one gives 123 + 1 = 124.

Thus, the result should be [1,2,4].

Input: digits = [9] Output: [1,0] Explanation: The array represents the integer 9. Incrementing by one gives 9 + 1 = 10.

Thus, the result should be [1,0].

Here is the my implementation:

function plusOne(digits: number[]): number[] {
   let n = digits.length;
    for(let i=n-1; i>=0; i--) {
        if(digits[i] < 9) {
            digits[i]++; return digits;
        }
        digits[i] = 0;
    }

    let newArr = Array.from({length: n+1}, () => 0);
    newArr[0] = 1;
    return newArr;
};

Please provide if you have more simplified and better solution.

Thanks.

Pierre-Henry Soria ✨ - Apr 8
item.author: urstrulyvishwak

item.title: Javascript 中的 maxSubArraySum | maxSubArraySum in Javascript
item.description:
发表于我们需要找到总和最大的连续子数组和。

例子：

输入：nums = [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4] 输出：6 解释：[4,-1,2,1] 的最大和 = 6。

这是代码：

 function maxSubArray(nums) { let max = -Infinity; let sum = 0; for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { sum = sum + nums[i]; if (max < sum) max = sum; if (sum < 0) sum = 0; } return max; };

请提供您拥有的任何简化解决方案。

谢谢。

Juhana Jauhiainen - Juhana Jauhiainen - Apr 21 4月21日



原文:

Vishwak ❤️ JS

Posted on Apr 30

We need to find the contiguous subarray sum which has the largest sum.

Example:

_

Input: nums = [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4] Output: 6 Explanation: [4,-1,2,1] has the largest sum = 6.

_

Here is the code:

function maxSubArray(nums) {
    let max = -Infinity;
    let sum = 0;
    for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) {
        sum = sum + nums[i];
        if (max < sum) max = sum;
        if (sum < 0) sum = 0;
    }
    return max;
};

Please provide any simplified solution you have.

Thanks.

Juhana Jauhiainen - Apr 21
item.author: urstrulyvishwak

item.title: 使用 Python 和机器学习检测假新闻。 | Detecting Fake News with Python and Machine Learning.
item.description:
杰森·奥蒙迪

发表于嗨👋！ 很高兴认识你们开发者😄。 在这里，我们将讨论如何使用 python 来检测假新闻。 本文旨在引导您完成使用 python 和 NLP 创建机器学习模型以成功检测假新闻的过程。
您将需要了解 Python 的基础知识、数据结构和算法、Python 库（如 Pandas、NumPy）以及数据分析中的方法（例如被动攻击分类器）。

关键术语

准备我们的数据集和工作环境首先，我们需要安装支持的python版本。 为此，您可以转到浏览器并搜索“下载 python 3”，然后在下载后安装。 这将因您将在计算机上使用的操作系统类型而异。 数据集

获取合适的数据集是任何数据科学工作中最重要的组成部分之一。 这些数据集可以在 Kaggle 网站上访问。

编码

现在在安装 python、库和数据集之后，是时候启动我们的文本编辑器（您可以使用 Jupyter notebook）并开始导入您需要的库。 我们现在可以查看前四个记录。 使用 Python，我们可以看到数据集分为以下列：id、title、author、text 和 label。 我们感兴趣的特征是标签和文本列。 文本栏包含文章的内容，而标签栏代表文章是否真实。 这是使用'1'（'REAL'）和'0'（'FAKE'）以二进制形式为我们预先制作的。
现在要初始化 PassiveAggressiveClassifier 以便将其合并到模型中，我们将使用“y_train”和“tfidf_train”。 

 pa_classifier=PassiveAggressiveClassifier(max_iter=50) pa_classifier.fit (tfidf_train, y_train)

最后，使用向量器可以预测文章是否可靠，我们将计算模型的准确性。 

 y_pred=pa_classifier.predict(tfidf_test) score=accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred) print(f'Accuracy: {round(score*100,2)} %')

您现在可以看到模型在进行测试时的准确性。 尽管您查看准确性，但您可能不知道成功预测和失败的数量。 为了访问此类信息，您可以使用混淆矩阵并得出结论。 这可以通过以下方式轻松完成：

confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred, labels=['FAKE','REAL'])

结论。

为了在这些困难时期有效打击错误信息，进一步推进此类模型至关重要。

迈克尔·米罗斯尼琴科 - Apr 29 4月29日



原文:

Jason Omondi

Posted on Apr 30

Hi 👋! Nice to meet you devs 😄. Here, we will talk about how we can use python to detect fake news. This article aims to walk you through the process of creating a machine learning model using python and NLP to successfully detect fake news.
You will need to know the fundamentals of Python, data structures and algorithms, python libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, and Methods in Data Analysis for example Passive Aggressive Classifiers.

Key Terms

• TfidfVectorizer • IDF (Inverse Document Frequency) • TF (Term Frequency)

• Passive Aggressive Classifier

Preparing our dataset and work environment First, we need to install a supported version of python. To do so, you can go to your browser and search ‘download python 3’ and then install after downloading. This will vary with the type of Operating system you will be using on your machine. Dataset

Acquiring a suitable dataset presents one of the most crucial components of any data-science endeavor. These Data sets can be accessed on the Kaggle website.

Coding

Now after installing python, the libraries, and the dataset too, it is time to launch our text editors (you can use Jupyter notebook) and begin importing the libraries you need. We can now view the first four records. Using Python, we can see that the data-set is divided into the following columns: id, title, author, text, and label. The features that are of interest to us are the label and text columns. The text column contains the contents of the article, whereas the label column represents whether the article is factual or not. This has been pre-made for us in binary form using ‘1’(‘REAL’) and ‘0’(‘FAKE’).
The PassiveAggressiveClassifier is to now be initialized in order to incorporate it into the model, we are going to use the “y_train” and “tfidf_train”.

pa_classifier=PassiveAggressiveClassifier(max_iter=50)
pa_classifier.fit (tfidf_train, y_train)

Finally, using the vectorizer you can predict whether an article is reliable or not and we are going to calculate our model’s accuracy.

y_pred=pa_classifier.predict(tfidf_test)
score=accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)
print(f'Accuracy: {round(score*100,2)} %')

You can now see the accuracy the model had while conducting its tests. Although you view the accuracy, you might not know the number of successful predictions and failures. In order to access such information, you can use a confusion matrix and draw conclusions. This can be easily done by:

confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred, labels=['FAKE','REAL'])

Conclusion.

It is essential that such models are further advanced in order to effectively combat misinformation in these difficult times.

Michael Mirosnichenko - Apr 29
item.author: jasonomondi

item.title: 一个关于机器学习平台的全能开源项目的设计理念 | The design concept of an almighty Opensource project about machine learning platform
item.description:
在我之前的文章“ 你应该尝试的机器学习全能开源项目”中，我介绍了 MetaSpore。 在本文中，我将详细介绍MetaSpore的设计理念。 下周我会更新使用MetaSpore快速搭建工业推荐系统的详细步骤。
本文揭示了MetaSpore的核心设计理念。

说到机器学习，人们往往会想到各种机器学习框架，比如TensorFlow、PyTorch等。很多模型在GitHub上实现Python代码。 但是，算法模型在具体业务场景下的实现还有很多困难。_以推荐系统为例，算法模型在具体业务场景下的实现会遇到以下问题：_

  • 这些过程在机器学习框架中通常是不可能的，因此需要一个大数据系统来处理批量或流式数据。
  • 那么如何处理数据碎片化、变长特性等问题常常困扰着算法工程师。
  • 如何训练稀疏特征模型并使用NLP预训练模型？ 在推荐场景中，我们需要能够大规模处理稀疏特征来建模用户和商品之间的有趣关系。 同时，多模态模型融合逐渐成为前沿方向。
  • 模型训练后如何高效进行在线预测？ 除了集群资源，还涉及到弹性调度和负载均衡。 这些问题需要在具有 CPU、GPU 和 NPUS 的异构环境中进行动态资源分配。 对于复杂模型，需要蒸馏、量化和其他方法来确保其预测性能。
  • 模型上线后，在线系统如何提取拼接特征，保证离线特征的一致性，量化评估算法效果？ 需要一个可以与在线系统集成的在线算法应用框架，可以读取各种在线数据源，并提供多层ABTest流量实验功能。
  • 最后，如何在算法实验中进行高效的迭代？ 程序员希望快速运行多个并行实验以提高业务性能，而不是陷入复杂的系统环境配置中。 在大型互联网工厂中，多个团队和不同系统经常解决这些问题。 例如，下图来自 Netflix 算法工程团队分享的推荐系统整体架构： 从上图可以看出，构建一个完整的工业级推荐系统是相当复杂和繁琐的，需要大量不同领域的知识和工程开发的投入。 中小企业缺乏标准化解决这些问题的人员配备和一站式平台。

满足企业和开发者获取算法业务开发最佳实践的需求。 具体而言，MetaSpore 具有以下核心功能设计理念：

  • 模型训练与大数据系统无缝集成，可直接读取各类数据湖、仓库的结构化和非结构化数据进行训练。 数据特征预处理和模型训练无缝衔接在一起，省去了繁琐的数据导入导出和格式转换。
  • 支持稀疏特征。 搜索和泛化场景需要大规模的稀疏嵌入层训练。 涉及到一些稀疏特征的处理，比如交叉组合、变长特征池化等，需要训练框架的特殊支持。
  • 提供高性能的在线预测服务。 在线预测服务支持神经网络（包括稀疏嵌入）、决策树和各种传统机器学习模型。 支持异构硬件计算加速，降低在线部署的工程门槛。
  • 统一离线特征计算。 通过统一的特征格式和计算逻辑，统一的离线特征计算节省了多个系统的重复开发，保证了离线特征的一致性。
  • 在线算法应用框架。 在线算法应用框架涵盖在线系统常用功能点，如多数据源自动特征提取、特征计算、预测服务接口、实验动态配置、ABTest动态切割流等。
  • 拥抱开源。 同时，MetaSpore 的开发理念是尽可能的拥抱成熟的开源生态，不去碎片化，降低学习门槛，让开发者能够根据现有经验快速开发。

例如，推荐广告的CTR估计和财务风险控制很常见。 在这种算法的实践中，对数据进行预处理非常重要。 数据的质量直接关系到最终的模型效果。 常见的数据处理包括特征拼接、空值填充、离散桶划分、异常值过滤、特征工程以及样本生成如验证集划分和随机shuffle。 这些步骤在工程实践中往往依赖大数据系统进行处理，而深度学习框架缺乏全面的大数据处理功能。 在这种情况下，数据和训练连接会出现很多问题。 大数据系统有多种存储格式和系统，深度学习框架难以一一采用。 传统上，算法工程师需要自己处理数据格式转换，将大数据系统的格式转换为深度学习框架可以识别的格式。 这不仅麻烦，还会产生数据冗余问题，而且实现增量流式学习也具有挑战性。 它还支持各种数据存储、数据湖（如 Hive）和流数据（如 Kafka）。 通过与 PySpark 对接，MetaSpore 可以直接训练 Spark 可以支持的所有数据源，大大简化了数据处理的 Pipeline。 在实现方面，MetaSpore 充分利用了 PySpark 提供的 PandasUDF 功能。 模型训练结果，例如预测值，也会以 Arrow 格式发送回 Spark，形成一个新的 DataFrame。 看一个代码示例： 

 # Defining Neural Networks module = metaspore.algos.MLP() # Create a PyTorch Distributed Training Estimator estimator = metaspore.PyTorchEstimator(module) # Read training and validation sets via Spark train_df = spark_session.read.csv('hdfs://movielens/train/') test_df = spark_session.read.csv('hdfs://movielens/test/') # Model training on the training set model = estimator.fit(train_df) # Use the trained model to make predictions on the test set test_prediction_result = model.transform(test_df) # The predicted result is Spark DataFrame，Operations that can be invoked on any DataFrame evaluator = pyspark.ml.evaluation.BinaryClassificationEvaluator() auc = evaluator.evaluate(test_prediction_result) print(auc)

您可以看到 MetaSpore 与 Spark 无缝集成。 中间读取的 CSV 可以替换为 Spark 支持的任意数据源，在 Spark 中经过复杂的数据处理后训练模型。

该特征通常使用 one-hot Encoding 转换为固定 ID，并映射到 Vector 作为学习参数。 在这种情况下，会出现两种问题。 一是特征ID的空间非常大，通常在亿级以上； 二是ID空间不固定，一些离散特征的值类型会不断变化。 针对第二个问题，MetaSpore提供了一套完整的动态稀疏特征学习方案，通过hash Table存储Embedding Table，实现Embedding Vector的添加和去除。 将原有的离散特征值散列、多值组合、多值池化集成定义为推荐场景的PyTorch Module。 使用 MetaSpore 定义一个稀疏 MLP 模型，如下所示：

 class MLPLayer(torch.nn.Module): def __init__(self, ...): super().__init__() self.sparse = metaspore.EmbeddingSumConcat(deep_embedding_dim, deep_column_name_path, deep_combine_schema_path) dense_layers=[] dnn_linear_num=len(hidden_lay_unit) dense_layers.append(metaspore.nn.Normalization(feature_dim*embedding_size)) dense_layers.append(torch.nn.Linear(feature_dim*embedding_size, hidden_lay_unit[0])) dense_layers.append(torch.nn.ReLU()) for i in range(dnn_linear_num - 2): dense_layers.append(torch.nn.Linear(hidden_lay_unit[i], hidden_lay_unit[i + 1])) dense_layers.append(torch.nn.ReLU()) dense_layers.append(torch.nn.Linear(hidden_lay_unit[-2], hidden_lay_unit[-1])) self.dnn = torch.nn.Sequential(*dense_layers) def forward(self, input_raw_strings): sparse = self.sparse(input_raw_strings) return self.dnn(sparse)

在上面的代码中，通过创建一个 EmbeddingSumConcat 层，您可以接受原始字符串类型的样本输入，自动散列、组合、池化（默认 Sum）和用于 forward 方法的 input_raw_strings。 它是由 PySpark（一个 Pandas DataFrame 对象）读取的一批表数据。 与 TorchRec 等推荐系统深度学习框架相比，API 层面简化，调用逻辑更直接、更易理解。

与 TFServing 不同，MetaSpore Serving 具有以下特点：

  • 支持更广泛的模型。 除了离线训练框架生成的稀疏NN模型，MetaSpore Serving还支持NLP、CV等NN模型，XGBoost、LightGBM等决策树模型。 以及 Spark ML、SKLearn 和其他机器学习库模型。
  • 集成特征处理逻辑。 特征计算是模型Serving的一个组成部分，如稀疏离散特征处理逻辑、NLP中的Tokenizer等。传统上，这些逻辑是在两个离线集合中实现的，效率低下，容易出现逻辑不一致等错误。 支持CPU、GPU、NPU等异构硬件加速。 模型预测通常需要选择不同的硬件在不同的场景下进行计算。 在 MetaSpore Serving 中，使用 OnnxRuntime 作为计算库。 并且特征处理被用作预测处理逻辑。 每个模型的预测计算，包括几个特征表计算和最终的 Onnx 模型计算，形成一个 DAG 计算图。 例如，在一个典型的 Wide&Deep 模型中，Serving 处的计算逻辑可以大致表示为：

    通过这种方式，MetaSpore Serving 可以轻松集成各种特征提取和计算模块，最大限度地与离线逻辑对齐，简化开发体验。

    对于 Table 类模型（稀疏或密集），MetaSpore 以 Arrow Table 作为离线输入，将特征计算逻辑表达为 Arrow Compute 的执行计划。 表达式图评估每个特征。 对于离散特征，支持散列和多值组合。 对于密集特征，支持归一化、二值化和桶拆分。 大部分计算可以直接使用 Arrow 的内置表达式来完成。 对于没有内置计算的 Arrow，您可以使用自定义计算内核注册自己的表达式。

    在实现方面，离线使用的特征操作的表达被序列化并保存在模型导出的目录中。 在线预测服务加载表达式后构造相同的 Arrow Compute 表达式，以便离线共享完全一致的特征计算。 此外，由于 Arrow Compute 支持过滤、连接等操作，LakeSoul 也支持输入到输入批次。 得益于Arrow列表达式的设计，这些计算都是向量化的，可以有比较高的性能。

    离线特征计算和离线输入计算是统一的箭头表格式。 例如，对于具有十个浮点类型特征输入的 XGBoost 模型，LakeSoul 用 Python 实现了一个调用 MetaSpore 预测服务的程序：

     import grpc import metaspore_pb2 import metaspore_pb2_grpc import pyarrow as pa with grpc.insecure_channel('0.0.0.0:50051') as channel: stub = metaspore_pb2_grpc.PredictStub(channel) # Construct a one-line, 10-feature sample row = [] values = [0.6558618,0.13005558,0.03510657,0.23048967,0.63329154,0.43201634,0.5795548,0.5384891,0.9612295,0.39274803] for i in range(10): row.append(pa.array([values[i]], type=pa.float32())) # Create Arrow RecordBatch rb = pa.RecordBatch.from_arrays(row, [f'field_{i}' for i in range(10)]) # Serialization RecordBatch sink = pa.BufferOutputStream() with pa.ipc.new_file(sink, rb.schema) as writer: writer.write_batch(rb) # Construct GRPC request for MetaSpore Serving payload_map = {"input": sink.getvalue().to_pybytes()} request = metaspore_pb2.PredictRequest(model_name="xgboost_model", payload=payload_map) # Call Serving service prediction reply = stub.Predict(request) for name in reply.payload: with pa.BufferReader(reply.payload[name]) as reader: tensor = pa.ipc.read_tensor(reader) print(f'Predict Output Tensor: {tensor.to_numpy()}')

    可以看出，在线预测也是输入特征的Arrow Table，可以直接调用MetaSpore Serving。 调用方式也统一离线。

    离线培训框架和在线 Serving 服务现已推出。 那么，在业务场景落地的一个算法，还是最后一步：在线算法实验。 在业务场景中，为了验证算法模型的有效性，需要建立基线并与新的算法模型进行比较。 因此，需要一个能够轻松定义算法实验、读取在线特征、调用模型预测服务的在线实验框架。 此外，还可以对多个实验进行流量分割，实现ABTest效果对比。 还需要一个配置中心来快速进行多次实验迭代，可以动态加载刷新实验和切流配置，支持实验参数热加载，以及各种调试和跟踪功能。 这个环节也直接决定了AI模型能否最终落地到实际的商业应用中。

    该框架基于SpringBoot + Spring Cloud，提供以下核心功能：

    开发者可以通过简单的注解添加实验类：

     @ExperimentAnnotation(name = "rank.widedeep") @Component public class WideDeepRankExperiment implements BaseExperiment<RecommendResult, RecommendResult> { @Override public void initialize(Map<String, Object> map) {} @SneakyThrows @Override public RecommendResult run(Context context, RecommendResult recommendResult) {} }

    然后使用 Spring Cloud 配置中心动态组装多个实验流，包括水平实验的流分割和垂直实验的序列。 以推荐系统为例，召回层有两个实验，排序层有两个实验，并且两层是正交的，这样可以通过如下配置组装在线实验过程：

     scene-config: scenes: - name: guess-you-like layers: - name: match normalLayerArgs: - experimentName: match.base ratio: 0.3 - experimentName: match.multiple ratio: 0.7 - name: rank normalLayerArgs: - experimentName: rank.wideDeep ratio: 0.5 - experimentName: rank.lightGBM ratio: 0.5 experiments: - layerName: match experimentName: match.base extraExperimentArgs: modelName: match.base matcherNames: [ItemCfMatcher] - layerName: match experimentName: match.multiple extraExperimentArgs: modelName: match.multiple matcherNames: [ItemCfMatcher, SwingMatcher, TwoTowersMatcher] - layerName: rank experimentName: rank.wideDeep extraExperimentArgs: modelName: movie_lens_wdl ranker: WideAndDeepRanker maxReservation: 100 - layerName: rank experimentName: rank.lightGBM extraExperimentArgs: modelName: lightgbm_test_model ranker: LightGBMRanker maxReservation: 100

    在SpringBoot项目中添加配置和实验类实现后，框架会自动创建并初始化实验类的对象并按照实验层的顺序执行，并根据切向流配置。 开发人员可以自由定义实验之间的输入和输出，提供了高度的灵活性。 同时该配置文件放置在配置中心，支持动态热更新、修改实验配置、切割流配置，可实时生效。 但是，在线功能可以来自多个来源，通常来自各种数据库、缓存和上游服务。 例如，在离线训练方面，MetaSpore 的离线训练框架 Bridges PySpark 和 PyTorch 这两个大数据和深度学习的流行框架，将它们的生态更好地无缝结合，解决了真实业务场景的痛点。 线上服务还与gRPC、SpringBoot、Spring Cloud全面集成，让开发者在现有标准技术栈中获得大数据智能应用落地的最佳实践。

    比如对于离线NLP大模型训练，有需求的开发者仍然可以使用现有的开源框架，比如OneFlow，来训练模型； 本文详细介绍MetaSpore一站式机器学习平台的设计理念和核心特点，帮助读者更好地了解 MetaSpore。 想了解更多关于 MetaSpore 业务场景实现的信息。

    下周我会更新使用MetaSpore快速搭建工业推荐系统的详细步骤。



原文:

In my previous article, "Almighty Opensource project about machine learning you should try out", I introduced MetaSpore. In this article, I will introduce MetaSpore's design philosophy in detail. Next week, I will update the detailed steps of using MetaSpore to build industrial recommendation systems rapidly.
How did MetaSpore move its complex recommendation algorithms beyond the Internet giants to reach the vast majority of SMEs and developers? This article unveils MetaSpore's core design philosophy.

1.What is a one-stop machine learning platform
When it comes to machine learning, people tend to think of various machine learning frameworks, such as TensorFlow and PyTorch, etc. Many models implement Python code on GitHub. However, there are still a lot of difficulties in implementing the algorithm model in specific business scenarios._ Taking the recommendation system as an example, the following problems will be encountered in the implementation of the algorithm model in specific business scenarios:_

  • **How to generate training data (samples)? **In recommendation scenarios, it is often necessary to splice user feedback signals (exposure, click, etc.) with various feature sources, perform necessary feature cleaning and extraction, and divide data into verification sets, negative sampling, and other complex data processing. These processes are usually impossible in machine learning frameworks, so a big data system is needed to process bulk or streaming data.
  • **How to transfer training data generated by big data platforms to a deep learning framework? **Frameworks such as TensorFlow and PyTorch have their data input formats and corresponding DataLoader interfaces, requiring developers to parse the data. So how to deal with data fragmentation, variable-length characteristics, and other problems often puzzle algorithm engineers.
  • **How to run a distributed training that can freely schedule cluster resources, including GPUs? **This may require a dedicated operations team to manage machine learning-related hardware scheduling.
  • How to train the sparse feature model and use the NLP pre-training model? In the recommendation scenario, we need to be able to handle sparse features on a large scale to model the interesting relationship between users and goods. At the same time, multimodal model fusion has gradually become the frontier direction.
  • How to make online predictions efficiently after model training? In addition to cluster resources, elastic scheduling and load balancing are also involved. These problems require dynamic resource allocation in a heterogeneous environment with CPUs, GPUs, and NPUS. For Complex models, distillation, quantification, and other means are necessary to ensure their predictive performance.
  • After the model goes online, how does the online system extract splicing features, ensure the consistency of offline features, and evaluate the algorithm effect quantitatively? An online algorithm application framework that can integrate with an online system read all kinds of online data sources and provide a multi-layer ABTest traffic experiment function is necessary.
  • Finally, how to conduct efficient iteration in an algorithm experiment? Programmers want to run multiple, parallel experiments quickly to improve business performance rather than get bogged down in complex system environment configurations. Multiple teams and different systems often solve these problems in large Internet factories. For example, the following image is from the overall architecture of the recommendation system shared by Netflix's algorithm engineering team: As can be seen from the above figure that building a complete, industrial-grade recommendation system is quite complex and tedious, requiring considerable knowledge in different fields and investment in engineering development. SMEs lack the staffing and a one-stop platform to solve these problems standardized.

_2.MetaSpore's one-stop machine learning platform_
The original intention of DMetaSoul to develop and open-source MetaSpore is to help enterprises and developers solve all kinds of problems encountered in the process of algorithm business development based on MetaSpore features and provide a one-stop development experience by using standardized components and development interfaces. Meet the needs of enterprises and developers to obtain algorithmic business development best practices. Specifically, MetaSpore has the following core functional design concepts:

  • Model training is seamlessly integrated with the big data system, which can directly read the structured and unstructured data of all kinds of data lakes and warehouses for training. Data feature preprocessing, and model training are seamlessly linked together, saving the tedious data import and export and format conversion.
  • Support for sparse features. Large-scale sparse Embedding layer training is necessary for search and generalization scenarios. Some processing of sparse features is involved, such as cross combination, variable-length feature pooling, etc., which requires special support from the training framework.
  • Provide high-performance online forecasting services. Online prediction services support neural networks (including sparse Embedding), decision trees, and a variety of traditional machine learning models. Supports heterogeneous hardware computing acceleration, reducing the engineering threshold for online deployment.
  • Unified offline feature calculation. Through the unified feature format and calculation logic, the unified offline feature calculation saves the repeated development of multiple systems and ensures the consistency of offline features.
  • Online algorithm application framework. The online algorithm application framework covers the common function points of online systems, such as automatic feature extraction from multi-data sources, feature calculation, predictive service interface, experimental dynamic configuration, ABTest dynamic cutting flow, etc.
  • Embrace open source. The MetaSpore platform provides several homegrown components to implement these core function points. At the same time, MetaSpore's development philosophy is to embrace the mature open source ecosystem as much as possible without fragmentation, lowering the barriers to learning and enabling developers to develop based on their existing experience quickly.

MetaSpore integrates data, algorithms, and online systems to provide a one-stop, full-process development experience for algorithms through these core functional points and design concepts.

2.1 **MetaSpore's integration with big data ecology** A large class of machine learning algorithms deals with structured tabular data and conduct prediction, classification, regression, and modeling. For example, CTR estimation of recommended advertising and financial risk control are common. In the practice of this kind of algorithm, it is very important to preprocess data. The quality of the data is directly related to the final model effect. Common data processing includes feature splicing, null value filling, discrete bucket division, outlier filtering, feature engineering, and sample generation such as verification set division and random shuffle. These steps often rely on big data systems for processing in engineering practice, while deep learning frameworks lack comprehensive big data processing functions. There will be many problems with data and training connection in this case. Big data systems have multiple storage formats and systems, making it difficult for deep learning frameworks to adopt one by one. Traditionally, algorithmic engineers are required to process data format conversion by themselves, converting the format of big data systems into formats that deep learning frameworks can recognize. Not only is this cumbersome, it creates data redundancy problems, but it is also challenging to implement incremental streaming learning. MetaSpore provides an offline training framework using the Parameter Server architecture for such scenarios. The MetaSpore offline training framework integrates seamlessly with PySpark, allowing you to input the Spark DataFrame directly into model training with no format conversion required. Spark supports multiple data sources, including CSV, Parquet, and ORC. It also supports various data stores, data lakes, such as Hive, and streaming data such as Kafka. By docking with PySpark, MetaSpore can directly train all the data sources Spark can support, greatly simplifying the Pipeline for data processing. In terms of implementation, MetaSpore takes advantage of the PandasUDF functionality provided by PySpark. PandasUDF is a Python vectorization UDF interface provided by PySpark that uses Arrow as an efficient data transfer protocol from JVM to Python. The Worker end of the MetaSpore training framework is a PandasUDF, which receives a batch of data and sends it to the model training module. The model training results, such as predicted values, are also sent back to Spark in Arrow format to form a new DataFrame. Take a look at a code example:

# Defining Neural Networks
module = metaspore.algos.MLP()
# Create a PyTorch Distributed Training Estimator
estimator = metaspore.PyTorchEstimator(module)
# Read training and validation sets via Spark
train_df = spark_session.read.csv('hdfs://movielens/train/')
test_df = spark_session.read.csv('hdfs://movielens/test/')
# Model training on the training set
model = estimator.fit(train_df)
# Use the trained model to make predictions on the test set
test_prediction_result = model.transform(test_df)
# The predicted result is Spark DataFrame，Operations that can be invoked on any DataFrame
evaluator = pyspark.ml.evaluation.BinaryClassificationEvaluator()
auc = evaluator.evaluate(test_prediction_result)
print(auc)

You can see that MetaSpore integrates seamlessly with Spark. The read CSV in the middle can be replaced by any data source supported by Spark, and the model is trained after complex data processing in Spark.

2.2 **MetaSpore's support for sparse discrete feature training** A large class of features is sparse and discrete in search, advertising, recommendation, and other personalized recommendation scenarios. This feature is usually converted to a fixed ID using one-hot Encoding and mapped to a Vector as a learning parameter. In this scenario, there will be two kinds of problems. One is that the space of feature ID is very large, usually over 100 million level; the other is that the ID space is not fixed, and the value types of some discrete features will change constantly. MetaSpore provides a Parameter Server architecture to split the Embedding Table for the first problem. To solve the second problem, MetaSpore provides a complete set of dynamic sparse feature learning schemes, storing Embedding Table through hash Table and realizing the addition and removal of Embedding Vector. The original discrete eigenvalue hash, multi-value combination, and multi-value pooling are integrated and defined as a PyTorch Module for the recommendation scenario. Define a sparse MLP model using MetaSpore as follows:

class MLPLayer(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, ...):
        super().__init__()
        self.sparse = metaspore.EmbeddingSumConcat(deep_embedding_dim,
                                           deep_column_name_path,
                                           deep_combine_schema_path)
        dense_layers=[]
        dnn_linear_num=len(hidden_lay_unit)
        dense_layers.append(metaspore.nn.Normalization(feature_dim*embedding_size))
        dense_layers.append(torch.nn.Linear(feature_dim*embedding_size, hidden_lay_unit[0]))
        dense_layers.append(torch.nn.ReLU())
        for i in range(dnn_linear_num - 2):
            dense_layers.append(torch.nn.Linear(hidden_lay_unit[i], hidden_lay_unit[i + 1]))
            dense_layers.append(torch.nn.ReLU())
        dense_layers.append(torch.nn.Linear(hidden_lay_unit[-2], hidden_lay_unit[-1]))
        self.dnn = torch.nn.Sequential(*dense_layers)

    def forward(self, input_raw_strings):
        sparse = self.sparse(input_raw_strings)
        return self.dnn(sparse)

In the above code, by creating an EmbeddingSumConcat layer, you can accept sample input of raw string type, automatically hash, combine, pooling (default Sum), and input_raw_strings for the forward method. It is a batch of table data read by PySpark (a Pandas DataFrame object). Compared with TorchRec and other recommendation system deep learning frameworks, the API level is simplified, and the call logic is more direct and easy to understand.

2.3 **MetaSpore model Serving service**
The MetaSpore platform integrates online model Serving (prediction) services. Unlike TFServing, MetaSpore Serving has the following characteristics:

  • Support a wider range of models. In addition to sparse NN models generated by the offline training framework, MetaSpore Serving also supports NLP, CV, and other NN models, XGBoost, LightGBM, and other decision tree models. And Spark ML, SKLearn, and other machine learning library models.
  • Integrate feature processing logic. Feature calculation is an integral part of model Serving, such as sparse discrete feature processing logic, Tokenizer in NLP, etc. Traditionally, these logics are implemented in two offline sets, which are inefficient and prone to logical inconsistencies and other errors. MetaSpore Serving integrates the computational logic for sparse features and shares a code offline.
  • Supports heterogeneous hardware acceleration, such as CPU, GPU, and NPU. Model prediction usually requires selecting different hardware to perform calculations in different scenarios. MetaSpore Serving is capable of supporting common hardware for heterogeneous computing.

In MetaSpore Serving, OnnxRuntime is used as the computational library. And feature processing is used as prediction processing logic. The prediction calculation of each model, including several feature table calculations and the final Onnx model calculation, forms a DAG calculation graph. For example, in a typical Wide&Deep model, the computational logic at Serving can be roughly expressed as follows:

In this way, MetaSpore Serving can easily integrate various feature extraction and calculation modules, maximizing alignment with offline logic and simplifying the development experience.

2.4 **MetaSpore is uniformly calculated in offline feature**
For the Table class model (sparse or dense), MetaSpore takes Arrow Table as input offline and expresses the feature calculation logic as an execution plan of Arrow Compute. An expression map evaluates each feature. For discrete features, hashing and multi-value combinations are supported. For dense features, normalization, binarization, and bucket splitting are supported. Much of this calculation can be done directly using Arrow's built-in expression. For Arrow, which does not have built-in computations, you can register your own expressions using a custom Compute Kernel.

In terms of implementation, the expression of feature operation used offline is serialized and saved in the directory exported by the model. The same Arrow Compute expression is constructed after the online prediction service loads the expression so that the completely consistent feature calculation can be shared offline. In addition, because Arrow Compute supports filtering, joins, and other operations, LakeSoul also supports input to input batches. Thanks to the design of Arrow column expression, these calculations are vectorized and can have relatively high performance.

The offline feature calculation and offline input calculation are unified Arrow Table format. Arrow itself provides a multi-language interface to create Arrow tables, so MetaSpore Serving also easily supports multiple language calls. For example, with an XGBoost model with ten float type feature inputs, LakeSoul implements a program in Python that calls the MetaSpore prediction service:

import grpc
import metaspore_pb2
import metaspore_pb2_grpc
import pyarrow as pa
with grpc.insecure_channel('0.0.0.0:50051') as channel:
    stub = metaspore_pb2_grpc.PredictStub(channel)
    # Construct a one-line, 10-feature sample
    row = []
    values = [0.6558618,0.13005558,0.03510657,0.23048967,0.63329154,0.43201634,0.5795548,0.5384891,0.9612295,0.39274803]
    for i in range(10):
        row.append(pa.array([values[i]], type=pa.float32()))
    # Create Arrow RecordBatch
    rb = pa.RecordBatch.from_arrays(row, [f'field_{i}' for i in range(10)])
    # Serialization RecordBatch
    sink = pa.BufferOutputStream()
    with pa.ipc.new_file(sink, rb.schema) as writer:
        writer.write_batch(rb)

    # Construct GRPC request for MetaSpore Serving
    payload_map = {"input": sink.getvalue().to_pybytes()}
    request = metaspore_pb2.PredictRequest(model_name="xgboost_model", payload=payload_map)
    # Call Serving service prediction
    reply = stub.Predict(request)
    for name in reply.payload:
        with pa.BufferReader(reply.payload[name]) as reader:
            tensor = pa.ipc.read_tensor(reader)
            print(f'Predict Output Tensor: {tensor.to_numpy()}')

It can be seen that the online prediction is also the Arrow Table of the input characteristics, and MetaSpore Serving can be directly invoked. The invocation method is also uniform offline.

2.5 [MetaSpore](https://github.com/meta-soul/MetaSpore**) online algorithm application framework**
Offline training frameworks and online Serving services are now available. Then, an algorithm in the business scene landing is still a final step: an online algorithm experiment. In a service scenario, to verify the validity of an algorithm model, a baseline needs to be established and compared with the new algorithm model. Therefore, an online experimental framework is needed which can easily define algorithm experiments, read online features, and call model prediction services. In addition, multiple experiments can be traffic segmented to achieve ABTest effect comparison. A configuration center is also needed to quickly carry out multiple experimental iterations, which can dynamically load refresh experiments and cut flow configurations, support hot loading of experimental parameters, and various debugging and trace functions. This link also directly determines whether the AI model can be finally implemented into practical business applications.

MetaSpore provides an online algorithm application framework that covers the entire flow of online experiments. This framework is based on SpringBoot + Spring Cloud and provides the following core functions:

1. Experiment Pipeline. Developers can add experimental classes with simple annotations:

@ExperimentAnnotation(name = "rank.widedeep")
@Component
public class WideDeepRankExperiment implements BaseExperiment<RecommendResult, RecommendResult>
{
    @Override
    public void initialize(Map<String, Object> map) {}

    @SneakyThrows
    @Override
    public RecommendResult run(Context context, RecommendResult recommendResult)
{}
}

The Spring Cloud configuration center is then used to dynamically assemble multiple experimental flows, including flow segmentation for horizontal experiments and sequence of vertical experiments. Take the recommendation system as an example, there are two experiments in the recall layer and two experiments in the sorting layer, and the two layers are orthogonal so that the online experiment process can be assembled through the following configuration:

scene-config:
  scenes:
    - name: guess-you-like
      layers:
        - name: match
          normalLayerArgs:
            - experimentName: match.base
              ratio: 0.3
            - experimentName: match.multiple
              ratio: 0.7
        - name: rank
          normalLayerArgs:
            - experimentName: rank.wideDeep
              ratio: 0.5
            - experimentName: rank.lightGBM
              ratio: 0.5
  experiments:
    - layerName: match
      experimentName: match.base
      extraExperimentArgs:
        modelName: match.base
        matcherNames: [ItemCfMatcher]
    - layerName: match
      experimentName: match.multiple
      extraExperimentArgs:
        modelName: match.multiple
        matcherNames: [ItemCfMatcher, SwingMatcher, TwoTowersMatcher]
    - layerName: rank
      experimentName: rank.wideDeep
      extraExperimentArgs:
        modelName: movie_lens_wdl
        ranker: WideAndDeepRanker
        maxReservation: 100
    - layerName: rank
      experimentName: rank.lightGBM
      extraExperimentArgs:
        modelName: lightgbm_test_model
        ranker: LightGBMRanker
        maxReservation: 100

After the configuration and experimental class implementation are added to the SpringBoot project, the framework will automatically create and initialize the objects of the experimental class and execute them by order of the experimental layer, and automatically select an experiment to execute in each layer according to the tangential flow configuration. The developer can freely define the input and output between experiments, providing a high degree of flexibility. At the same time, this configuration file is placed in the configuration center, supporting dynamic hot update, modification of experimental configuration, and cutting flow configuration, which can take effect in real-time. **2.Online feature extraction framework. **In online prediction, we need to construct an Arrow Table of features. However, online features can come from multiple sources, often with various databases, caches, and upstream services. MetaSpore, based on SpringBoot JPA, encapsulates a feature extraction code generation framework that automatically generates database access code only after the developer defines the schema and data source of the feature.

**3.MetaSpore provides a Client implementation of online Serving, **supporting SpringBoot and providing annotation injection to access Serving.

2.6 MetaSpore embraces the open-source ecosystem
MetaSpore itself is an open-source project, and MetaSpore uses a mix of mature open source ecosystems. For example, in offline training, MetaSpore's offline training framework Bridges PySpark and PyTorch, two popular frameworks in big data and deep learning, seamlessly combine their ecology better to address the pain points of real business scenarios. The online service is also fully integrated with gRPC, SpringBoot, and Spring Cloud, allowing developers to obtain the best practices of landing big data intelligent applications in the existing standard technology stack.

MetaSpore's philosophy is to embrace the open-source ecosystem fully. For example, for offline NLP large model training, developers who have the need can still use the existing open-source framework, such as OneFlow, to train the model; MetaSpore can continue to provide the ability to incorporate pre-training of the large model into multimodal model learning, as well as online Serving of the NLP model, making the NLP large model more inclusive.

3.The conclusion
This article details the design philosophy and core features of MetaSpore's one-stop machine learning platform to help readers better understand MetaSpore. Want to learn more about MetaSpore business scenario implementation.

Next week, I will update the detailed steps of using MetaSpore to build industrial recommendation systems rapidly.
item.author: qazmkop

item.title: 使用 CSS 创建烟花效果 | Creating a firework effect with CSS
item.description:
上周我用 CSS 创建了一个烟花效果。 它相对简单（每个烟花只需要一个 HTML 元素）和可自定义（它使用 CSS 自定义属性来自定义颜色、大小、位置......）

这是效果的演示（全屏查看）：

在我继续之前，让我添加一些免责声明：首先，以下代码是原始代码的简化版本，查看上面的演示以获得完整的可定制代码（但可能有点复杂）。 其次，开发演示很有趣，但它可能不是最有效的代码方式。

这个怎么运作

这个想法是让一个具有绝对大小背景的小元素放置在不同的相对位置（例如，使用百分比）。 绝对和相对的使用在这里是必不可少的。 拥有绝对大小的背景意味着它们的大小不会根据容器的大小而改变，但它们的位置是相对的，因此在调整容器大小时它会改变（或给人以改变的印象）。

点之间的相对距离始终相同。 但是随着容器的增长，它们之间的绝对距离也会扩大——类似于真正的烟花的工作方式。

查看右侧箭头所指的圆圈。 它的位置是 100% 水平和 50% 垂直。 容器很小的时候是一样的，但是随着容器的增长，它看起来很多。 然而，它仍然处于与以前相同的相对位置：100% 水平和 50% 垂直。

编码

在对事情将如何工作进行（简短）描述之后，让我们动手编写代码！ 一个很好的 lea 方法是通过做。 随意按照步骤进行（您需要自己添加一些东西，这将使您的烟花更加独特。）

如上所述，HTML 部分很简单：每个烟花只需要一个元素：

 <div class= "firework" ></div>

我把它做成了一个<div>但它可以是任何其他元素。 如果我们想让它更易于访问，我们可以添加一个role “img”来表示它是一个图像和一个带有简短描述的aria-label ：“烟花动画卡通”。 但我们可以争辩说它没有必要，因为它更具表现性并且已经是空的。 所以，让我们暂时保持简单。

我们首先定义烟花的基本样式：一个简单的块容器绝对定位在屏幕上：

 .firework { position : absolute ; top : 50% ; left : 50% ; transform : translate ( -50% , -50% ); width : 0.5vmin ; aspect-ratio : 1 ; background : /* background intentionally blank */ ; background-size : 0.5vmin 0.5vmin ; background-repeat : no-repeat ; }

让我们一点一点地看一下属性和值：

  • position: absolute; 在不影响其他元素的情况下，拥有绝对位置是理想的选择。
  • top: 50%; left: 50%; 我们可以稍后更改（如果您已经深入研究代码，您会看到这些从烟花到烟花的变化。）
  • transform: translate(-50%, -50%); 通过水平和垂直平移元素 -50%，当我们改变它的大小时，它会在所有方向上增长（而不是向右下方增加）。
  • width: 0.5vmin; aspect-ratio: 1; 我使用了 vmin 单位，所以它是响应式的，但你可以使用 px、rem 或你最喜欢的 CSS 单位。
  • background-size: 0.5vmin 0.5vmin; 我们设置了固定的背景大小，因此一旦我们定义了渐变（见下文），它们将始终具有相同的大小，而与容器的大小无关。
  • background-repeat: no-repeat; 否则，我们会拥有太多，而且看起来不好看。

对于背景，我们将添加一堆像这样的径向渐变：

 radial-gradient ( circle , yellow 0 . 5 vmin , #0000 0 ) 50 % 0 %

注意：因为background-size是平方的，所以我们的演示不需要关键字circle 。 我添加了它以防我有不同大小的背景。

此渐变将在烟花元素的中心 (50%) 顶部 (0%) 放置一个大小为 0.5vmin 的圆圈。 现在，我们将在不同位置编写许多其他渐变。 只需确保将它们全部分布在容器中即可。 不要有空的一面，而另一面是满的。 尝试平等地添加到所有象限（请记住，当容器扩展时，“侧面”烟花会看起来很奇怪。）我总共添加了 30 个背景（每个象限 7 个）。 作为建议，如果可以，请避开角落。 它们是“异常值”，当动画处于活动状态时，它看起来只是“meh”。

谈论动画......让我们定义动画！ 它将分为两个部分：飞上天空的烟花（压缩）和烟花爆炸。

 @keyframes firework { 0 % { transform : translate ( -50% , 60vh ); width : 0.5vmin ; opacity : 1 ; } 50 % { width : 0.5vmin ; opacity : 1 ; } 100 % { width : 45vmin ; opacity : 0 ; } }

它首先将烟花元素移动到屏幕底部之外（ translate(-50%, 60vh) ），并使其保持小（ width: 0.5vmin ）和可见（ opacity: 1 ）。 元素中指定的变换是translate(-50%, -50%) 。 因此动画会将元素从底部转换为其原始元素（100%）。

在动画进行到一半时，我们保持元素小而可见，从那时起，它将扩展到最终大小（ 45vmin ）并与背景融合（通过设置opacity为0 ）。

 .firework { animation : firework 2s infinite ; ... }

最后，我对烟花的::before和::after伪元素应用了相同的样式，然后对它们进行了一些小的修改（旋转、缩放、使用 3D 旋转，你选择），所以我有三倍无需添加更多背景或元素的粒子数量（以及“不同位置”）：

 .firework , .firework ::before , .firework ::after { content : "" ; ... } .firework ::before { transform : translate ( -50% , -50% ) rotate ( 25deg ) !important ; } .firework ::after { transform : translate ( -50% , -50% ) rotate ( -37deg ) !important ; }

对于::before和::after ， rotate()的角度是随机的。 但是，如果没有它，效果也可能看起来很酷。 随意尝试和实验。

结果

完成这些步骤后，我们有一行 HTML 和 50-60 行 CSS（取决于径向渐变的数量）。 结果将如下所示：

比上面显示的更简单，但代码也简单得多。 是时候个性化它并让它成为你自己的了！

结论

最后，为了让事情更容易编辑（但操作起来有点复杂），我用一些自定义属性替换了一些值。 我没有将这部分添加到本文中，因为我想保持简单，但是您可以在文章开头的演示代码中看到变量。

我希望你喜欢这篇文章。 如果您开发自己的版本或对上述版本进行更改，请分享。 我会喜欢检查它并探索​​代码:)

Article image by Erwan Hesry on Unsplash . Erwan Hesry 在 Unsplash 上的文章图片。



原文:

Last week I created a firework effect with CSS. It is relatively simple (it only requires one HTML element per firework) and customizable (it uses CSS custom properties to customize colors, sizes, positions...)

Here is a demo of the effect (see in full screen):

Before I continue, let me add a couple of disclaimers: first, the following code is a simplified version of the original one, view the demo above for the full customizable code (but it may be a bit complex). And second, this is fun to develop demo, but it may not be the most efficient thing to do code-wise.

How it works

The idea is to have a small element with absolute-sized backgrounds placed in different relative positions (e.g., using percentages). The use of absolute and relative is essential here. Having absolute-sized backgrounds means that their size will not change depending on the size of the container, but their position is relative, so it will change (or give the impression of changing) when the container is resized.

The relative distance between the dots is the same at all times. But as the container grows, the absolute distance between them expands too-similarly to how real fireworks work.

Look at the circle on the right side pointed by an arrow. Its position is 100% horizontal and 50% vertical. It was the same when the container was little, but it looks like a lot as the container grows. Yet, it is still in the same relative position as before: 100% horizontal and 50% vertical.

The code

After a (short) description of how things will work, let's get our hands dirty with the code! A great way of lea is by doing. Feel free to follow the steps along (you will need to add some things by yourself, which will make your fireworks more unique.)

HTML

As mentioned above, the HTML part is straightforward: we just need one element for each firework:

<div class="firework"></div>

I made it a <div> but it could be any other element. If we wanted to make it more accessible, we could add a role of "img" to indicate that it is an image and an aria-label with a short description: "animated cartoon of a firework." But we could argue that it's not needed as it's more presentational and is already empty. So, let's keep it simple for now.

CSS

We start by defining the basic styles for our firework: a simple block container absolute positioned on the screen:

.firework { position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); width: 0.5vmin; aspect-ratio: 1; background: /* background intentionally blank */ ; background-size: 0.5vmin 0.5vmin; background-repeat: no-repeat;
}

Let's see the properties and values little by little:

  • position: absolute;: the element will be moved around the screen. Having an absolute position is ideal for that without affecting other elements.
  • top: 50%; left: 50%;: as a default, the firework will be at the absolute center. We can change that later (if you dug into the code already, you saw these changes from firework to firework.)
  • transform: translate(-50%, -50%);: this is important. By translating the element -50% horizontally and vertically, it will grow in all directions (instead of increasing towards the bottom right side.) when we change its size.
  • width: 0.5vmin; aspect-ratio: 1;: the element will be a tiny square. I used the vmin unit, so it's responsive, but you can use px, rem, or your favorite CSS unit.
  • background-size: 0.5vmin 0.5vmin;: this is important too. We set a fixed background size, so once we define the gradients (see below), they will always have the same size, independently of how big the container is.
  • background-repeat: no-repeat;: we don't want the gradients to repeat. Otherwise, we'd have too many, and it wouldn't look nice.

For the background, we are going to add a bunch of radial gradients like this one:

radial-gradient(circle, yellow 0.5vmin, #0000 0) 50% 0%

Note: because the background-size is squared, the keyword circle is not really needed for our demo. I added it in case I had different sized backgrounds.

This gradient will place a circle of size 0.5vmin in the center (50%) top (0%) of the firework element. Now, we will code many other gradients in different positions. Just make sure that you distribute them all across the container. Don't have an empty side while another is full. Try to add equally into all the quadrants (remember that when the container expands, a "sided" firework will look weird.) I added a total of 30 backgrounds (7 per quadrant). As a recommendation, avoid the corners if you can. They are "outliers" and make it look just "meh" when the animation is active.

And talking about animations… Let's define the animation! It will divide into two parts: the firework flying up into the sky (compressed) and the firework explosion.

@keyframes firework { 0% { transform: translate(-50%, 60vh); width: 0.5vmin; opacity: 1; } 50% { width: 0.5vmin; opacity: 1; } 100% { width: 45vmin; opacity: 0; }
}

It starts by moving the firework element outside of the bottom of the screen (translate(-50%, 60vh)), and keep it small (width: 0.5vmin) and visible (opacity: 1). The transform specified in the element is translate(-50%, -50%). So the animation will translate the element from the bottom to its original (at 100%).

Halfway through the animation, we are keeping the element small and visible, and from then on, it will expand to its final size (45vmin) and fuse with the background (by setting an opacity of 0).

.firework { animation: firework 2s infinite; ...
}

Finally, I applied the same styles to the ::before and ::after pseudo-elements of the firework, and then made some minor modifications to both of them (rotating, scaling, using 3D rotation, you pick), so I had triple the number of particles (and in "different positions") without having to add any more backgrounds or elements:

.firework,
.firework::before,
.firework::after { content: ""; ...
}
.firework::before { transform: translate(-50%, -50%) rotate(25deg) !important; }
.firework::after { transform: translate(-50%, -50%) rotate(-37deg) !important;
}

The angle of the rotate() is random for both the ::before and ::after. The !important is necessary to override the one from the animation. However, the effect may look cool without it, too. Feel free to try and experiment.

The result

After following these steps, we have a single line of HTML and 50–60 lines of CSS (depending on the number of radial gradients). The result will look like this:

Simpler than the one displayed above, but the code is also considerably simpler. Time to personalize it and make it your own!

Conclusion

As a final touch and to make things easier to edit (but a bit more complicated to follow), I replaced some of the values with some custom properties. The CSS variables make it easier for me to include other fireworks with minimal code changes. I didn't add that part to this article because I wanted to keep it simple, but you can see the variables on the demo's code at the beginning of the article.

I hope you liked the article. If you develop your own version or make changes to the version above, please share it. I'll enjoy checking it and exploring the code :)

Article image by Erwan Hesry on Unsplash.

item.title: 您对 Oculus Quest 2 或其他 VR 硬件有何看法？ | What are your thoughts on the Oculus Quest 2 or other VR hardware?
item.description:
嘿偷看！

我最近一直在考虑购买 Oculus Quest 2。 像 Vader Immortal 这样的游戏太诱人了（顺便说一句，现在有 50% 的折扣！ ）。 然后是SynthVR ，我敢肯定我可以很容易地迷失几个小时！ 更不用说，有一些非常漂亮的建筑应用程序可以让你创建一个家或建筑物，然后穿过它。

我倾向于 Oculus，因为它价格低廉且具有无线功能。 我也有几个来自家乡的朋友，他们每个人都有一个，我认为与他们重新联系会很有趣，四处游荡，玩一些推杆推杆（他们选择的游戏，哈哈）。

那么，大家觉得呢？ 如果你有 Quest，我很想听听你推荐什么游戏。 如果你有其他形式的 VR，请告诉我！ 在我冒险购买之前，我很想了解更多信息。 🙂 🙂



原文:

Hey peeps!

I've been considering picking up an Oculus Quest 2 recently. Games like Vader Immortal are sooooo tempting (by the way, it's 50% off right now!). And then there's SynthVR which I'm sure I could easily get lost in for hours on end! Not to mention, there's some really slick architecture apps that will let you create a home or building and then walk through it.

I lean toward the Oculus because of its low price and wireless-ness. I also have a couple of friends from back home that each have one and I think it'd be fun to reconnect with them, goof around, and play some putt putt (their game of choice, haha).

So, what do y'all think? If you have a Quest, I'd love to hear what games you'd suggest. If ya have some other form of VR, then please tell me about it! I'm curious to learn more before I take the plunge and make the purchase. 🙂

item.title: 约翰变得灵活 | John Gets Flexible
item.description:
几周后我将参加一个假单身派对，我们将主要打高尔夫球。 我小时候上过课，在过去 15 年左右的时间里每年大约玩一次，这意味着我很糟糕，很可能是小组中最差的。

这确实是我认为您可以玩的最谦卑的运动，但当您获得完美的一击时，它也是最令人满意的。 让您“痒”玩更多游戏的那个。 无论如何，过去两个月左右我一直在练习，我开始对自己的比赛感觉更好。

就像编码一样，需要练习和重复才能开始感到舒服。 当你掌握一个方面时，你会编织另一个层次或概念，并将其添加到你的曲目中。 现在开始我的学习，但首先……

推特计数更新！ 可悲的是，我在 33 岁时仍然停滞不前。我失去了一对，但在过去一周获得了一对，因此净值为 0。推特@miyaocodes让我知道我需要练习编码或打高尔夫球。

更多 Flexbox 实践

这是一个缓慢的一周，但富有成果。 随着我对 Flexbox 进行了大量练习，我变得更加灵活。 我们遇到了一些有趣的挑战，我们只是复制了一些图像，其中形状内部的形状内部有一堆形状等等。

第一次挑战后，我学会了由内而外的开始！ 起初，我想通过创建最小或最大高度/宽度来调整父形状的大小。 然而，让元素内部的内容帮助形成其父元素的形状要容易得多。

我还意识到宽度通常由硬件固定。 我们的显示器、电话等只能这么宽，而网站通常会坚持这个宽度。 但是，长度可以是无限的。 我知道你在深夜滚动末日试图到达底部，但没有底部！

为了给你一个可视化，这是我们的第一个挑战。

挑战

结果

最困难的部分可能是命名我的元素。 我会选择数字、字母、颜色吗？ 我最终按颜色命名它们，然后是“a/b/c”，如下所示：

 `<div class='turquoise a'>`

这样我就可以区分具有相同颜色的元素，还可以在需要时将它们组合在一起。 您可以在此处查看第一个挑战的代码。

第二个挑战，我认为我从内而外开始时做得更好，这消除了定义大部分元素大小的需要。 我还用颜色命名它们，但如果我需要设置特定元素的样式，则使用“nth-of-type(1)”。 这是我们在一周中学到的东西。

请参阅此处的代码差异。

挑战 2

结果 2

我们快速介绍了 MAMP。 什么是 MAMP？ 让我试着解释一下。

它使用自己的脚本语言 PHP 来归档、存储页面并将其推送到本地 Web 环境中。 它允许我们只为自己发布网站，就像私人放映电影一样。 它还允许我们分解页面，以便更好地组织代码。 例如，我们的 HTML 文档可以为页眉、主要内容部分和页脚提供单独的文档。 像这样的东西：

将代码拆分为组织之外的单独文档有什么好处？ 我认为主要的好处是关于变化。 假设您对网站上的多个页面使用相同的页眉或页脚，这是一种非常常见的做法。 如果您想在所有页面的标题上添加临时消息怎么办？ 也许您正在举办假日促销活动。 好吧，您可以只编辑标题文档上的代码，而不是编辑每个单独页面的代码，并且您网站每个单独页面上的代码保持不变！

如果有没有负面影响的捷径，我完全赞成！ 在接下来的几周内，我们将更多地了解 PHP 及其脚本语言。 我对此很期待！

我更新的 NBA 拍摄：

勇士7场战胜凯尔特人。



原文:

I’m going on a pseudo-bachelor party in couple weeks where we’ll be mostly golfing. I took lessons as a kid and have played about once a year for the past 15 years or so, which means I’m awful and will likely be the worst of the group.

It is truly the most humbling sport I think you can play, but also the most satisfying when you get that one perfect hit. The one that gives you the “itch” to play more. In any case, I’ve been practicing over the past two months or so and I’m starting to feel better about my game.

Just like with coding, it takes practice and repetition to start to feel comfortable. As you master one aspect, you weave in another layer or concept and add it to your repertoire. Now onto my learnings, but first…

Twitter count update! Sadly, I remain stagnant at 33. I lost a couple, but gained a couple over the past week so net 0. Tweet @miyaocodes to let me know I need to practice coding or golfing.

More Flexbox Practice

It was a bit of a slower week, but fruitful. I’m even more flexible as I got a ton more practice with Flexbox. We had a couple of fun challenges where we just copied some images with a bunch of shapes inside of shapes inside of shapes and so on.

After the first challenge, I learned to start from the inside out! At first, I wanted to size the parent shapes by creating a min or max height/width. However, it’s much easier to just let the content inside of the element help form the shape of its parent.

I also came to the realization that width is generally fixed by the hardware. Our monitors, phones etc can only be so wide and sites generally stick to that width. However, length can be infinite. I know you’ve doom scrolled late at night trying to get to the bottom, but there is no bottom!

To give you a visualization, here’s the first challenge we were tasked with.

The Challenge

The Result

The most difficult part might’ve been naming my elements. Do I go with numbers, letters, colors? I ended up naming them by color followed by “a/b/c” like this:

`<div class='turquoise a'>`

That way I could differentiate the elements with same color, but also style them together when needed. You can view the code here for the first challenge here.

The second challenge, I think I did a much better job as I started from the inside out, which removed the need to define most of the element’s sizes. I also named them by their color, but used “nth-of-type(1)” if I needed to style a specific element(s). This was something we learned over the week.

See the difference in code here.

Challenge 2

Result 2

Mac Apache MySql PHP (MAMP)

We got a quick intro to MAMP. What is MAMP? Let me try to explain.

MAMP allows us to store our webpages locally on our computer. It uses its own scripting language, PHP to file, store and push the pages onto the local web environment. It allows us to publish websites to just ourselves, like a private screening of a movie. It also allows us to break up our pages so we can better organize our code. For example, our HTML doc can have a separate docs for the header, main content section and footer. PHP will stitch the docs together and display it as if it were one fluid doc. Something like this:

What’s the benefit of splitting up your code into separate docs beyond organization? I think the main benefit is about changes. Say you use the same header or footer for multiple pages on your site, which is a pretty common practice. What if you wanted to add a temporary message on the header for all pages? Perhaps you’re running a holiday sale. Well, instead of editing the code for each individual page, you can edit the code on just the header doc instead and your code on each individual page of your website remains the same!

If there’s a shortcut with no negative side effects, I’m all for it! In the coming weeks, we’ll be learning more about PHP and it’s scripting language. I’m looking forward to it!

MY UPDATED NBA TAKE:

Warriors over Celtics in 7 games.
item.author: miyaocodes

item.title: DragonRuby：旋转矩形 | DragonRuby: Rotating Rectangles
item.description:
在DragonRuby中，绘图原语之一是“实体”——实际上是一个矩形。

矩形由右下角的原点 ( x , y ) 和高度/宽度大小 ( h , w ) 定义。

例如，此代码大致在屏幕中间绘制了一个黑色矩形：

 def tick ( args ) args . outputs . solids << { x: 490 , y: 310 , w: 300 , h: 100 , } end

矩形很简单并且（可能）很快。 但是如果我们想把矩形放在一个角度怎么办？ 还是转一圈？

对于精灵，有一个angle属性。 这适用于矩形吗？ 很不幸的是，不行。

我们可以制作一个矩形图像并在精灵中使用它，但这似乎很浪费。 我不知道调整精灵的大小是否非常耗费资源，但我确信它比改变简单矩形的大小需要更多的周期。

幸运的是，有一条中间道路。

从 2.26 发行说明（是的，这是我唯一能找到正式记录此功能的地方，尽管它在示例中使用）：

在调用 ~(boot|tick)~ 之前，会添加一个大小为 1280x1280 的白色实体作为渲染目标。 您可以使用这个预定义的渲染目标

创建实体并获得 ~args.outputs.sprites~ 相关功能。

换句话说，可以像这样创建与上面相同的矩形：

 def tick ( args ) args . outputs . sprites << { x: 490 , y: 310 , w: 300 , h: 100 , r: 0 , g: 0 , b: 0 , path: :pixel , } end

关键部分是path: :pixel并创建一个精灵而不是实体。

如上所述， :pixel渲染目标是白色的，因此要获得与将颜色设置为黑色之前完全相同的结果。 如果没有指定r g b值，它将是一个白色矩形。

现在，我们可以旋转这个矩形吗？

当然！

 def tick ( args ) args . outputs . sprites << { x: 490 , y: 310 , w: 300 , h: 100 , r: 0 , g: 0 , b: 0 , path: :pixel , angle: 45 , } end

一圈又一圈地旋转

这是一个旋转矩形的示例：

 def tick ( args ) args . outputs . sprites << { x: 490 , y: 310 , w: 300 , h: 100 , r: 0 , g: 0 , b: 0 , path: :pixel , angle: args . tick_count % 360 , } end

这很棒，但如果你宁愿让它像这样旋转呢？

通过一些调查，您可能会发现angle_anchor_x和angle_anchor_y属性。 你会认为那些指的是坐标网格上的点是可以原谅的。 但他们没有！

相反， angle_anchor_x和angle_anchor_y是相对于精灵本身的百分比。

换句话说，如果两个锚点都设置为0.5 （默认值），旋转的中心将是精灵的中间（宽度的一半和高度的一半）。

介于0和1之间的锚点值将位于精灵内部。 但是大于1或小于0的值将在精灵之外。

这里有一点参考：

快乐的矩形旋转！



原文:

In DragonRuby, one of the drawing primitives is a "solid" - a rectangle, actually.

Rectangles are defined by the origin point of the bottom-right corner (x, y) and a size in height/width (h, w).

For example, this code paints a black rectangle in roughly the middle of the screen:

def tick(args) args.outputs.solids << { x: 490, y: 310, w: 300, h: 100, }
end

Rectangles are simple and (presumably) fast. But what if we want to put the rectangle at an angle? Or spin it around?

For sprites, there is an angle attribute. Will that work for rectangles? Unfortunately, no.

We could make a rectangle image and use it in a sprite, but that seems wasteful. I don't know if resizing a sprite is very resource-intensive, but I'm sure it takes more cycles than changing the size of a simple rectangle.

Fortunately, there is a middle way.

From the 2.26 release notes (and yes, this is the only place I could find this functionality formally documented, though it is used in examples):

** [API] Pre-defined ~:pixel~ render target now available. Before ~(boot|tick)~ are invoked, a white solid with a size of 1280x1280 is added as a render target. You can use this predefined render target to

create solids and get ~args.outputs.sprites~ related capabilities.

In other words, it is possible to create an equivalent rectangle to the above like this:

def tick(args) args.outputs.sprites << { x: 490, y: 310, w: 300, h: 100, r: 0, g: 0, b: 0, path: :pixel, }
end

The key piece is path: :pixel and creating a sprite instead of a solid.

As is mentioned above, the :pixel render target is white, so to get exactly the same results as before the color is set to black. If no rgb values were specified, it would be a white rectangle.

Now, can we rotate this rectangle?

Sure!

def tick(args) args.outputs.sprites << { x: 490, y: 310, w: 300, h: 100, r: 0, g: 0, b: 0, path: :pixel, angle: 45, }
end

(By the way, angles in DragonRuby are in degrees.)

Spinning Round and Round

Here is an example of spinning the rectangle around:

def tick(args) args.outputs.sprites << { x: 490, y: 310, w: 300, h: 100, r: 0, g: 0, b: 0, path: :pixel, angle: args.tick_count % 360, }
end

This is great and all, but what if you'd rather have it spin around like this?

With some sleuthing, you might find the angle_anchor_x and angle_anchor_y attributes. And you would be forgiven for thinking those refer to points on the coordinate grid. But they do not!

Instead, angle_anchor_x and angle_anchor_y are percentages relative to the sprite itself.

In other words, if both anchors are set to 0.5 (the default), the center of the rotation will be the middle of the sprite (half of the width and half of the height).

0,0 is the bottom-left corner of the sprite, as shown above:

def tick(args) args.outputs.sprites << { x: 490, y: 310, w: 300, h: 100, r: 0, g: 0, b: 0, path: :pixel, angle: args.tick_count % 360, angle_anchor_x: 0, angle_anchor_y: 0, }
end

Anchor values between 0 and 1 will be inside the sprite. But values greater than 1 or less than 0 will be outside the sprite.

Here is a little reference:

Happy rectangle rotating!

item.title: 用 TypeScript 替换 YAML | Replacing YAML with TypeScript
item.description:
原帖： https ://nabeel.dev/2022/04/30/yaml-alternative

您是否厌倦了复制粘贴和编辑大量 YAML 文件？ 在这篇文章中，我建议使用 TypeScript 来定义您的服务，并使用 Handlebars 模板或 Kubernetes NodeJS 客户端来更轻松地管理您的部署。 您可以在https://github.com/nabsul/k8s-yaml-alternative找到一些示例代码来演示这一点。

介绍

多年来，我一直在使用签入 Git 存储库的 YAML 文件来管理我的 Kubernetes 部署。 但是随着部署数量的增加，这种方法开始遇到问题：

  • 很多 YAML 都是一遍又一遍地重复的样板。
  • 全局更改很繁琐，需要编辑存储库中的所有文件。

我最近开始尝试以不同的方式管理我的部署。 在这篇文章中，我将描述实现此设计所采取的步骤。 我将使用的示例是运行三个应用程序的集群：

  • 具有两个副本的 NGINX 服务暴露了 80 端口
  • 暴露端口 4222 和 8222 的单个 NATS 实例
  • 没有端口但需要一些环境变量的自定义应用程序

从你自己的定义开始

我通常首先决定我将使用什么语言/系统来开始构建我的部署。 对于我的集群，这是 YAML 文件，但也可能是 Terraform 或 Helm 图表。 使用这种方法，我的应用程序需求退居二线，重点变成：“这个系统需要什么才能工作？”。 以 YAML 中指定的Kubernetes 部署为例：

 apiVersion : apps/v1 kind : Deployment metadata : name : service1 namespace : k8s-ts-test labels : app : service1 spec : replicas : 2 selector : matchLabels : app : service1 template : metadata : labels : app : service1 spec : containers : - name : service1 image : nginx:latest ports : - containerPort : 80 name : port80

我真正关心或控制上述规范的哪些部分？ 副本数、图像标签和应用程序端口。 就是这样，二十行中的三行。 其他一切都是样板。

与其试图让我们的应用程序适应 Kubernetes 的需求，不如从一个只包含我们关心的细节的规范开始。 根据我要运行的初始服务列表，我们可以定义如下所有内容：

 const services = { service1 : { image : ' nginx:latest ' , replicas : 2 , ports : [ 80 ] }, service2 : { image : ' nats:latest ' , replicas : 1 , ports : [ 4222 , 8222 ] }, service3 : { image : ' app:latest ' , replicas : 1 , env : { VAR1 : ' Some Value ' , VAR2 : ' another value ' , } } }

从这样的规范开始让我专注于我关心的细节。 您可以清楚地看到我计划部署多少个应用程序，以及这些应用程序之间有何不同。 稍后我们可以担心 YAML/Terraform/Helm 细节。 此外，我可以改变对 YAML/Terraform/Helm 问题的看法，并且仍然将这个定义作为我以后所做的一切的起点。

从自定义定义到 YAML

使用Handlebars模板可以轻松地将此自定义规范转换为常规 YAML。 您可以在此处查看所有模板，但这里有一个小示例：

 spec: containers: - name: {{name}} image: {{image}} {{#if ports}} ports: {{#each ports}} - containerPort: {{this}} name: port{{this}} {{/each}}

使用这种方法请注意：

  • 保证我的所有部署看起来都一样。
  • 如果我需要更改我的所有服务（例如 API 版本或命名空间更改），我可以通过一个模板更改轻松完成。

您可以通过运行npm run generate来生成/generated.yaml 。 然后，您可以使用kubectl apply -f generated.yaml一次性部署所有服务。

完全跳过 YAML

在这个 repo 中，我还演示了如何完全消除对 YAML 的需求。 我没有生成 YAML 并运行kubectl ，而是使用@kubernetes/client-node npm 包直接部署到 Kubernetes。 这比 YAML 生成需要更多的工作，但有几个优点。 最大的优点是 npm 包包含V1Secret 、 V1Service等的 TypeScript 定义。 与编写 YAML 相比，这使您的部署具有强类型化并减少了出错的可能性。 您可以按照/deploy.ts脚本查看这一切是如何工作的，但它的核心是一个简单的循环：

 for ( var [ n , s ] of Object . entries ( services )) { console . log ( `Deploying ${ getName ( namespace , n )} started` ) await makeSecret ( namespace , n , s ) await makeDeployment ( namespace , n , s ) await makeService ( namespace , n , s ) console . log ( `Deploying ${ getName ( namespace , n )} complete\n` ) }

接下来是什么？

我才刚刚开始重新思考我想如何管理和部署我的 Kubernetes 集群。 我认为这是一个好的开始，我希望在我继续实验的过程中分享更多的经验。 作为接下来的步骤，我将考虑删除更多在创建集群时所做的“点击操作”：

  • 登录数字海洋仪表板以创建新集群
  • 手动设置负载均衡器
  • 查找负载均衡器 IP 地址并配置 DNS 以指向新集群
  • 配置所有基础设施（docker repo、AWS）和应用程序机密

至于我预见的挑战：在这个例子中，应用程序的定义是通用的，即使你想完全跳过 Kubernetes，你也可以使用它们。 但是，我在集群中运行了两个特定于 Kubernetes 的应用程序： KCert和 ecr -login-renewal 。 这些应用程序需要围绕服务帐户和权限进行特殊的 Kubernetes 配置。 我还不确定如何干净地编码这些。

如果您喜欢这个想法，请在您自己的设置中尝试一下。 如果您习惯使用 NodeJS/TypeScript 编写代码，请尝试使用 Kubernetes 客户端方法。 或者，如果您只是想简化模板，试试 Handlebars 模板。



原文:

Original Post: https://nabeel.dev/2022/04/30/yaml-alternative

TL;DR

Are you tired of copy-pasting and editing a ton YAML files? In this post I suggest using TypeScript to define your services and either Handlebars templates or the Kubernetes NodeJS client to more easily manage your deployments. You can find some sample code that demonstrates this at https://github.com/nabsul/k8s-yaml-alternative.

Introduction

I've been using YAML files checked into a Git repo to manage my Kubernetes deployment for many years now. But as the number of deployments grows, this approach starts to run into problems:

  • A lot of the YAML is boilerplate that is repeated over and over again.
  • Global changes are tedious, requiring editing all the files in your repo.

I've recently started experimenting with managing my deployments in a different way. In this post I will describe the steps I take to reach this design. The example that I will be using is a cluster running three application:

  • An NGINX service with two replicas exposing port 80
  • A single instance of NATS exposing ports 4222 and 8222
  • A custom application that doesn't have ports but requires some environment variables

Start with your Own Definition

I usually start building out my deployments by first deciding what language/system I will use. For my cluster this has been the YAML files, but it could also have been something like Terraform or Helm charts. With that approach, my application requirements take a back seat and the focus becomes: "What does this system require to work?". Take for example a Kubernetes deployment specified in YAML:

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata: name: service1 namespace: k8s-ts-test labels: app: service1
spec: replicas: 2 selector: matchLabels: app: service1 template: metadata: labels: app: service1 spec: containers: - name: service1 image: nginx:latest ports: - containerPort: 80 name: port80

What parts of of the above specification do I really care about or control? Number of replicas, image tag, and the application port. That's it, three lines out of twenty. Everything else is boilerplate.

Instead of trying to fit our application into what Kubernetes wants, let's start with a specification that only includes the details that we care about. Based on the initial list of services that I want to run, we can define everything in as follows:

const services = { service1: { image: 'nginx:latest', replicas: 2, ports: [80] }, service2: { image: 'nats:latest', replicas: 1, ports: [4222, 8222] }, service3: { image: 'app:latest', replicas: 1, env: { VAR1: 'Some Value', VAR2: 'another value', } }
}

Starting with the specification like this keeps me focused on the details that I care about. You can clearly see how many applications I plan to deploy, and how those applications differ from each other. We can worry about YAML/Terraform/Helm details later. Moreover, I can change my mind about the YAML/Terraform/Helm question and still keep this definition as the starting point for everything I do later.

From Custom Definition to YAML

Converting this custom specification into regular YAML can easily be done using Handlebars templates. You can see the all of the templates here, but here is a small sample:

    spec:
      containers:
      - name: {{name}}
        image: {{image}}
        {{#if ports}}
        ports:
        {{#each ports}}
        - containerPort: {{this}}
          name: port{{this}}
        {{/each}}

The handlebars library is used in /generate.ts to create all of the YAML in one go. With this approach notice that:

  • All of my deployments are guaranteed to look the same.
  • If I need to make a change to all of my services (API version or namespace change for example) I can easily do it with one template change.

You can generate /generated.yaml by running npm run generate. You can then deploy all of the services in one go with kubectl apply -f generated.yaml.

Skipping YAML Altogether

In this repo I also demonstrate how to eliminate the need for YAML completely. Instead of generating YAML and running kubectl, I use the @kubernetes/client-node npm package to directly deploy to Kubernetes. This requires a little more work than YAML generation, but has several advantages. The biggest advantage is that the npm package includes TypeScript definitions for V1Secret, V1Service, and so on. This makes your deployment strongly typed and reduces the possibility of errors compared to authoring YAML. You can follow the /deploy.ts script to see how this all works, but at the heart of it is a simple loop:

for (var [n, s] of Object.entries(services)) { console.log(`Deploying ${getName(namespace, n)} started`) await makeSecret(namespace, n, s) await makeDeployment(namespace, n, s) await makeService(namespace, n, s) console.log(`Deploying ${getName(namespace, n)} complete\n`)
}

What Next?

I'm only starting to rethink how I want to manage and deploy my Kubernetes clusters. I think this is a good start, and I hope to share more learnings as I continue to experiment. As next steps I'm going to be looking into removing more of the "click-ops" that I do when creating my cluster:

  • Logging into the digital ocean dashboard to create a new cluster
  • Manually setting up the load balancer
  • Finding the load balancer IP address and configuring DNS to point to the new cluster
  • Configuring all the infrastructure (docker repo, AWS) and application secrets

As for challenges I foresee: In this example, the applications are defined generically such that you could use them even if you wanted to skip Kubernetes altogether. However, I have two Kubernetes-specific applications that I run in the cluster: KCert and ecr-login-renewal. These applications require special Kubernetes configurations around service accounts and permissions. I'm not yet sure how to cleanly encode those.

If you like this idea, give it a try in your own setup. If you're comfortable writing code in NodeJS/TypeScript, try out the Kubernetes client approach. Or if you're just looking to simplify your templates, give Handlebars templates a try.

无人机操作简单，使用寿命长，让您飞行而不必担心发生意外。 这款相机专门设计用于以无与伦比的 2 轴分辨率无线拍摄高清照片。 高清 1080p 视频和 4K 录制。

微型鹦鹉 ANAFI 无人机与其他无人机不同，因为它配备了 4K Wi-Fi 折叠臂和后置摄像头以及光圈和电动无人机。 借助广角摄像头和后置镜头，您可以通过名为 Parent Phone 应用程序的应用程序体验 360 度 ANAFI。

最终决定

无人机技术的进步创造了新的原创设备，可以制作令人惊叹的 4K、6K 和 8-K 分辨率视频无人机。 这些现在更适合制作 4K 视频的无人机制造商，因为大多数 1000 岁以下的最佳无人机都能够捕捉 4K 分辨率的视频。

item.description:
这款 2000 美元以下的最佳游戏 PC 是一个安全而直接的游戏开始。 它配备了最先进的技术，可以以极低的价格玩最新的游戏。 制造商制造的计算机市场不像 2022 年那么庞大。有很多选择通过提供更多数量和更高级的功能来相互竞争，而这些功能似乎对普通用户来说是无法使用的。 这可能会让您相信没有办法以最优惠的价格获得您的钱。
如果您是游戏爱好者，并且了解游戏设备，那么您使用的设备至关重要。 这就是为什么我们创建了一个低于 2000 美元的预建游戏 PC 列表的原因。

addrCanonName = Nothing}, host name: Just "cognito-idp.us-east-1.console.amazonaws.com", service name: Just "443"): does not exist (Name or service not known) addrCanonName = Nothing}，主机名：仅“cognito-idp.us-east-1.console.amazonaws.com”，服务名称：仅“443”）：不存在（名称或服务未知）

item.title: Appwrite 社区报告 #4 | Appwrite Community Report #4
item.description:
用于Appwrite的haimantika mitra

发表于大家好！ 来自 Appwrite 团队的问候👋

欢迎回到另一份每周社区报告，我们将在其中讨论过去一周 Appwrite 生态系统中发生的所有开源优点！ 这一次，我们有一些惊喜赠品，Appwrite x DEV Hackathon 进度更新、错误修复、新功能、使用 Appwrite 构建的 Google Docs 克隆等等！ 请继续关注到最后😉

把它带到 Appwrite hack 上！
  • 我们发布了一个与 Star Wars + Open Source 一致的秘密公告。 你能猜出它可能是什么吗？ 会是什么？ 它可能是错误的、有趣的，甚至是正确的！有资格获得🎁* 喜欢这篇文章

    敬请期待🤫

    我们听取您的反馈并尝试每周实施！

    以下是我们本周解决的问题：

    数组参数的默认限制，让 Appwrite 更安全

  • 用子查询替换 json 属性，使某些查询更快更安全。
  • 保存前通过 CLI 进行端点验证
  • 能够显示用户日志的运行时

    • 说说Appwrite的权限
    了解如何使用 Gordon Hayes 的 Appwrite 和 Flutter 创建 Google 文档克隆
  • 我们正在等待查看您构建的内容，请务必将它们放在这里。

    • Shabil Nedumpally - Shabil Nedumpally - Apr 28 4月28日



    原文:

    haimantika mitra for Appwrite

    Posted on Apr 30

    Hello everyone! Greetings from team Appwrite 👋

    Welcome back to yet another weekly community report where we talk about all the open source goodness that happened in the Appwrite ecosystem over the past week! 🤩

    TLDR; This time around we have some surprise giveaways, Appwrite x DEV Hackathon progress updates, bug fixes, new features, a Google Docs clone built with Appwrite, and a lot more! Stay tuned till the end 😉

    📢 What’s new:

    • Anom Chakravorty just built a snake game with Appwrite! 😮 Do you have a wacky wildcard solution too? Bring it on to Appwrite hack!
    • There’s a secret announcement we made that aligns with Star Wars + Open Source. Can you guess what it might be? 🤔
      🎁We are selecting a few random winners!🎁There’s some speculation with what our big news will be on May 4th. What will it be? An NFT? OSS related? 🤷‍♀️Take a guess! It can be wrong, funny, or even correct!To be eligible for a 🎁* Like this post

      * Comment with your guess

    P.S - We hear that there will be cool giveaways soon. Stay tuned 🤫

    ✅ Issues solved:

    We hear your feedback and try to implement them every week!

    Here are the issues we’ve fixed this week:

    ⚒️ What we’re currently working on:

    • Default limits on array parameters to make Appwrite more safe
    • Replacing json attributes with subqueries, to make some queries faster and safer.
    • Endpoint validation by CLI before saving
    • Runtimes to be able to display user logs

    🗣 Discussions:

    Let’s talk about Appwrite’s permissions
    🔗 https://github.com/appwrite/appwrite/discussions/2689

    📘 Resources:

    • Learn how to create a Google docs clone with Appwrite and Flutter by Gordon Hayes
    • 30 days of Appwrite is now updated, have you checked them out already?

      30 Days of Appwrite is a month long event focused at teaching you about all of Appwrite's core concepts and getting you ready to build production ready apps with Appwrite.

      We are awaiting to see what you build, be sure to drop them here .

    Shabil Nedumpally - Apr 28
item.author: haimantika

item.title: 您会使用应用程序来跟踪博客文章吗？ | Would you use an app to track blog posts?
item.description:
卢克·恩布雷

发表于我想开始一个新项目，但我正在寻找其他有同样问题的人。

有很多与开发相关的博主，他们都在自己的网站上发布新的博文。 您是否会使用一个网站 (SaaS)，每次您关注的某个博客发布新内容时都会通知您？ 您会收到一封电子邮件或短信，说您需要查看新帖子，并且每个帖子都可以很好地组织到阅读列表中。

您仍然会被定向到原始博客文章的网站，但要解决的主要问题是跟踪新博客文章并通知您并让您保持最新状态。

你怎么看，这是你会使用和支付的东西吗？



原文:

Luke Embrey

Posted on Apr 30

I want to start working on a new project, but I am looking to find other people who have the same problem.

There are lots of dev related bloggers out there, they all post new blog posts on their own websites. Would you use a website (SaaS) that notified you every time some blog you follow posted new content? You get an email or SMS to say you need to check out a new post, and each post could be nicely organised into a reading list.

You'd still get directed to the original blog post's website, but the main problem being solved is keeping track of new blog posts and notifying you and keeping you up to date.

What do you think, is this something you would use and pay for?
item.author: embluk

item.title: 明智地使用 package.json | Use package.json wisely
item.description:
阿米尔·H·易卜拉希米

发表于我们只解释下面这段代码

 "peerDependencies" : { "react" : ">=16.8" } , "peerDependenciesMeta" : { "react" : { "optional" : true } }

这是来自 npm docs 的确切报价：

在某些情况下，您希望表达您的包与主机工具或库的兼容性，但不一定需要此主机。 这通常被称为插件。 值得注意的是，您的模块可能会暴露主机文档所期望和指定的特定接口。

乍一看没有多大意义，让我们继续举个例子，
假设你有一个 npm 包，它只是一个反应钩子，如果你认为这很愚蠢，你应该看看这些愚蠢的存储库（ react-hook-form ， SWR ， zustand ）
所以你需要对你的包react ，因为你需要使用内置的钩子（ useState ， useContext ，...）
但这会使发​​布变得有点苛刻，因为您不想构建反应并将其捆绑到您的输出中。

让我们把它变成一个简单的钩子，我要做一个计数器，但这次用减速器:)

 import { useReducer } from ' react ' export const useCounter = ( initial ) => { const [ counter , inc ] = useReducer ( s => s + 1 , initial ) return [ counter , inc ] }

如果你不明白为什么将 react 捆绑到我的小 5 行 react-hook 中是个坏主意，我们应该聊天； 这可能是一个职业的好时机。 笑话够了； 这一小段代码的输出包大小将接近反应包大小的孔大小。

除了大小之外，您不能在一个项目中使用多个版本的 react，因此您必须强制所有开发用户像您一样使用确切的 react 版本。

如果不是，它会在您的屏幕上显示一个大警告，请安装“react”🥹。

但是如果找到一个，它会检查它是否与您作为反应值传递的版本兼容 (">=16.8")，如果不兼容，您知道🥹，但它不会在兼容的情况。

注意：当我写这篇文章时，r​​eact 18 已经发布，它似乎与以前的兼容，所以在那之前>=16.8可以访问所有钩子并且可以完成工作🚀

我不认识你，但我的安装过程是干净的。 这就是peerDependenciesMeta的设计目的。

这是来自 npm docs 的确切报价：

当用户安装你的包时，如果 peerDependencies 中指定的包尚未安装，npm 将发出警告。 具体来说，它允许对等依赖项标记为可选。

你看，我告诉你这是为了消除无用的警告在这种情况下，将对等依赖项标记为可选可确保如果主机上未安装 react 包，npm 不会发出警告。

"This allows you to integrate and interact with a variety of host packages without requiring all of them to be installed." “这使您可以集成各种主机包并与之交互，而无需安装所有主机包。” npm said with joy. npm 高兴地说。



原文:

Amir.H Ebrahimi

Posted on Apr 30

We are going to explain only the following piece of code

 "peerDependencies": { "react": ">=16.8" }, "peerDependenciesMeta": { "react": { "optional": true } }

Here is the exact quote from npm docs:

In some cases, you want to express the compatibility of your package with a host tool or library, while not necessarily doing a require of this host. This is usually referred to as a plugin. Notably, your module may be exposing a specific interface, expected and specified by the host documentation.

It doesn't make much sense at the first look, so let us continue with an example,
imagine you have an npm package that is just a single react hook, if you think this is stupid, you should take a look at these stupid repositories (react-hook-form, SWR, zustand)
so you need the react for your package, cause you need to use builtin hooks (useState, useContext, ...)
But this makes publishing just a little bit harsh, cause you don't want to build and bundle the react into your output.

let's make it a simple hook, I'm going to make a counter but this time with a reducer :)

import { useReducer } from 'react' export const useCounter = (initial) => { const [counter, inc] = useReducer(s => s+1, initial) return [counter, inc]
}

If you don't understand why it's a poor idea to bundle the react into my little 5 lines of react-hook, we should chat; it could be a good time for a vocation. Enough joking; the output bundle size of this tiny section of code would be near the size of the hole react package size.

Besides the size, you can not use multiple versions of the react inside one project, so you are crucially forcing all of your dev users to use the exact react version as you do.

The peerDependencies is here to save you a day, in case you passed the react to thepeerDependencies field in your package.json whenever the developer tries to install your custom package npm first looks at your node_modules and see do you have any version of the react installed or not. If it is not, it put a big warning on your screen that please install 'react' 🥹.

But if found one it checks whether it is compatible with the version you passed as the value of the react (">=16.8") or not, if it is not compatible, you know 🥹, but it won't do anything in the compatible case.

Note: when I'm writing this post react 18 has been released and it seems to be compatible with the previous ones, so till then >=16.8 has access to all hooks and can do the job 🚀

I don't know you but I one my installation process to be clean. and here is what peerDependenciesMeta is designed for.

What is the peerDependenciesMeta field?

Here is the exact quote from npm docs:

When a user installs your package, npm will emit warnings if packages specified in peerDependencies are not already installed. The peerDependenciesMeta field serves to provide npm with more information on how your peer dependencies are to be used. Specifically, it allows peer dependencies to be marked as optional.

You see, I told you this for eliminating useless warning In this case, marking a peer dependency as optional ensures npm will not emit a warning if the react package is not installed on the host.

"This allows you to integrate and interact with a variety of host packages without requiring all of them to be installed." npm said with joy.
