这是一种注入类型，攻击者可以在其中插入可以破坏身份验证的恶意代码/命令/查询。 在这种攻击中，攻击者主要将 SQL/NoSQL 查询插入应用程序中的特定输入字段，该字段进一步用于数据库查询。 该攻击主要绕过身份验证、授权，获取完整的SQL/NoSQL数据库的内容，并在数据库中执行创建、修改、删除数据等任务。

防止 SQL/NoSQL 注入攻击

对于开发人员来说，清理输入数据应该是一种非常常见的做法，这些数据可以通过可用的 NPM 包轻松管理。对于 SQL 数据库，node-mysql 包可用于数据清理。 如果使用 MongoDB，那么 NPM 包 express-mongo-sanitize 可以用于所需的任务。

这是攻击者以客户端（浏览器）端脚本的形式插入不需要的脚本的攻击类型，主要是通过编码或验证的输入表单。

防止 NodeJS API 遭受 XSS 攻击

  1. 对于这种攻击，我们可以使用 xss-clean NPM 包。 这种依赖主要是防止用户在输入时插入脚本/HTML。

  2. 在此攻击中，攻击者用于获取敏感数据，例如用户密码或个人 PIN/识别号码。 在此类攻击中，攻击者最有可能使用自动化软件来生成大量的值猜测，并尝试匹配任何一个与用户信息匹配的值。 使用2路加密保存的数据也可以被攻击者破解，因此开发人员尝试使用单向加密方法。

    防止蛮力攻击

    1. 如果你使用 express，那么 express-rate-limit NPM 包本身可以为你做很多事情。 这个 NPM 包有助于防止 DOS 和蛮力攻击。

    2. 最有效的方法之一是限制完成身份验证过程（如登录、OTP 检查）的请求。 这样任何自动化软件都不能通过多次请求来尝试破坏身份验证。

    3. 始终以加密格式保存密码、OTP 等数据，并使用不易解密的格式。 有很多可用的软件包可以完成这项任务。 我更喜欢使用提供单向加密并在加密后添加盐的方法。

    4. 最流行的方法之一是大型组织在两步验证过程中采用的最有效的这些攻击方法之一。

    结论

    这些对于任何 Web 应用程序来说都是非常常见的攻击，但是如果您使用带有 Express 框架的 NodeJS，那么处理这些攻击非常容易。 作为开发人员，我们应该在开发任何级别的应用程序时始终使用这些做法。



原文:

Being a backend developer, One of the most important task is to make the API secure from different types of attacks. As a backend developer, you could have heard about different attacks on servers like XSS, SQL Injection attacks, DOS attacks, and other similar attacks.

It is very necessary to write the code in a way so that it can be unaffected from these attacks. So in this article, we will see the necessary steps which should be taken while working with Nodejs to avoid such attacks.

Most Common Attacks:

1. DOS Attacks

DOS attack mainly crush/ shut down the hosting server, or a network that makes it unreachable for the intended user. No one will be able to use the hosted application. In this type of attacks, attackers use script to continuously sending the request, creating huge traffic on server simultaneously. There are two general methods of DoS attacks: flooding services or crashing services.Flood attacks occur when the system receives too much traffic for the server to buffer, causing them to slow down and eventually stop, And crashing service cause the target system or service to crash.

Prevent a NodeJS API from DOS Attacks

To avoid this attacks limit should be set for the input reqeust and If you are using express framework then its very easy for developer to handle these because express framework itself have builtin capabilities to handle this. For DOS attacks express has a built-in body-parse which can be used, We can use body-parser to limit the size of input body.

const express = require('express');
const app = express();
app.use(express.json({ limit: '20kb' })); // Here input body limit is 20 kb

Another npm package which can be used to prevent DOS attack is express-rate-limit, which mainly set the limit of requests coming from a specific user/machine. If user touches the limit provided in specific time then, NodeJS will lock him to make further requests and show him an error message.

const limit = rateLimit({
  max: 100,// max requests
  windowMs: 30 * 60 * 1000, // 1 Hour
  message: 'You have touched the maximum limit of request' // error message
});

app.use('/route', limit); // Setting limit on specific route

2. SQL/NoSQL Injection

It is a type of injection where attackers can insert malicious code/command/query which can break the authentication. In this attack, the Attacker mainly inserts the SQL/NoSQL query to a specific input field in the application which further gets used in the database query. This attack mainly bypass authentication, authorization, retrieves the content of the complete SQL/NoSQL database, and perform task like creating, modifying, deleting data in the database.

Preventing SQL/NoSQL Injection Attacks

For a developer, It should be a very common practice to sanitize the input data which can be easly managed by the available NPM packages.For SQL database node-mysql package can be used for data sanitization. And if MongoDB is used then NPM package express-mongo-sanitize can be used for the required task.

3. Cross-site scripting(XSS)

This is type of attacks in which Attacker inserts the unwanted script in the form of client(Browser) side script, Mainly over input forms which are encoded or validated.

Prevent a NodeJS API from XSS Attacks

  1. For this attack, we can use the xss-clean NPM package. This dependency mainly prevent users from inserting Scripts/HTML on input.

  2. Helmet NPM package helps you secure your Express apps by setting various/special HTTP headers.

// Data Sanitization against XSS
app.use(xss());
// For various HTTP headers
app.use(helmet());

4. Brute Force Attacks

In this attack, Attackers used to obtain sensitive data such as user passwords or personal PIN/identification number. In such attacks, attackers most likely uses automated software to generate huge guesses of values and tries to match if any one matches with the user information. Data saved with2 way encryption can also be cracked by the attackers, So developers try to use one-way encryption methods.

Preventing Brute Force Attacks

  1. If you using express, then express-rate-limit NPM package can itself many things for you. This NPM package helps to prevent from DOS & Brute Force Attacks both.

  2. One of the most effective approaches is to limit the request where authentication process(like login, OTP check) is done. So that any automated software can't attempt for breaking the authentication by macking the request so many times.

  3. Always save the data like password, OTP in encrypted format with format which can't be decrypt easily. There are so many packages available which does this task. I prefer to use the method which provides one way encryption and add a salt after the encryption.

  4. One of most popular approach which is being followed by the bigger organisation in two-step authentication process which works best of these attacks.

Conclusion

These are very common attacks for any web application, But if you are using NodeJS with Express framework then it's very easy to handle these attacks. And as a developer, we should always use these practices while developing any level of application.
item.author: pankajkumar

item.title: 聊天机器人第 1 部分：NLP 的幕后 | The Chatbot Part 1 : Behind the curtains of NLP
item.description:
我开始这个博客系列的目的是记录我构建功能性聊天机器人应用程序时的所有步骤。 然而，在撰写本文时，我已经将一个在 Flask 后端提供服务的聊天机器人模型连接到了一个 React 前端。 结果只用了几天。

我在 React 方面的经验和对客户端-服务器模型的熟悉使我能够相当轻松地连接模型和前端。 然而，构建聊天机器人的逻辑我从来没有费心去理解。 这是跟随教程的最大陷阱——你倾向于尽快看到成品。 现在，让 GitHub Copilot 自动完成大部分代码并没有帮助。

从那以后的日子里，我有时间反思整个过程。 尽管本文最初旨在涵盖构建聊天机器人模型所涉及的所有步骤，但这样做甚至会破坏完成所有这些的目的。 将理论与代码与实现分开是非常重要的。 所以我们从理论开始。 我向你保证，这绝对不会无聊！

自然语言处理 - 简称 NLP - 是人工智能聊天机器人的核心。 一个正常运行的聊天机器人应用程序依赖于模型理解人类语言及其复杂性的能力。

现在，计算机以你看到的数字进行思考和交谈。 那么如何在人类语言和机器语言之间进行翻译呢？ 这是我们深入了解 NLP 背后的一些核心概念的地方（基本上是我在尝试分解聊天机器人的功能时发现的所有新东西）

词干化与词形还原

词是复杂的东西。 它们的含义可以根据上下文而改变。 从一个句子中取出一个词，什么都不会改变。 或者一切都可以。 作为人类，我们已经习惯于自然地区分所有可能的变化而无需思考。

但是你怎么能教机器所有这些东西呢？ 简短的回答是——你没有。 相反，你教它根据模式行事。 你帮助它了解如何与你交谈。 根据您输入的性质回复您。 毕竟，这是构建聊天机器人的唯一目的，不是吗？

现在，如果是这样的话，我们可以自由地抽象出所有使语言如此复杂的细节。 如果我们从这个词中得到的只是它添加到句子中的上下文，我们不能减少说“gener”的慷慨和慷慨吗？ 如果我们还处理与一般等词可能混淆的问题，我们确实可以。 这是“词干”的核心——你将一个词简化为它的词根——也称为它的“引理”。

词形还原基本上做同样的事情。 但是，有一个关键的区别。 我不想自己解释，而是向您指出有关堆栈溢出的答案：

最后一点非常重要。 根据您的用例，您可以随意选择其中一个。 当我们到达那里时，我将讨论实施。

词袋

假设我们已经使用我们的样本数据完成了词形还原（或词干化）。 我们的模型现在应该知道要寻找什么了。 一个词汇。 请注意，它所知道的只是您对其进行的训练！

但是聊天机器人需要理解完整的句子。 这些句子可能包含多个不属于其当前词汇的单词。

我们将如何处理这个问题？ 再次记住，目标是让模型现在以某种方式为句子分配上下文——不多也不少。 我们需要根据其有限的词汇量找到一种方法来做到这一点。 这就是“词袋”（BoW）概念的用武之地。

尽管我更愿意将实现细节留到以后的帖子中，但如果不这样做，就很难深入研究 BoW 的概念。

聊天机器人应用程序——本质上是大多数 NLP 算法——执行所谓的分类。 这正是它听起来的样子。 您将输入分组到几个可能的类之一（在我们的例子中是“上下文”） - 预先准备好的类。 例如，我们可以构建一个仅理解上下文“问候”和“再见”的聊天机器人。 请注意，这些上下文纯粹是任意的。 知道了这个细节，我们就可以继续讨论一个词袋是如何在事物方案中变得直观的。

所以你有两件事 - 你的词汇和你的课程，即上下文。 当我们在 NLP 的情况下处理监督学习时（我们明确地训练它如何理解它的世界），我们也有我们训练它的数据（一组句子，每个句子都标有上下文）。 例如，假设我们的词汇表包含引理“hi”、“bye”、“fly”和“bear”。 假设我们的课程是“问候”和“告别”。

给定我们有标签的数据，我们获取我们拥有的每个句子，计算我们知道出现在这个句子中的单词的出现次数，即我们对参数（我们的单词）进行加权，并将该权重分配给训练数据中定义的标签。 每个句子基本上都归结为一个“词袋”。

例如，在我们的例子中，假设我们有句子“嗨！你今天早上好吗？” 在我们的训练数据中，带有“问候”的标签。 我们注意到它包含我们词汇表中的一个词——“hi”。 因此，参数“hi”的频率为 1 的句子必须将“greeting”类设置为 1，即“greeting”为“true”，“farewell”类为“false”/0。 从本质上讲，我们在尝试保留上下文感的同时将句子简化为数字。

使困惑？ 你可能想再读一遍。 或者看看这个超级有用的视频：

语料库

诚然，了解语料库的含义对于理解 NLP 或聊天机器人的工作原理并不重要。 然而，它是在类似项目中经常出现的术语之一（也是你可以随意抛出的那些花哨的词之一，以暗示你真正了解你的东西的人——不管你是否真的了解你的东西！）。

本质上，语料库是您标记的文本（或音频）数据——您最终用于训练模型的数据。 但是，它也可以扩展到用于对单词和句子进行词形还原的文本正文。 基本上任何带有标签的文本（或音频）正文。

结论

因此，我已经剖析了所有对于理解基于 NLP 的聊天机器人如何在引擎盖下工作至关重要的理论。 在接下来的文章中，我们可以（最终）深入了解实现和编码细节。 与往常一样，请随时让我知道您对这篇文章的看法。 并且看到大部分内容是根据我自己对事物的理解编写的，如果我不在任何地方，请纠正我！



原文:

I started this blog series with the intention of documenting all my steps as I built a functional chatbot application. However, at the time of writing this article, I've already connected a working chatbot model served on a Flask backend to a React frontend. It ended up only taking a couple of days.

My experience with React and familiarity with the Client-Server model allowed me to connect the model and frontend rather easily. However, the logic of building the chatbot I never bothered to comprehend. This is the biggest pitfall of following tutorials - you tend to get caught up in seeing a finished product as soon as possible. Now, having GitHub Copilot autocomplete most of the code did not help matters.

In the days since, I've had time to reflect on the whole process. Although initially this article was meant to cover all the steps involved in building the chatbot model, doing so would have defeated the purpose of even going through all this. It is very important to separate the theory from the code from the implementation. So we're starting off with the theory. I promise you it'd be anything but boring!

The basics of NLP

Natural Language Processing - NLP in short - is at the core of an AI-powered chatbot. A functioning chatbot application relies on the model's ability to comprehend the human language and its intricacies.

Now, computers think and talk in numbers you see. So how do you translate between human and machine language? This is where we get into some of the core concepts behind NLP (basically all the new stuff I've come to discover while trying to break down how the chatbot functions)

Stemming vs Lemmatization

Words are complicated things. Their meaning can change based on context. Taking a word away from a sentence and nothing could change. Or everything could. As humans, we've become attuned to naturally distinguish between all the possible variations without a sliver of thought.

But how can you teach a machine all these things? The short answer is - you don't. Instead, you teach it to act based on patterns. You help it understand how to talk to you. To respond to you based on the nature of your input. After all, that is the singular purpose upon which a chatbot is built is it not?

Now if that is the case, we're free to abstract away all those fine details that make language as intricate as it is. Cannot we reduce generous and generosity to say "gener" if all we're deriving from the word is the context it adds to a sentence? Provided we also deal with the possible confusion with words like general, we indeed could. This is at the heart of "Stemming" - you reduce a word to its root - also called its "lemma".

Lemmatization does basically the same thing. However, there is a key difference. Rather than explain it myself, I'd like to point you to this answer on stack overflow :

That last point is very important. Based on your use case, you're free to go with one or the other. I'll go over implementation when we get there.

Bag of Words

So say we've done our lemmatization (or stemming), using our sample data. Our model would now have an idea of what to look for. A vocabulary. Do note that all it knows is all you've trained it on!

But a chatbot will need to understand complete sentences. These sentences will likely contain multiple words that are not part of its current vocabulary.

How would we deal with this? Again, remember that the goal is to get the model to now assign context to sentences in some way or the other - nothing more and nothing less. We would need to find a way to do this based on the limited vocabulary it has. This is where the concept of "Bag of Words" (BoW) kicks in.

As much as I'd prefer to leave the implementation details to a future post, it'd be rather hard to dive into the concept of BoW without doing so.

A chatbot application - and essentially the majority of NLP algorithms - perform what is known as classification. And that means exactly what it sounds like. You group an input into one of several possible classes (in our cases "contexts") - classes that are prepared in advance. For example, we can build a chatbot that understands solely the contexts "greeting" and "goodbye". Note that these contexts are purely arbitrary. Knowing this detail, we can move onto how a Bag of Words becomes intuitive in the scheme of things.

So you have two things - your vocabulary and your classes i.e contexts. As we're dealing with supervised learning in the case of NLP (we train it explicitly how to understand its world), we also have the data we train it with (a set of sentences each labelled with their context). As an example, say our vocabulary contains the lemma "hi", "bye","fly" and "bear". Say our classes are "greeting" and "farewell".

Given we have labelled data, we take each sentence we have, count the occurrences of the words that we know that appear in this sentence i.e we weight the parameters (our words), and assign that weight the label as defined in the training data. Each sentence essentially boils down to a "bag of words".

For example, in our case, say we have the sentence "Hi! How are you this morning?" in our training data, with a label of "greeting". We notice that it contains a word from our vocabulary - "hi". So the sentence with the parameter "hi" having a frequency of 1 must have the "greeting" class set to 1 i.e "true" for "greeting" and "false"/0 for the class "farewell". Essentially, we're reducing sentences to numbers while trying to retain a sense of context.

Confused? You might probably want to read all that again. Or take a look at this super helpful video:

Corpus

Admittedly, knowing what corpus means isn't critical to understanding how NLP or a chatbot works. However, it's one of those terms that tends to come up a lot in a similar project (and one of those fancy words that you could casually throw around to hint to someone that you REALLY know your stuff - regardless of whether you actually do or not!) .

Essentially what a corpus is is your labelled text (or audio) data - the data you ultimately use to train your model. However, it can also extend to the bodies of text used to lemmatize your words and sentences as well. Basically anything that is a labelled body of text (or audio).

Conclusion

So I've dissected all the theory that would be essential to understanding how an NLP-based chatbot works under the hood as best I understand it. In the following post, we can (finally) dive into the implementation and coding specifics. As always, feel free to let me know your thoughts on the article. And seeing as a majority of this was written based on my own understanding of matters, please do correct me if I'm off anywhere!
item.author: jdprabasha

item.title: MongoDB CRUD 数据库操作🍃 | The MongoDB CRUD Database Operations🍃
item.description:
本文向您解释了 MongoDB CRUD 操作，任何数据库中的四个主要数据库操作。 它们使您能够从数据库中创建、读取、更新和删除数据。 创造

这就是您将新项目添加到数据库的方式。 这些项目可以是对象或单个项目。 如果该集合不存在，此操作将为您创建一个新集合。 在这种情况下，集合是指您的数据库的名称。 下面是一个如何将项目插入名为students 的数据库的示例。 您可以同时插入一个或多个。

读

此操作使我们能够从名称本身读取数据库中可用的信息。 我们使用一个名为 find 的方法来执行此操作。

在括号内，您输入您正在寻找的特定项目。

更新

此操作有助于对已经存在的数据库进行更改。 在括号内，您将指定要交换的值对或要在其中添加的值对。

删除

删除集合中不需要的对象或项目。 与插入命令类似，您可以同时删除一个或多个。

我希望这能让你的生活更轻松。

快乐编码！🍃​☕​



原文:

This article explains MongoDB CRUD operations to you, the four primary database operations in any database. They enable you to Create, Read, Update and Delete data from your Database. The CRUD acronym stands for;

C- Create

R -Read

U -Update

D -Delete

Create

The C stands for Create. This is how you add new items to your Database. The items may be objects or individual items. If the collection doesn’t exist, this operation will create a new one for you. Collection, in this case, refers to the name of your Database. Below is an example of how you insert items into a database called students. You can insert one or many at the same time.

db.collection.insertOne()

db.collection.insertMany()

Read

This operation enables us to read the information available in our Database from the name itself. We use a method called to find to do this.

db.collection.find()

find with no query inside returns the entire database.

db.collection.find(query)

Inside the bracket, you feed the specific item you are looking for.

Update

This operation helps one make changes to an already existing database. Inside the brackets, you will specify the value pairs you are interchanging or the ones you’re adding inside.

db.collection.updateOne()

db.collection.updateMany()

Delete

Delete the object or items that you do not want in your collection. MongoDB provides the following methods for deleting. Similar to the insert command, you can delete one or delete many simultaneously.

db.collection.deleteOne()

db.collection.deleteMany()

I hope this makes your life easier.

Happy Coding!🍃​☕​

item.title: 在哪里部署您的 node.js 应用程序 [2022 年 4 月] | Where to deploy your node.js app [April 2022]
item.description:
这些是我最近尝试部署后端应用程序的一些服务。

伟大的 DX、文档和社区。 与所有 npm 包不兼容。

开始

目前仅与 npm 兼容。 他们通过电子邮件快速回复。

铁路.app

铁路真的很有前途。 网络应用 UX/UI 让我想起了Linear 。 非常好的文档。

渲染网站

后端应用程序的类似 Vercel 的体验。 如果你在免费套餐上，有点慢。 出色的开发人员体验，易于使用和上手。

数字海洋

学习曲线略高，但很好的选择。 大量的教程和优秀的文档。

与数字海洋几乎相同。 真的很受初学者欢迎。

飞行控制

我还没有机会尝试的新工具，但看起来很有希望:)

如果您喜欢它，请考虑在 Twitter 上关注我:)



原文:

These are some of the services I've been trying lately to deploy my backend applications.

Cloudflare Workers

Great DX, docs and community. Free course on egghead.io. Not compatible with all npm packages.

Begin

Begin deploys your app to AWS while offering a "vercel-like" frontend. Only compatible with npm for now. They respond quickly by email.

Railway.app

Railway is really promising. The web-app UX/UI reminds me of Linear. Really good docs.

Render.com

Vercel-like experience for backend apps. Kinda slow if you're on the free tier. Great developer experience and easy to use and get going.

Digital Ocean

Slightly higher learning curve but great option. Tons of tutorials and great documentation.

Heroku

Pretty much same as Digital Ocean. Really popular for beginners.

Flight Control

New tool I haven't had the chance to try yet but looks really promising :)

If you liked it consider following me on twitter :)

item.title: 我喜欢 Chakra-UI 的 8 个原因 | 8 Reasons Why I Love Chakra-UI
item.description:
安詹·肖莫德

发表于在这篇博客中，我将告诉你我喜欢 Chakra-UI 的 8 个原因。

现在为什么要使用 UI 框架而不是自定义 CSS 样式？

原因可能是：

  • 你不擅长 CSS 或者你不喜欢它。
  • 从头开始的 CSS 工作量太大。
  • 你很着急。
  • 你在设计方面很糟糕等等......

视频教程

现在阅读博客后，如果您想开始使用 chakra-UI，可以观看下面的视频教程。

原因

  • 易于上手：Chakra-UI 易于上手。 只需安装软件包并开始编码。 这是从 nextjs 和 chakra-UI 开始的方法。
 yarn create next-app <my-app> cd <my-app> yarn add @chakra-ui/react @emotion/react@^11 @emotion/styled@^11 framer-motion@^6

然后在_app.js文件中：

 import { ChakraProvider } from ' @chakra-ui/react ' function MyApp ({ Component , pageProps }) { return ( < ChakraProvider > < Component {... pageProps } / > < /ChakraProvider > ) } export default MyApp

你可以看到很容易开始使用 Chakra-UI 和 nextjs。 在 Material-UI 等其他框架中，您必须为 nextjs 做一些额外的设置。

  • 漂亮的设计：Chakra-UI 的设计非常漂亮。 它很简单，没有任何不必要的动画之类的东西。 对眼睛好。


  • 主题：Chakra-UI 有一个漂亮而有用的主题。 一种
    主题只是预定义样式的集合，如颜色、排版等。您可以轻松创建主题并使用它。 从这里观看主题

  • 还有大量的速记属性可以让你的工作变得超级轻松

  • 轻松且非常可定制：Chakra-UI 非常可定制。 您可以根据自己的喜好轻松自定义它。 您可以覆盖整个主题。 添加可在任何组件中使用的自定义图层样式。

  • 在该组件中，您可以传递所有有效的 chakra-ui 道具。

  • 内置钩子：Chakra-UI 有很多有用的钩子，可以让你的工作变得超级简单。 例如，当您想要创建某种切换模式（如侧边导航）时， useDiscloure挂钩非常有用。

  • 与其他库集成：您可以轻松地将 Chakra-UI 与 framer-motion 和 storybook 等其他库集成。

所以，这就是我喜欢 Material-UI 的原因。

这就是今天的内容。 我会在下一篇博客中见到你。

在那之前，保持安全并快乐。



原文:

Anjan Shomodder

Posted on Apr 30

In this blog, I will tell you 8 reasons why I love Chakra-UI.

Now why would you want to use a UI framework instead of custom CSS styling?

The reason might be:

  • You are not good at CSS or you don't like it.
  • CSS from scratch is too much work.
  • You are in a hurry.
  • You are terrible at design and so on...

Video Tutorial

Now after reading the blog, you can watch the video tutorial below if you want to get started with chakra-UI.

Reasons

  • Easy to get started: Chakra-UI is easy to get started. Just install the package and start coding. Here's how to start with nextjs and chakra-UI. 
yarn create next-app <my-app>
cd <my-app>
yarn add @chakra-ui/react @emotion/react@^11 @emotion/styled@^11 framer-motion@^6

Then in the _app.js file:

import { ChakraProvider } from '@chakra-ui/react' function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) { return ( <ChakraProvider> <Component {...pageProps} />
 </ChakraProvider>
 )
} export default MyApp

You can see it is very easy to get started with Chakra-UI and nextjs. In other frameworks like Material-UI, you have to do some extra setup for nextjs.

  • Beautiful design: Chakra-UI has a very beautiful design. It is simple and doesn't have any unnecessary animation and things like that. Good for eyes.


  • Theme: Chakra-UI has a beautiful and useful theme. A
    theme is just a collection of predefined styles like colors, typography, etc. You can easily create your theme and use it. Watch the theme from here

  • Style Props: With Style Props, you can use almost any CSS property as props. There are also a huge amount of shorthand properties that will make you work super easy

  • Easily and very Customizable: Chakra-UI is very customizable. You can easily customize it to your liking. You can override the whole theme. Add custom layer styles that can be used in any component.

  • Chakra Factory Function: It has a factory function that allows you to make any Html element to the Chakra component. In that component, you can pass all the valid chakra-ui props.

  • Built-in hooks: Chakra-UI has a bunch of useful hooks that will make you work super easy. For example, useDiscloure hooks are really useful when you want to create some kind of toggle mode like side navigation.

  • Integration with other libraries: You can easily integrate Chakra-UI with other libraries like framer-motion, and storybook.

So, these are the reasons why I love Material-UI.

That's it for today. I will see you in the next blog.

Until then, stay safe and be happy.

item.title: 地图 | 🇧🇷 🇧🇷 | HackerRank #30 | Map | 🇧🇷
item.description:
比阿特丽斯·马希尔

发表于参考文献

地图：甲骨文
哈希映射：甲骨文
地图: DevMedia



原文:

Beatriz Maciel

Posted on Apr 30

Este problema pede para que, a partir de uma lista telefônica, seja inserido um número e retorne a mesma quantidade de contatos relativo à esse número.

O código começa assim:

//Complete this code or write your own from scratch
import java.util.*;
import java.io.*;

class Solution{
    public static void main(String []argh)
    {
        Scanner in = new Scanner(System.in);
        int n=in.nextInt();
        in.nextLine();
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++)
        {
            String name=in.nextLine();
            int phone=in.nextInt();
            in.nextLine();
        }
        while(in.hasNext())
        {
            String s=in.nextLine();
        }
    }
}

=========

O resultado final é:

package com.possible.map;

import java.util.*;
import java.io.*;

public class Solution {
    public static void main(String[] args) throws FileNotFoundException {
        Scanner in = new Scanner(new File("input.txt"));
        int n = in.nextInt(); // quantidade de contatos que vc quer
        in.nextLine();
        HashMap<String,Integer> listaTelefonica = new HashMap<>();

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            String name = in.nextLine(); // pega o nome
            int phone = in.nextInt(); // pega o telefone
            listaTelefonica.put(name, phone);
            in.nextLine();
        }

        while (in.hasNext()) {
            String s = in.nextLine();
            if(listaTelefonica.containsKey(s)) // se existir
                System.out.println(s + "=" + listaTelefonica.get(s));
            else
                System.out.println("Not found");
        }
    }
}

=========

Referências

Map : Oracle
HashMap : Oracle
Map : DevMedia

item.description:

AELA，2020 年。 Disponivel em： https: //medium.com/aela/cores-em-ui-um-guia-r%C3%A1pido-para-usar-em-seus-projetos-31ccffe3e16b Acesso em 06 de abr。 de 2022. 2022 年。



原文:

Guilherme Thomas

Posted on Apr 30

Trabalho apresentado para curso de Tecnologia em Analise e Desenv. de Sistemas.

Introducao A constante evolução tecnológica já faz parte do nosso dia a dia em casa e em nossas empresas, e obviamente também em nossos colégios e faculdades. Onde ontem usávamos apenas cadernos e giz de louca, hoje temos acesso ao uso de computadores pessoais, equipamentos de áudio e vídeo como projetores e microfones, além de utilidades que facilitam o dia de um professor tornando o ambiente de estudo mais interativo. Neste PIM temos a oportunidade de fazer com que o sistema de gerir o estoque de equipamentos audiovisuais de um colégio possa também ser atualizado, hoje sendo este gerenciamento feito de maneira manual e após a entrega do nosso sistema poderá ser realizado por aplicativo, agilizando e facilitando essa tarefa.

Como base para tal sistema, temos o auxilio das materias do atual bimestre, sendo elas Economia e Mercado onde apresentamos os agentes econômicos e a viabilidade econômica; Engenharia de Software podemos montar os Requisitos necessários para o Sistema; Projeto de Interface com Usuário conseguimos montar protótipos de interface demonstrando funcionalidades e maneira de uso; e por fim, Programação Orientada a Objetos I entrando na parte técnica do Sistema e explicando os conceitos usados de POO como heranças e classes.

2 MERCADO

2.1 Agentes Econômicos

Para haver em primeiro lugar um Mercado onde haja negociações, é necessário que existam agentes econômicos dispostos a realizar trocas e transações. Podemos considerar que agentes econômicos podem ser aqueles que criam uma determinada demanda de serviço ou produto, aqueles que ofertam produtos ou serviços ou mesmo entidades que realizem a regulamentação de algum desses processos.

Existem 3 principais agentes econômicos citados na teoria econômica que são as Famílias, Empresas e o Governo. Famílias são unidades consumidoras e também podem ser detentoras de fatores de produção (como terrenos e o trabalho, e em nosso projeto, o colégio Vencer Sempre); Empresas são as que produzem serviços e bens, e para isso usam os fatores de produção acima citados (terrenos, mão de obra); Temos também o Governo atuando na tentativa de diminuir desigualdades econômicas e na manutenção e eficiência do sistema econômico como um todo.

Definimos então que os agentes econômicos em nosso projeto serão nossa empresa de Desenvolvimento de Software como uma Empresa oferecendo um produto e o colégio Vencer Sempre composto por unidades de Família que necessitam deste produto.

2.2 Viabilidade Econômica

A necessidade do estudo da viabilidade econômica do presente projeto origina-se com intuito de projetar o retorno financeiro do mesmo, se haverá prejuízo ou lucro e no caso de possível lucro, quantificar o tempo necessário para tal.

É importante salientar que, como citado por Machado (2016) a microeconomia buscará o equilíbrio entre o mercado de bens e serviços na formação de preços entre oferta e demanda, buscando um meio termo entre acessibilidade de valores 8

para quem compra e contrata e maximize a renda de quem oferece.

O primeiro passo então é calcularmos qual será o custo de desenvolvimento desse software, quanto tempo levará para termos ele usável, quais imprevistos podem surgir durante seu desenvolvimento bem como riscos (por exemplo um funcionário abandonar o projeto, desistência ou quebra de contrato por parte da contratante, etc). 


Para termos uma ideia de custo, atuaremos nesse case como uma empresa onde há um desenvolvedor de nível Pleno e um desenvolvedor de nível Júnior, ambos com domínio da stack Java/Kotlin, além de um funcionário administrativo. Com base em pesquisa de mercado, o salário de um desenvolvedor dessa tecnologia nível Pleno é de R$ 6.100,00, um desenvolvedor Júnior de R$ 3.300,00 e um Assistente Administrativo de R$ 1.800,00. Adicionamos aproximadamente 30% sobre cada salário para encargos (Impostos, INSS). Somente com mão de obra teremos um custo mensal de R$ 14.560,00.

Com o fornecimento do material de trabalho, podemos colocar em orçamento dois notebooks com processador Ryzen 5500u ou equivalente, 12GB de RAM e um SSD de 256GB, configuração satisfatória para o cenário de desenvolvimento atual, para o administrativo podemos ter um notebook com uma configuração mais singela contando com processador Intel i3 10110u, 8GB de RAM e um SSD de 256GB que já atenderá as demandas de pacote Office e Web. Os 3 equipamentos foram cotados da marca Lenovo, facilitando questões de pagamento e logística, com valor total de R$ 9.500,00.

Estipulamos também que por se tratar de uma empresa em fase inicial, buscaremos um lucro reduzido para focar no crescimento definindo o mesmo em 10%, chegando então ao gasto inicial do primeiro mês de R$ 26.400,00. Colocando como o preço de venda do software o valor de R$ 8.000,00 e um custo mensal de assinatura de R$ 650,00 e projetando uma venda desse sistema a 9

cada 25 dias, em um ano e meio começamos a ter todas as despesas cobertas e uma porcentagem de lucro.

O Colégio Vencer Sempre então terá o custo definido em R$ 8.000,00 pela aquisição do sistema e um custo mensal de R$ 650,00 que contemplará tanto a manutenção de possíveis bugs como a realização de implementações extras. 

Ademais, existem diversas possibilidades para iniciar o case da nossa empresa de Desenvolvimento, onde podemos contratar desenvolvedores de maneira PJ ou freelance visando pagar apenas as horas trabalhadas, diminuindo nosso valor de venda e manutenção e podendo priorizar o fechamento de mais contratos. 

Para fim de comparação, nosso custo por hora para esses mesmos dois desenvolvedores seria R$ 52,80, e com um prazo de 20 dias úteis teríamos um gasto de R$ 9.292,00 no desenvolvimento desse software, e projetando para somente o sistema do Colégio Vencer Sempre ao menos 40 horas de manutenção ao mês, podemos diminuir o gasto mensal anterior em 70%, agora sendo R$ 5.640,00 (Administrativo, impostos e as 40 horas de manutenção do software).
Com dois desenvolvedores dedicados a criar esse sistema do zero, e devido a sua complexidade razoavelmente baixa, podemos fixar como prazo de entrega cerca de dois meses mais aproximadamente sete dias para realizar testes com ela já implementada.









                                              10

3 ENGENHARIA DE SOFTWARE II

Com o auxílio da disciplina de Engenharia de Software II, aluno ou grupo deve propor: Quais Requisitos Funcionais, Requisitos Não Funcionais e de Negócios serão necessários para a confecção deste sistema. Serão necessárias as especificações de Interfaces, das regras de negócio e das mensagens a serem exibidas.

3.1 Qualidade

Antes de se iniciar o desenvolvimento de um software, é interessante, até mesmo essencial, ter em mãos informações que garantem que o projeto do software irá atender exatamente o que é esperado. Para isso existem normas tanto nacionais quanto internacionais que norteiam a direção para existir uma mínima qualidade na entrega e manutenção de software. 

Podemos listar algumas como a - ISO/IEC 9126 que foca na qualidade do software e propõe seis (6) atributos, sendo elas: Funcionalidade; Confiabilidade; Usabilidade; Eficiência: Manutenibilidade; Portabilidade. E incluem subcaracterísticas dentro de cada desses atributos, a norma ISO/IEC 9126 permite visualizar o que seria necessário para definir essa qualidade empregada.

ISO/IEC 12207 que estabelece uma estrutura comum para os ciclos de desenvolvimento de software, essa estrutura consiste em processos modulares que são categorizados pelo seu objetivo no dito ciclo, sendo quatro (4) categorias, em resumo: Processos Fundamentais (envolvem aquisição, fornecimento, desenvolvimento); Processos de Apoio (envolvem documentação, configurações, validações, auditoria); Processos Organizacionais (envolvem a gerência, infraestrutura, recursos humanos) e Processos de Adaptação (que envolvem atividades básicas como linguagem usada, organização).

Além de outras normas ISO como ISO 10011 (que trata do processo de auditoria para sistemas), IEEE P1061 (Metodologia de métricas para produtos de 11

software) e também um modelo chamado CMM que apesar de não ser uma norma ISO, é uma avaliação de qualidade do processo de desenvolvimento de software muito bem aceita no mercado.

CMM que significa em portugues Modelo de Maturidade em Capacitação, é resumido como um complicado das melhores práticas de desenvolvimento presentes em outras normas e avaliações. O mesmo trata os níveis de maturidade em uma escala de 5 (cinco) itens, sendo o Nível 1 ou Inicial, e na sequência os níveis Repetíveis, Definidos, Gerenciados Quantitativamente e Em Otimização.
Podemos pontuar também algo escrito na própria guideline do CMM, página 14, seção 1.6

“...Sound judgment is necessary to use the CMM correctly and with insight. Intelligence, experience and knowledge must shape an appropriate interpretation of the CMM in a specific environment. That interpretation should be based on the business needs and objectives of the organization and the projects. A rote, checklist-oriented application of the CMM has the potential to harm an organization rather than help it…”

Em tradução resumida, o bom senso é necessário para se usar o CMM, e a aplicação passo a passo do CMM pode ser mais prejudicial a uma empresa do que o contrário.

E para basear a criação da nossa empresa fictícia de desenvolvimento de software, usaremos então com bom senso as orientações do CMM como norte. Sendo assim, podemos concluir que iniciaremos como Nível de Maturidade um (1), com as práticas de desenvolvimento do software em si alcançando o nível dois (2), pois estaremos já demonstrando o básico em gerenciamento de custos e da qualidade do software entregue.

3.2 Requisitos

Requisitos funcionais e não funcionais de um software, de forma simplificada são uma descrição do que o mesmo deve realizar para atender a necessidade do negócio ou cliente.
Também atualmente são utilizados conceitos diferentes de requisitos para definir os objetivos de um sistema, conceitos como OKRs (Objectives and Key Results), método que ficou famoso por ter apoiado o crescimento do Google e que foi criado pelo ex-CEO da Intel, onde os objetivos são qualitativos, e os resultados quantitativos; BDD (Behavior Driven Development) que é uma técnica para desenvolver software de maneira ágil baseada na integração de regras de negócio com a linguagem de programação, focando os testes no comportamento do software e alinhando a comunicação entre time e cliente final; Bem como conceitos de “Produto” e Métricas para Produto e “User History”.

É importante utilizarmos os requisitos demonstrados em Engenharia de Software II para montar uma base do necessário em nosso sistema de Gerenciamento de Estoque Tecnológico para o Colégio Vencer Sempre, e coletarmos feedbacks dos nossos usuários para poder ter uma melhora constante desse sistema.

Requisitos não Funcionais funcionam como uma parte do escopo do nosso produto, sendo qualidades ou restrições específicas a nossa implementação, sendo necessários para expressar a qualidade dentro do contexto do case. Podemos categorizar os requisitos não funcionais em partes, como Requisitos do Produto, Requisitos Organizacionais e até Requisitos Internos. Um exemplo claro e moderno é de um software que lida com informações sensíveis, a fim de atender os requerimentos da LGPD.

Regra de Negócio

Em 1989 a IBM anunciou um sistema para gerir o desenvolvimento de software chamado de AD/Cycle para sua SSA (Systems Application Architecture), sendo definido como “um requisito sobre as condições ou manipulações de dados expressos em termos de empresa comercial ou domínio de aplicação" criando então o conceito de regra de negócio onde é necessário um agente e uma condição. Regras de Negócio são um conjunto de operações condicionais ligadas a um resultado de dados; e dados são a essência dos sistemas de informação empresariais.

Juntamente aos Requisitos Funcionais e Não Funcionais, as Regras de Negócio constroem o conceito de Requisitos de Software, aliando o necessário aos usuários para que trabalhem na ferramenta, com premissas do desenvolvimento de software e critérios do próprio negócio que precisam ser atendidos.
Algumas das regras de negócio do nosso case envolvem o gerenciamento de material tecnológico do colégio Vencer Sempre, hoje o gerenciamento é realizado de maneira manual, levando tempo e mais suscetível a erros. As regras que já temos para essa organização são garantir que os equipamentos e salas que são reservados pelos professores estejam corretamente organizadas no dia para impedir que mais de um equipamento ou sala seja reservada no mesmo horário por professores diferentes, por exemplo.

Vamos então classificar nossas necessidades e caminhos que o software irá percorrer para permitir a reserva de uma sala ou equipamento, bem como seus avisos de confirmação e erro.

Sendo a reserva de equipamento nosso principal Requisito de Negócio, usaremos o exemplo e as demais Regras de Negócio ficarão em anexo a este projeto.

4.0 INTERFACE

4.1 Interação

Segundo Rocha e Baranauskas (2003) o conceito da Interface ser responsável apenas pela comunicação com o usuário, hardware e software evoluiu e devemos hoje levar em consideração mais aspectos humanos durante essa comunicação, logo, Interação e Interface são duas coisas que caminham juntas para criar o melhor ambiente possível.

Para criar nossa Interação, levamos em consideração muitos aspectos da usabilidade, principalmente as definidas pelas NBR ISO/IEC 9126-1 onde usabilidade é a “capacidade do software de ser compreendido, aprendido, operado e atraente ao usuário, quando usado sob condições específicas" (ABNT, 2003, p. 7).

A começar pela tela de Login, com espaços claros para inserir nome de usuário e senha, bem como requisitar acesso em algum caso específico do Professor ainda não ter sido incluído, e a opção de Redefinir a senha em caso de esquecimento.

Logo após, temos a tela do menu principal, com todas as funções (dos Professores), de onde os mesmos podem escolher o que será realizado, como por exemplo ir direto para a Reserva de Equipamentos, ou visualizar os equipamentos e salas disponíveis, checar o calendário, acessar o próprio perfil, pedir suporte ou realizar o logout do aplicativo. Tudo centralizado no mesmo local e de fácil acesso, com ícones distintos, cores sóbrias além da fonte escolhida ser de fácil leitura em qualquer aparelho móvel.

O foco do menu principal é ter uma produtividade veloz na hora de realizar a reserva do equipamento ou sala necessária, sendo essas opções as primeiras disponíveis na aplicação, o que garante também uma curva de aprendizagem muito baixa, afinal segundo (FERRÉ et al., 2001) os usuários não podem gastar muito tempo aprendendo como os sistemas trabalham, além de escolhas mais óbvias como garantir um menu em portugues e botões de tamanho médio pra grande.

4.2 Entrada de Dados, Processamento e Saídas

Para o funcionamento fluido do nosso aplicativo, bem como de qualquer sistema, é necessário que sejam fornecidas informações a ele. Todo algoritmo vai

18 precisar de um "input", seja ele um sensor, um botão, um campo para escrever e a partir disso as operações e cálculos serão realizados.

O conceito mais básico de programação e sistemas eh o modelo IPO, que significa Input-Process-Output, funcionando como exemplos do mundo real como o Sistema Respiratório, onde é feito o “Input” de Oxigênio, a Hematose pode ser considerada o “process”, onde é realizada as operações com o oxigênio garantindo a oxigenação das células, e o “output” sendo o gás carbônico antes presente no sangue sendo expirado. 

O mesmo acontece em diversas fases do aplicativo, na tela de Login é necessário que o usuário preencha as informações de conta e senha, onde o sistema irá verificar se a conta e senha batem com informações já armazenadas, então mostrando uma saída de informação booleana baseada nisso: se as informações estão corretas, o usuário é levado ao menu principal, e se nao estiverem corretas, uma mensagem de erro é exibida na tela. 

Tela de Reservas - Usuário escolhe as opções disponíveis da lista fornecida, apenas clicando sobre a mesma (input), o processamento é realizado por nosso código e as informações são salvas numa base de dados SQL, usando. 

Tela de Horários - Usuário determina o horário inicial da sua reserva com data e hora, por padrão o sistema assume o uso por duas horas mas o usuário pode selecionar mais horas de uso. Ao clicar em “Salvar Horários" as informações são enviadas a base de dados SQL e a mensagem com “Reserva Confirmada!” seguida da data e horários selecionados aparece na tela, acompanhadas de duas opções: Voltar ao Menu Inicial ou checar o Calendário.

Tela de Salas - Mostra todas as salas disponíveis de ambos os Prédios 1 e 2, nomeando cada sala, mostrando qual está sob reserva do usuário logado, o mesmo pode alterar a sala (input) e salvar sua sala com horário inicial informado também 

                                              19

podendo ser alterado, e as informações novamente são salvas e o output será a mensagem de confirmação de reserva informando o horário inicial e final da reserva.

A estrutura básica de entrada, processamento e saída de dados do aplicativo se manterá dessa maneira. Com a interface pensada em produtividade, eliminamos boa parte dos problemas citados no Livro-Texto - Unidade I, que podem comprometer o uso pelo usuário, como necessitar de diversos treinamentos; não lembrar de funcionalidades pouco utilizadas; não aprender todas as funcionalidades; levar tempo demais para realizar a tarefa desejada.

5 ORIENTAÇÃO A OBJETOS

5.1 Paradigmas de Programação

Um paradigma de programação nada mais é que a forma como o programador e equipe conduziram a construção do sistema ou módulo programado, é uma maneira de padronizar e determinar uma visão para aquilo que vai ser construído. Conforme as tecnologias foram evoluindo, podemos observar também a evolução constante das linguagens e seus paradigmas: Os primeiros computadores eram programados somente através de código binário, fomos evoluindo para o Assembly, Algol, Linguagem B, Linguagem C, C++, C#, Java, Python, Go e de diversas maneiras. Importante deixarmos claro que não existe uma linha de sucessão entre as linguagens, mas sim que foram sendo criadas conforme necessidades diferentes surgiam, e poderes de processamento maior são mais disponíveis. 

O Python por exemplo é uma linguagem com a sintaxe derivada do C, e inclusive sua implementação funciona por C, hoje a linguagem é usada principalmente para Análise de dados como Machine Learning e Data Science e também no desenvolvimento de projetos web. Cada linguagem citada possui atualizações recentes e continua em uso em determinadas funções, devemos 
                                              20

sempre ver a linguagem como uma ferramenta que vai nos ajudar a alcançar o objetivo desejado. 

O paradigma de orientação a objetos é um entres os quatro (4) paradigmas de programação, ele se baseia na interação entre diversas unidades que chamamos de objetos. É uma maneira de aproximar o mundo real do mundo da computação, facilitando a interpretação de conceitos para introdução em sistemas.

Com a Orientação a Objetos, a classe é uma estrutura fundamental e será nosso molde das estruturas, um exemplo básico do funcionamento:
Em um sistema de estoque de vendas de carros, um carro certamente estará em nosso sistema como uma classe, pois todos os carros possuem um padrão mais ou menos definido: eles têm quatro (4) rodas, um volante, aceleram, etc. Além de outras características como a Marca, o Nome do Modelo, ano de fabricação. 

Podemos declarar então, usando Java no exemplo, o ano de fabricação e a marca do veículo:

public class Carro { public int ano; public String marca;

}

Com isso, podemos definir as características dos veículos em nosso sistema, declarando agora os objetos que usarão essas características da seguinte maneira:

          Carro boxter = new Carro();
            boxter.ano = 2005
            boxter.marca = “porsche”

Agora temos como uma instância da class Carro, o objeto boxter, com seus atributos de marca e ano, e podemos definir muitas outras instâncias usando a mesma classe anterior.

Os objetos também podem ter ações em nosso sistema, como por exemplo de um carro, acelerar e frear. Em Orientação a Objetos a ação será chamada de Método, e podemos declarar esse método como:

       public void acelerar() {
                 System.out.println(“De zero a cem em 5 segundos!”);
                                          }

Logo, criar sistemas e aplicativos utilizando linguagens Orientadas a Objeto traz vantagens como ser mais rápido em criar algo pois podemos reutilizar o código, utilizando dos objetos que já criamos, não sendo necessário criar nada do zero; É fácil de realizar alterações pois podemos alterar métodos e classes, ou criar novas, que impactam todo o sistema; além de ser mais fácil de ser entendido por outro programador que estiver lendo um código já existente.

5.2 POO no projeto

Herança é um dos conceitos principais quando tratando-se de linguagens Orientadas a Objeto, trata-se da possibilidade de criar uma classe nova em nosso sistema, a partir de uma classe que já existe previamente, criando-se uma subclasse. 
Em nosso aplicativo, a nossa chamada superclasse ou classe "mãe" será a Usuarios, e nossas subclasses serão a Professores e Administradores. 
A superclasse Usuário terá a informação de ID para o aplicativo, a subclasse Professores terá então por definição o ID embarcado, e as opções de acesso a reserva de equipamento e reserva de sala, por sua vez, a subclasse Administradores será uma subclasse de Professores, contendo ID, os métodos de reserva de equipamentos e também o acesso a alterar, incluir e excluir professores e equipamentos no nosso banco de dados SQL.

Em Kotlin, podemos realizar essas operações de herança declarando no cabeçalho da classe após dois pontos (Classes em Kotlin ficam ao final). A estrutura então se define assim:

open class Usuario() class Professor() : Usuario() Fica claro ao decorrer do Bimestre, nas aulas apresentadas bem como em seu conteúdo de Livros-Texto e atividades, como a junção das matérias é fundamental para o desenvolvimento deste projeto, bem como para o desenvolvimento de software por si só. É necessário levar em conta a organização de tempo para a entrega satisfatória, a organização financeira para que o desenvolvimento tenha lucro e seja atrativo ao investimento por parte do Colégio Vencer Sempre; Atenção aos detalhes durante a produção do mesmo para garantir com que todos os Requisitos sejam cumpridos, o principal sendo a reserva de equipamentos por parte dos professores, cuidados ao elaborar os requisitos não funcionais e de negócio, pois apesar de estarem “fora” do uso dos professores ainda assim serão parte fundamental das estratégias de desenvolvimento.

Os processos de desenvolvimento de Interface, começando por um desenho básico em lápis e papel, até ser levado a um programa próprio a isso, organizar logicamente as etapas de telas, cores e fontes que tragam bem estar ao uso do aplicativo, bem como acessibilidade em questões de daltonismo ou visão prejudicada até a parte de Linguagem de Programação, em sua escolha, definição das regras de Heranças e Classes, entendimento do paradigma proposto para que seja possível usar esses recursos da melhor maneira, tudo isso é possível de se observar durante as etapas definidas do projeto, atendendo aos pontos principais do case e entregando o aplicativo.

REFERÊNCIAS

SILVA, Maria Valesca Damásio de C. Introdução às teorias econômicas. Salvador: UFBA, 2016.

CARDOSO, Rodrigo Pinheiro. Objectives and Key Results (OKR) Aplicado a uma empresa industrial: Um estudo de caso. Porto, Portugal: FEP, 2020.

GRADY, Jeffrey. System Engineering Planning and Enterprise Identity. Florida: CRC Press, 1995.

LEWIS, John. Java Software Solutions: Foundations of Program Design. 6 Edicao. Londres: Pearson, 2008.

Websites

CUNHA, Fernando. Requisitos funcionais e não funcionais: o que são? Mestres da Web, 2022. Disponível em: https://mestresdaweb.com.br/tecnologias/requisitos-funcionais-e-nao-funcionais-o-que-sao/. Acesso em 12 de abr. de 2022

GOMES, Vanessa. Métricas de Qualidade de Software. TI Especialistas, 2016. Disponível em: https://www.tiespecialistas.com.br/metricas-de-qualidade-de-software/. Acesso em 13 de abr. de 2022.

Salários de Java Developer em Brasil. Glassdoor, 2021. Disponível em: https://www.glassdoor.com.br/Sal%C3%A1rios/java-developer-sal%C3%A1rio-SRCH_KO0,14.htm?clickSource=searchBtn. Acesso em 07 de abr. de 2022.

AD/CYCLE: IBM'S SAA Application Development Solution. IBM, 1990. Disponível em: https://www.ibm.com/common/ssi/cgi-bin/ssialias?appname=skmwww&htmlfid=897%2FENUS290-529&infotype=AN&mhq=service%20initializer&mhsrc=ibmsearch_a&subtype=CA Acesso em 17 de abr. de 2022.

Cores em UI: Um Guia Rápido Para Usar em Seus Projetos. AELA, 2020. Disponivel em: https://medium.com/aela/cores-em-ui-um-guia-r%C3%A1pido-para-usar-em-seus-projetos-31ccffe3e16b Acesso em 06 de abr. de 2022.
item.author: guithomas

item.title: 优化网络图像的一条规则 | One rule for optimizing images on the web
item.description:
优化图像的一个很好的经验法则：延迟加载首屏以下的所有内容，急切加载首屏以上的所有内容，并将获取优先级设置为 high 。 以 webp/avif 格式提供所有图像。

尽管本机延迟加载现在正在受到关注。 我不推荐使用它，因为它只是在用户的网络很慢时才延迟加载，这是不可靠的，并且会损害您网站的带宽使用。 在此处阅读更多此新功能https://addyosmani.com/blog/lazy-loading/

只有 Chrome 和 Firefox 支持AVIF。 如果您在构建时生成图像，则使用 AVIF，如果在运行时生成图像，则使用 WebP。

至少有两种方法可以在 HTML 中提供优化的图像：

  • 图片元素
  • 两者的工作方式非常相似，但图片元素允许声明回退，而 img 元素不允许。

    如果您使用的是 Next.js，我强烈建议您查看 next/image 组件。 它具有很好的默认值，适应最佳实践，并且在小型和大型项目中表现出色。 https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/image-optimization https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/image-optimization



原文:

A good rule of thumb for optimizing images: Lazy load everything that is below the fold, eagerly load everything above the fold, with fetch priority set to high. Serve all images in webp/avif format.

Although native lazy loading is getting traction right now. I would not recommend using it, since it is only lazy-loading if the user's network is slow, which is unreliable and can hurt your website's bandwidth usage. Read more this new feature here https://addyosmani.com/blog/lazy-loading/

AVIF images are ~20% smaller than WebP ones, but they also take 20% longer to generate. AVIF is supported only by Chrome & Firefox. Use AVIF if you generate images in build time and WebP if in runtime.

WebP is well supported in all major browsers, except older versions of Safari, so remember to always have a fallback for JPEGs

There are at least two ways to serve optimized images in HTML:

  • Picture element
  • Img element

Both work very similarly, but the picture element allows to declare fallback whilst img element does not.

If you're using Next.js I highly recommend checking out next/image component. It has great defaults, adapts best practices, and works great in small & big scale projects. https://nextjs.org/docs/basic-features/image-optimization
item.author: bmstefanski

item.title: 数字世界为防止全球变暖做出贡献的 3 种方式 | 3 Ways Digital World is Contributing to Prevention of Global Warming
item.description:
所以说全球变暖是一个严重的问题并没有错。 无论我们生活在世界的哪个地方，我们都一定会感受到天气的不同。 造成这种情况的原因有很多，但是砍树是我在写这篇文章时关心的事情。

当我们从一个有混凝土丛林的地方旅行到一个被树木覆盖的地方时，我们一定都感受到了天气的变化。 这些树木显然为我们提供了新鲜空气、氧气、食物等。因此，不可否认的是，应该不惜一切代价拯救它。

据我了解，我觉得数字化在拯救树木和避免全球变暖方面有很大帮助。 我们将讨论数字化通过拯救树木直接帮助我们的各种用例。

本文主要关注造纸行业以及造纸行业对全球变暖的贡献。 因此，就像我一样，如果您不了解纸张制造机制，请不要担心我会为您服务。

首先，我们将讨论有关纸张制造过程的研究。 发布后，我们将讨论数字世界在减少其使用方面所起的作用。

造纸工艺。

免责声明：我从未参与过造纸行业，因此所有信息均基于我的研究。 此外，我们将讨论一个广泛的制造过程，而不是深入的。

基本上，造纸过程从砍树开始，这对任何事情都是一个明显的危险信号。 砍伐树木后，他们会通过清理树皮，因为树皮对造纸过程没有任何贡献。

尽管树皮可用于发电等其他用途。 在去除树皮过程之后，木材被送去切成小块。 然后将小块与水和其他化学物质一起煮沸，从而去除使混合物变软的树纤维。

去除纤维完成后，实际的纸浆就准备好了，使用一种化学物质，最终被清洗，使树有纸一样的质地。
最后，混合机将其转换成大片材，卷成大辊并包装。 包装好纸张后，可以将其打包到不同的地方。 根据需要，笔记本、书籍、日记本和包装纸由该片材进一步制造。

这是造纸过程的概述。 要考虑的主要事情是砍伐树木。 每个人都知道树木是大自然的礼物，即使砍伐一棵树也会以某种方式造成伤害。

主要是我们需要讨论技术在防止全球变暖中的作用。 技术的适应在帮助减少纸张使用方面发挥了至关重要的作用。 让我们讨论一下。

学校和学院。

所以我们大多数人在上我们的学校、学院或任何机构时都必须参加考试。 这些考试通常是离线的。 您需要回答一张答题纸上的一些问题。

在新冠疫情期间，几乎所有考试都在线进行。 这次在线考试极大地保护了树木。 和之前一样，我们不必在表格中填写答案，相反，我们只需要在在线门户网站上回答问题。

现在将参加考试的学生人数与使用的纸张数量相乘。 我们可以清楚地看到它节省了大量的纸张。 这张纸可以用在其他需要的地方。 就像在线考试一样，大多数学院和学校都鼓励在线提交项目。

该项目的硬拷贝，论文需要打印很多页。 现在，这直接影响了树木，因为对纸张的更多需求导致更多的树木砍伐。

政府/私人办公室。

如今，政府和私人组织正朝着通信和数据保存的数字技术发展。 他们中的许多人以 Google 文档、Microsoft Word 或任何类似软件的形式存储文档。

这有助于他们维护大量数据。 当需要搜索任何记录时，这也有助于他们。 显然，之前需要的人工工作被系统提供的算法所取代。 众所周知，组织需要维护数百万条记录。 当然，如果按照传统方式进行，对纸张的需求量会很大。

迈向数字世界帮助世界避免了全球变暖的灾难。 这是我们大多数人忽略的技术的一个积极点。

电子邮件。

谈到较早的时代，当我们需要发送一些信息时，有发送信件和明信片的传统。 将电话介绍为某人以减少信件的必要性。

但是，在需要保存记录的地方，即使是现在，首选的方式还是信件的硬拷贝。 这就是电话无法替换字母的原因。

邮件来救我们了。 使用信件的各个行业已经转移到邮件中，只需单击一个按钮，他们就可以在一秒钟内将数据、文件、文本等发送给另一个人。

尽管在某些地方，信件仍然被认为是一种正式的交流方式。 但是，其他各种休闲和正式使用信件硬拷贝的地方已成功地被电子邮件取代。

现在，如果我们考虑数十亿用户发送信件的硬拷贝进行通信，那么树木和其他资源（如燃料和电力）的数量将会受到影响。 我们认为这种贡献是理所当然的，但显然，它在避免全球变暖方面具有很大的潜力。

最后的想法。

我们知道有多种因素导致了全球变暖的世界危机。 每个人都在努力为避免全球变暖做出贡献。

上面提到的几点是技术为我们提供的一些好处。 作为受过教育的技术用户，我们应该尝试用技术替代纸张。

虽然纸张是可回收的，但总是最好减少它的使用。 技术对我们的影响还有很多其他方式。 但是这篇文章是您思想的食物，因此您也可以在其他地方推广技术的使用。 此外，如果您有一些其他人可以实施的想法，请在评论中分享它们，我们可以一起让世界变得更美好。

关于作者

你可以在Twitter 、 Linkedin 、 Telegram和Instagram 上与他联系。 订阅时事通讯以获取最新的策划内容。 不要犹豫在任何平台上说“嗨”，只需说明您在哪里找到我的个人资料的参考



原文:

So it won’t be wrong to say that global warming is a serious issue. Wherever we are living in the world we must have felt some difference in the weather. There are various reasons which are responsible for that, but the cutting of a tree is something which I am concerned about while writing this article.

We all must have felt a weather change while we are travelling from a place where there is a concrete jungle to an area that is covered with trees. The trees obviously provide us with fresh air, oxygen, food etc. So there is no denying the fact that it should be saved at any cost.

As per my understanding, I feel becoming digital has helped a lot in respect of saving trees and avoiding global warming. We will be discussing various use cases where being digital has helped us directly by saving trees.

Mainly this article is focused on the paper manufacturing industry and the contribution of the paper industry to global warming. So just like me if you aren’t aware of the paper manufacturing mechanism, don't worry I got you covered.

Firstly we will discuss the research about the manufacturing process of the paper. Post that, we will discuss the role that the digital world has played in reducing their usage.

Paper Manufacturing Process.

Disclaimer: I am never been a part of the paper manufacturing industry, so all the information is based on my research. Moreover, we will discuss a broad manufacturing process, not the in-depth one.

Basically, the paper manufacturing process starts with cutting the tree, which is a clear red flag for anything. After cutting the tree they are passed for clearing of their bark, as bark has no contribution to the paper manufacturing process.

Although the bark can be used for other purposes like electricity production. After the bark removal process, the wood is sent for chopping into smaller pieces. The small pieces are then sent for boiling with water and other chemicals, which remove tree fibres that make the mixture soft.

After the removal of fibre is completed, the actual pulp is ready, using a chemical that is finally washed to give the tree a paper-like texture.
Finally, the blending machine converts it into a big sheet that is rolled into big rollers and packed. Once the paper sheet is packed it can be parcelled to different places. Based upon the need the notebook, books, diary, and packing paper are further manufactured from the sheet.

That is an overview of the paper manufacturing process. The main thing to consider is the cutting of trees. Everyone is aware about trees are the gift of mother nature, cutting even a single tree is causing harm in some way or other.

Mainly we need to discuss the role of technology in the prevention of global warming. The adaption of technology has played a vital role in helping the reduction of paper usage. Let's discuss that.

The schools and The colleges.

So most of us while attending our school, colleges or any institute must have given exams. These exams are normally offline in nature. You're required to answer some of the questions on a piece of the answer sheet.

During this corona period, almost all the exams went online. This online exam resulted in the enormous protection of trees. Like earlier, we don't have to fill the sheets with answers, contrary to that we just need to answer the question on an online portal.

Now multiply the number of students giving exams versus the number of paper sheets used. We can clearly see that it has saved a lot of paper. This paper can be used in other places where it is kind of necessary. Just like online examinations most colleges and schools are encouraging online submission of projects.

The hard copy of the project, thesis requires a lot of pages to be printed. Now, this directly impacts the trees as more requirements for paper lead to more cutting of trees.

Government / Private Offices.

Nowadays the government and private organisations are moving towards digital technology for communication and data-keeping. Many of them are storing the document in the form of Google docs, Microsoft word or any similar software.

This has helped them to maintain huge data. This also helped them when there is a need to search for any record. Obviously, the manual effort that was required earlier is replaced by the algorithm provided by the system. As we all know that there are millions of records that an organisation need to maintain. Of course, there would be a huge requirement of paper if done the traditional way.

Moving towards the digital world has helped the world in avoiding the disasters of Global Warming. This is a positive point of technology that most of us ignore.

The Electronic Mail.

Talking about the older era there was a tradition of sending letters, and postcards when we need to send some information. Introduction of the telephone as someone to reduce the necessity of letters.

But still wherever there is a requirement of record keeping, even now the preferred way is a hard copy of letters. This was the reason the telephone was not able to replace the letters.

The mail came to our rescue. Various industries which were using letters have moved to mail in which they can send data, files, text etc to another person within a second with just a click of a button.

Although in some places letters are still considered an official way of communication. But various other places for casual and official use of a hard copy of the letter have been successfully replaced by electronic mail.

Now if we think about billions of users sending hard copies of letters for communication the number of trees and other resources like fuel, and electricity would be impacted. This contribution is something that we take for granted but obviously, it was having a lot of potential in avoiding global warming.

Final Thoughts.

We know there are various factors that are contributing to the world crisis of global warming. Everyone is trying to contribute in a good way to avoid global warming.

The above mention points are a few of the benefits that technology has provided us. As educated users of technology, we should try to replace paper usage with technology counterpart.

Although paper is recyclable but is always better to reduce its usage. There are various other ways as well in which technology is impacting us for good. But this article was food for your thoughts so that you can promote the usage of technology in other places as well. Also if you have some ideas which others can implement do share them in the comments, together we all can make the world a better place.

About The Author

Apoorv Tomar is a Software developer and part of Mindroast. You can connect with him on Twitter, Linkedin, Telegram and Instagram. Subscribe to the newsletter for the latest curated content. Don’t hesitate to say ‘Hi’ on any platform, just stating a reference of where did you find my profile

item.title: FIGMA 约束：使用 FIGMA 中的约束创建自适应布局的快速分步指南。 | FIGMA CONSTRAINTS: A QUICK, STEP-BY-GUIDE TO CREATING AN ADAPTIVE LAYOUT USING CONSTRAINTS IN FIGMA.
item.description:
约书亚·丁帕

发表于这应该可以帮助您控制设计在不同屏幕尺寸和设备上的外观。 在本教程中，我们将学习如何使用简单的工具来设计我们的 UI、使用约束创建响应式和自适应布局、设计简单的图标以及制作粘性导航栏。

在我们开始实践之前，让我们更多地讨论一下约束是如何工作的。

它是如何工作的

约束水平和垂直工作。 您可以沿 X（水平）和 Y（垂直）轴应用约束。

水平的
水平约束由沿 x 轴调整框架大小时图层的行为方式定义。

  • 左：保持图层相对于框架左侧的位置。
  • 右：保持图层相对于框架右侧的位置。
  • 中心：保持图层相对于框架水平中心的位置。
  • 比例：将图层的大小和位置定义为框架尺寸的百分比，然后在调整大小时保持这些比例。

垂直的
垂直约束定义了当您沿 Y 轴调整框架大小时图层的行为方式。

  • 顶部：保持图层相对于框架顶部的位置。
  • 底部：保持图层相对于框架底部的位置。
  • 顶部和底部：保持图层大小和相对于框架顶部和底部的位置。 调整大小时，它可能会导致图层沿 Y 轴增长或收缩。
  • 比例：将图层大小和位置定义为框架尺寸的百分比，然后在调整大小时保持这些比例。

注意：根据层的位置，多个约束可能会达到相同的结果。

如何将约束应用于图层

您可以将约束应用于框架内的任何图层，但无法对框架外的图层或自动布局框架中的图层使用约束。

  • 您还可以对嵌套在其他框架中的框架应用约束。
  • 约束可以水平和垂直应用于图层。
  • 您可以使用约束来定义层在滚动原型中应如何响应。
  • 您不能对组应用约束，组将从其中包含的图层继承其边界，并且该组被视为具有边界的单个图层。

注意：如果您将约束应用于组，Figma 会将约束应用于组内的各个层或元素。

让我们为层设置约束。 使用下拉菜单设置图层约束或单击交互式图表中的线条以选择约束 3) 如果您希望图层保持在同一位置，即使在滚动时，选中 FIX POSITION WHEN 旁边的框滚动

提示：按住 SHIFT 可选择或应用多个约束。 例如，左右约束。

有时您可能希望通过按住修改键来调整框架或图层的大小，而不使用应用于图层的约束；

  • 约束和布局网格

    约束为您提供了在框架内定位图层的主要框架。 当涉及到更复杂的设计时，我们需要更精确的控制和灵活性，约束确保我们的设计在调整大小时表现如何。 这就是当您在框架内使用网格时网格发挥作用的地方，Figma 会将框架内的任何图层与该网格对齐。

    现在让我们开始实际操作。

    第1步

    让我们在下面设计这个 UI。

    背景图片：Unsplash， https ://unsplash.com/photos/bTxMLuJOff4 字体：Roboto，H1 字体大小：96 粗体，H2 40 常规。 图标颜色：5DCB90。

    字体颜色：FFFFFF。

    处理约束是我们工作流程的关键部分。 当我们为多个屏幕进行设计时，元素、组和组件需要足够灵活以应对任何内容或屏幕变化，因此我们可以选择一个框架并在横向和纵向之间切换。

    组件默认总是left和top，当你尝试调整组件大小时，它们不会按比例调整大小，这就是为什么我们需要改变每个元素的约束，从默认的top和left到center和top，因为我们想要所有组件居中。

    第2步

    确保从图标组到导航组的所有元素和组约束都分配到顶部和中心，除了背景，它将被设置为顶部和左右，按住 shift 或从约束的下拉列表中选择控制板。

    现在尝试从横向切换到纵向，我相信我们的图层已经自适应，是的，就是这么简单。

    很高兴我们可以从横向更改为纵向，但并非所有组件都可以在一定程度上调整大小。 例如，我们的导航菜单和内容太多，无法容纳在手机等小型设备中。

    第 3 步

    让我们创建我们的第一个移动屏幕。 按键盘上的 F，转到手机，选择 iPhone x

    注意：在设计移动屏幕时使用布局网格，这将有助于将项目捕捉到正确的位置。

    在这种情况下，将布局网格设置为 8

    将所有内容从网页屏幕复制并粘贴到手机。 元素对于我们的手机屏幕来说太大了，我们需要一个接一个地调整所有组件的大小以适应我们手机屏幕的屏幕。

    让我们调整背景图像的大小，调整它的大小以适应 iPhone x 框架的屏幕，并将背景高度设置为 752dp，接下来是我们的文本。 按键盘上的 K 更改为比例（这有助于按比例调整组件的大小，并调整左右间距为 16dp 的文本。

    选择所有元素，包括背景，将它们分组并命名为 HERO。 对于图标，它们不适合，因为它们越来越大。 所以我们必须把它们分成两组，选择第一个、4个和组，接下来的4个仍然在原来的组内做同样的事情叫logos，将它们堆叠在一起，距离为40dp，logo组应该是距背景底部 40dp 的距离。

    将文字和观看按钮从徽标向上移动 40dp，并确保所有元素都居中。

    注意：我们在网络上的播放按钮在移动设备上不需要，因为它应该处于悬停状态，而我们在移动屏幕上没有。 通过单击图层面板上的眼睛图标将其隐藏。

    第4步

    创建移动导航栏
    在顶部，远离我们的图层面板上的英雄组，我们应该创建一个粘性移动导航栏，使用矩形工具创建高度为 60dp 和宽度为 375dp，这将跨越我们的 iPhone x 从左到右屏幕。 将我们的颜色更改为不透明度为 70% 的黑色，并添加 40% 的背景模糊效果。

    这个菜单的处理方式会有所不同，因为它不是本机应用程序，所以我们不会把它放在底部，最好我们使用汉堡菜单，因为我们的导航很多。

    要创建汉堡菜单，绘制一个宽度为 30dp 的简单矩形，高度为 2dp，圆角半径为 1dp，复制此矩形，间距为 8，将其设为一个组，并将其命名为汉堡，制作顶部和底部 24dp 并将其从矩形内的左侧移动 16dp。

    提示：Figma 中的一个很酷的功能，您可以对放置在组中的元素进行着色。

    所以选择组并为其添加颜色。

    让我们创建我们的明暗模式图标，使用我们的椭圆工具制作一个完美的圆形，按住 SHIFT、24dp 的高度和宽度，复制距离为 12dp 的圆形，选择两者并转到我们的布尔选择减去选择。 使颜色与汉堡图标相同，并将其从右侧和水平中心移动 16dp。

    选择所有创建的三个组件，将它们分组并将组命名为 MENU，将汉堡包的约束设置为顶部和左侧，将月亮图标设置为顶部和右侧。 对于我们的矩形，它应该设置为顶部，双选左右按住 shift 以适应它。 导航应该通过单击下面的对话框来固定，在此之前，该对话框表明 FIXED POSITION WHEN SCROLLING 将起作用，确保框架的高度比屏幕长，然后将高度设置为 1500dp。 您可以通过单击演示文稿进行测试，然后尝试滚动。

    注意：当您使用比例调整文本大小时，可能会在字体大小上添加一个额外的小数，最好将其更改为整数，以便您的开发人员可以更好地理解。

    第 5 步

    为最小的屏幕而设计
    对于最小的屏幕，我们将使用宽度为 320dp 的 iPhone SE。 复制我们的手机屏幕，在键盘上按 F，进入我们的设计面板，点击下拉框，然后选择 iPhone SE。

    我们需要做一些调整，就像我们在之前的移动屏幕上对我们的图标和文本所做的那样。 除了徽标和文字外，其他所有内容都保持不变。
    对于我们的徽标，按住 OPTION 用于 MAC 或 ALT 用于 WINDOWS 从侧面调整大小，这应该同时影响左右，确保左右有 16dp 间距，也可以通过使用比例在我们的键盘上按 K 来缩小或缩小文本，我们还应该在左右两边都有 16dp 的间距，记住要查看字体大小，因为缩放可能会为字体大小添加一个额外的小数点。

    结论

    我们所有的屏幕都完全适应每种屏幕尺寸，我们现在已经到了本教程的结尾。 你已经学会了如何使用约束来使你的布局自适应，如何使用布尔工具创建简单的图标，如汉堡包和明暗模式图标，并学习了如何制作粘性导航栏。 如果您没有按照教程进行操作，请单击可以测试最终结果的设计。

    Lakshan Dissanayake - Lakshan Dissanayake - Apr 29 4月29日



原文:

Joshua Dimkpa

Posted on Apr 30

Constraints in Figma tells Figma how layers should respond when you resize their frames. This should help you control how designs look across different screen sizes and devices. In this tutorial, we will learn how to use simple tools to design our UI, create responsive and adaptive layout using constraints, design simple icons and make sticky navigation bar.

Before we get practical, let's talk more about how constraints work.

How Does It Work

constraints work Horizontally and vertically. You can apply constraints along the X (horizontal) and Y (Vertical) axis.

Horizontal
The horizontal constraints are defined by how a layer should behave as you resize the frame along the x-axis.

  • Left: maintains the positions of the layer relative to the left side of the frame.
  • Right: maintains the positions of the layer relative to the right side of the frame.
  • Left and Right: this will maintain the layer size and position relative to both sides of the frame, and may cause layers to grow or shrink along the x-axis when resized.
  • Centre: maintains the position of the layer, relative to the horizontal center of the frame.
  • Scale: will define the size and position of the layer as a percentage of the dimension of the frame and will then maintain those proportions as you resize it.

Vertical
The vertical constraints define how a layer should behave as you resize the frame along the Y-axis.

  • Top: Maintains the positions of the layer relative to the top of the frame.
  • Bottom: maintains the positions of the layer relative to the bottom of the frame.
  • Top and Bottom: Maintains the layer size and positions relative to the top and bottom of the frame. It May cause layers to grow or shrink along the Y-axis when resized.
  • Scale: Will define the layer size and position as a percentage of the frame dimension, it will then maintain those proportions as you resize it.

NOTE: Depending on the positions of the layer, more than one constraint may achieve the same result.

How to apply constraints to a layer

You can apply constraints to any layer within a frame, it will not be possible to use constraints on layers outside of a frame or layers in an auto-layout frame.

  • You can also apply constraints to the frame you have nested within other frames.
  • Constraints can be applied to layers horizontally and vertically.
  • You can use constraints to define how layers should respond in scrolling prototypes.
  • You can not apply constraints to groups, a group will inherit its bounds from the layers contained within it, and the group is considered a single layer with bounds.

NOTE: If you apply constraints to a group, Figma applies constraints to the individual layers or elements inside the group.

Let us set the constraints for a layer. 1) Select the layer or parent within the frame, the blue dotted line on the canvas shows which constraints are currently applied

2) Adjust the vertical and horizontal constraints in the constraints settings in the properties panel.

  • Use the drop-down menu to set the layers constraints or click on the lines in the interactive diagram to select a constraint 3) If you want the layer to stay in the same position, even when scrolling, check the box next to FIX POSITION WHEN SCROLLING

TIP: Hold SHIFT to select or apply more than one constraint. For instance, left and right constraints.

Sometimes you may want to resize a frame or layer without using the constraints applied to a layer by holding down a modifier key;

  • MAC: Press down COMMAND when you resize
  • WINDOWS: Press down CTRL when you resize

Constraints and layout grids

Constraints give you a primary framework for positioning layers within a frame. When it comes to more complex designs, we need more precise control and flexibility, Constraints ensure how our designs behave when resized. That’s where grids come into play when you use a grid within a frame, Figma aligns any layers within the frame to that grid.

Now lets get practical.

STEP 1

Let us design this UI below.

Background Image: Unsplash, https://unsplash.com/photos/bTxMLuJOff4 Font: Roboto, H1 Font size: 96 bold, H2 40 regular. Icon color : 5DCB90.

Font color: FFFFFF.

Working with constraints is a crucial part of our workflow. as we design for several screens, elements, groups, and components need to be flexible enough to react to any content or screen change, so we can select a frame and switch between landscape and portrait.

Components are always left and top by default, and when you try to resize your components, they do not resize proportionately, This is why we need to change the constraints of each element, from the default top and left to center and top because we want all components centered.

STEP 2

Be sure all elements and groups constraints are assigned to the top and center from the icon group to the navigation group, except for the background, which will be set to top and left and right holding shift or selecting from the drop-down on the constraints panel.

Now try switching from landscape to portrait, I believe our layers are adaptive already, and yes, it is that simple.

It is nice we can change from landscape to portrait, Yet not all components can resize to an extent. For example, our navigation menu, and contents are too many to fit in smaller devices such as phones.

STEP 3

Let us create our first mobile screen. Press F on your keyboard, Go to phone, select the iPhone x

NOTE: When designing mobile screens use a layout grid, which will help in snapping items to the proper position.

In this case, set the layout grid to 8

Copy and paste all contents from the web screen to mobile. Elements should be too big for our mobile screen, we will need to resize all components one after the other to fit the screen of our mobile screen.

Let’s resize our background image, resize it to fit the screen of the iPhone x frame, and set the height of the background to be 752dp, Next is our text. Press K on the keyboard to change to scale(this helps to resize a component proportionately, and resize out text with a space of 16dp from the left and right.

Select all elements, including the background, group them and call it HERO. for the icons, they will not fit as they are longer and bigger. So we will have to split them into two groups, select the first, 4, and group and the next 4 do the same still inside the original group called logos, stack them on each other with a distance of 40dp, the logo group should be 40dp distance from the bottom of the background.

Move text and watch button 40dp upwards from the logos, and make sure all elements are centered.

NOTE: the play button we have on the web will not be needed on the mobile, as this should be on a hover state which we do not have on our mobile screen. Hide it by clicking the eye icon on the layer panel.

STEP 4

Creating a mobile navigation bar
At the top, away from our hero group on the layer panel, we should create a sticky mobile navigation bar, using the rectangle tool to create a height of 60dp and width of 375dp, and this will span from left to right of our iPhone x screen. Change our color to black with 70% opacity, and add an effect of a background blur of 40%.

This menu will be handled differently, as it is not a native app, so we will not have it at the bottom, it's best we use the hamburger menu since our navigations are many.

To create the hamburger menu, Draw a simple rectangle with a width of 30dp, and make the height 2dp with a corner radius of 1dp, duplicate this with a distance of 8 between, make it a group, and name it a hamburger, make the top and bottom 24dp and move it 16dp from the left inside the rectangle.

TIP: one cool feature in Figma, you can color group elements placed in a group.

So select the group and add color to it.

Let's create our light and dark mode icon, grab our ellipse tool make a perfect circle holding SHIFT, 24dp height and width, duplicate the Circle with a distance of 12dp, select both and go to our boolean select subtract selection. Make the color the same as the hamburger icon, and move it 16dp from the right and center horizontally.

Select all the three components created, group them and name the group MENU, set the constraints of the hamburger to top and left, and the moon icon to top and right. for our rectangle, it should be set to top, double select left and right holding shift for its adaptiveness. The navigation should be made fixed by clicking on the dialogue box below, which states FIXED POSITION WHEN SCROLLING before this will work, make sure the height of the frame is longer than the screen, then set the height to 1500dp. You can test this out by clicking on presentation and then try scrolling.

NOTE: when you resize a text using a scale, it could add an extra decimal to the font size, it's best to change this to a whole number so your developers can understand better.

STEP 5

Designing for the smallest screen
For the smallest screen, we will be using the iPhone SE which has a width of 320dp. Duplicate our mobile screen, hit F on your keyboard, go to our design panel, click on the drop-down FRAME, and select iPhone SE.

We will need to make some adjustments as we did on our icons and text on the previous mobile screen. Every other thing remains the same aside from the logos and text.
For our logos, hold OPTION for MAC or ALT for WINDOWS resize from the side, this should affect both left and right simultaneously, make sure this has 16dp spacing left and right also scale or text down by hitting K on our keyboard using the scale, we should also have 16dp spacing on both left and right, remember to look at font size as scaling may add an extra decimal point to font size.

Conclusion

All our screens are fully adaptive to every screen size, We’ve now reached the end of this tutorial. You have been able to learn how to use constraints to make your layout adaptive, how to create simple icons like that of the hamburger and dark and light mode icon using the boolean tool, and learned how to make a sticky navigation bar. If you didn’t follow along with the tutorial, click on the DESIGN where you can test the final result.

Lakshan Dissanayake - Apr 29
item.author: joshuadimkpa

item.title: macOS 和 Linux 有什么区别？ | What Is The Difference Between macOS and Linux?
item.description:
这意味着这些系统是相似的，但不是二进制兼容的。 此外，Mac OS 包含大量非开源程序，并且是基于非开源库构建的。 在这篇博文中，您将对Linux 和 Mac有一个很好的了解。

毫无疑问，Linux 是一个优越的平台。 但是，与其他操作系统一样，它也有局限性。 对于一组非常特殊的任务（例如游戏），Windows 操作系统可能会更好。 同样，对于另一组任务（例如视频编辑），Mac 驱动的系统可以派上用场。

您可能听说过 Macintosh OSX 只是具有更具视觉吸引力的界面的 Linux。 这不是真的。 但 OSX 部分构建在名为 FreeBSD 的开源 Unix 衍生产品之上。 直到最近，FreeBSD 的联合创始人 Jordan Hubbard 还是苹果公司的 Unix 技术总监。

由于 90% 的用户更喜欢 Windows，因此 Windows 在其他两个方面占主导地位。 值得在 Mac 上安装 Linux 吗？

一些 Linux 用户发现 Apple 的 Mac 计算机非常适合他们。 如果您需要在 OS X 旁边安装 Linux 操作系统，并且您知道自己在做什么，请安装它，否则就可以购买一台不同的、更便宜的计算机来满足您的所有 Linux 需求。

为什么Linux不好？

虽然 Linux 发行版提供了出色的照片管理和编辑，但视频编辑很差，甚至根本不存在。 没有办法绕过它 - 要正确编辑视频并创建专业内容，您需要使用 Windows 或 Mac。 尽管 Linux 比 Windows 安全得多，甚至比 macOS 更安全，但这并不意味着 Linux 没有安全漏洞。 苹果是 Linux 吗？

这个决定是在 DOS 的初始阶段做出的，随后的 Windows 版本采用了它，就像 BSD、Linux、Mac OS X 和其他类 Unix 操作系统继承了 DOS 设计的许多部分一样。 谁拥有 Linux？

谁“拥有”Linux？ 在其开源许可下，Linux 对所有人免费开放。 正如他们中的一些人所建议的那样，如果您发现从一个操作系统启动到另一个然后虚拟机是要走的路，我不介意启动到不同操作的操作系统，所以我使用 Bootcamp。

没有单一的方法来打包软件。 没有标准的桌面环境。 对游戏的支持不好。

桌面软件仍然很少见。



原文:

Mac OS is based on a BSD code base, while Linux is an independent development of a Unix-like system.

This means that these systems are similar, but not binary compatible. Furthermore, Mac OS contains a large number of programs that are not open source and are built on non-open source libraries. In this blog post, you will get a great perception of Linux and Mac.

Which is better Mac OS or Linux?

Unquestionably, Linux is a superior platform. However, it, like other operating systems, has limitations. For a very particular set of tasks (such as gaming), the Windows operating system may be better. And, similarly, for another set of tasks (such as video editing), a Mac-powered system can come in handy.

Is Mac OS built on Linux?

You may have heard that Macintosh OSX is simply Linux with a more visually appealing interface. It's not true. But OSX is built in part on an open-source Unix derivative called FreeBSD. And until recently, FreeBSD co-founder Jordan Hubbard was Apple's director of Unix technology.

Which is better Linux, Windows, or Mac?

Windows dominates the other two since 90% of users prefer Windows. Linux is the least used operating system, with users accounting for 1%. … Linux is a free operating system that anybody can download and use.MAC is more expensive than Windows, and the user is forced to purchase an Apple-built MAC system.

Is it worth installing Linux on Mac?

Some Linux users have found Apple's Mac computers to work well for them. … Mac OS X is a great operating system, so if you bought a Mac, stick with it. If you need to have a Linux operating system alongside OS X and you know what you're doing, install it, otherwise get a different, cheaper computer for all your Linux needs.

Why is Linux bad?

While Linux distros offer excellent photo management and editing, video editing is poor to non-existent. There's no way around it – to properly edit a video and create something professional, you need to be using Windows or Mac. …All in all, there are no real killer Linux apps that a Windows user would covet.

Is Linux Safer Than Mac?

Although Linux is considerably more secure than Windows and even a bit more secure than macOS, that doesn't mean Linux is without security vulnerabilities. Linux doesn't have as many malware, security flaws, backdoors, and exploits, but they are there.

Is Apple a Linux?

macOS, the operating system used on Apple desktop and laptop computers, and Linux are both based on the Unix operating system, which was developed at Bell Labs in 1969 by Dennis Ritchie and Ken Thompson.
This decision was taken initial stages of DOS, and subsequent versions of Windows adopted it, just as BSD, Linux, Mac OS X, and other Unix-like operating systems inherited many parts of DOS design. The Windows NT kernel serves as the foundation for all Microsoft operating systems today.

Who Owns Linux?

Who “owns” Linux? Under its open source license, Linux is freely available to everyone. Linus Torvalds, the founder of Linux, owns the trademark on the name "Linux.". Linux source code is copyrighted by its many individual authors and licensed under the GPLv2.

Does Windows work well on Mac?

Windows works great on Macs, I currently have Bootcamp windows 10 installed on my MBP 2012 mid and have no issues. As some of them have suggested, if you find that booting from one OS to another then virtual box is the way to go, I don't mind booting to an OS different operating, so I use Bootcamp.

What are the disadvantages of Linux?

Disadvantages of Linux OS:

No single way to package software. No standard desktop environment. Bad support for games.

Desktop software is still rare.
