item.title: 修复 - 找不到名称 '__dirname'.ts(2304) | Fixing - Cannot find name '__dirname'.ts(2304)
item.description:
亚历山大·金

发表于当我们尝试在 TS 文件中使用__dirname时会发生这种情况。

解决此问题的步骤：

安装节点类型： npm i -D @types/node

将类型应用于 tsconfig.json：

 " compilerOptions " : { " types " : [ " node " ], },

然后使用它：

 import * as path from ' path ' console . log ( __dirname )



原文:

Alexander Kim

Posted on Apr 30

It happens when we try to use __dirname in a TS file.

Steps to fix this:

Installing node types: npm i -D @types/node

Applying types in to tsconfig.json:

"compilerOptions": { "types": ["node"],
},

Then using it:

import * as path from 'path' console.log(__dirname)
item.author: avxkim

item.title: 关于锐化你的工具 | On Sharpening Your Tools
item.description:
Save some time to dream Save some time for yourself Don't let your time slip away Or be stolen by somebody else
忏悔的时候，小时候，我清楚地记得用我父亲的一把凿子作为螺丝刀。 我想我应该为此道歉。

昨天我收到了一封可爱的电子邮件，其中包含以下问题：

程序员花费一些工作时间来修复/锐化他们的工具是否很常见？

当我写下我的回复时，我请求允许引用他们的问题。 发件人有义务，所以这就是我写的（经过一些编辑以反映更广泛的受众）。

修复/锐化工具的价值

这是一个很好的问题，我有两种思路。

首先，我不知道您是否听说过 DEV，但这类问题是社区喜欢与之互动的问题。 我为Forem工作，为 DEV 提供支持的软件的维护者； 所以我对派人去那里有点偏见。

其次，让我们看看和我的具体情况和理解。

我假设在使用我的工具的过程中，我会探索这些工具以获得进一步的实用性或“生产力”收益。 这建立在我正在寻找结果而不是在座位/键盘上的想法的基础上。 请知道，我认为绝对令人愉快的一个结果是简单地玩电脑。 就像用刀无所事事地削木头一样令人愉快； 虽然有形的结果是一根尖棒，但无形的结果是一种惊奇感。 举个例子，我花了大约 2 个小时编写了一个 emacs lisp 函数，它帮助我整理了我们的拉取请求模板和提交消息的混合）。 花时间来做这件事有（或曾经？）一些结果：

首先，该解决方案为我每次拉取请求节省了大约 20 秒的时间。 自编写该功能以来，我已使用该功能大约 150 次，因此花费 2 小时节省了 50 分钟。 此外，节省下来的 20 秒还包括微决策。

通过自动化其中一些任务，我减少了一个可能导致决策疲劳的位置。

其次，我现在有一个关于如何做到这一点或什么是可能的心理途径； 这帮助我快速编写了一个脚本，以方便编写周末报告（10 分钟左右？）。

我现在每周使用这个新功能大约 3 次。 我可能会节省 30 秒的复制粘贴时间以及多个工具的上下文切换。

这种模式在其他任务中重复出现。

我早就映射了Cmd + . 在规范/测试和文件之间切换。 仅此一项就帮助我始终思考我的测试以及维持该路径所需的可预测文件系统结构。

我看过其他开发人员打开他们的项目导航树，然后单击文件夹以进入相关测试。 这会消耗决策资源。

我最近在安装tmr.el ，这是一个 Emacs 的计时器包。 为什么？ 因为我现在可以轻松地在 Emacs 中设置计时器。 我不需要拿起我的手机（这种不同的行为已经打破了我的思维）。

我预计会经常使用tmr.el吗？ 不，因为我在电脑前不经常需要计时器。 但它就在那里。 我练习设置它。

使用 Emacs 的最后一个案例是我为帮助我的 org-roam contexts所做的工作。 我可以选择一组标签（或一组命名的标签）来自动过滤我的 Org-Roam 命令：捕获、查找、插入或CaPF 。

这意味着我可以轻松地在同一目录中编写工作笔记和播放笔记。 然后，我既可以互连这些笔记，又可以帮助我不要“意外地”互连它们。 这有助于我删除决策点。

它还帮助我开始将我的博客文章从独立的 Hugo 文件转移到我的PKM系统中。

当我在处理一个我很好理解的问题时，我会尝试对我的编辑器进行所有这些“探索”。

当我试图通过更复杂或更模糊的事情进行推理时，我会避免尝试同时深入我的文本编辑器。 但我可能会快速记下告诉自己“嘿，你做了这件事……也许在你完成后花点时间考虑一下。”

我希望这能提供一些背景信息，说明您个人为什么要这样做，以及如何不是请求许可，而是让许可成为您的软件开发过程的一部分。

以一个问题结尾

现在我想知道，你在磨砺你的工具方面学到了什么？

学分

照片： Marco Verch在Creative Commons 2.0下的带有复制空间的木制背景工具



原文: 
Save some time to dream
Save some time for yourself
Don't let your time slip away
Or be stolen by somebody else
— John Mellencamp, Save Some Time to Dream

Confession time, as a child, I have a distinct memory of using one of my father’s chisels as a screw driver. I suppose I should apologize for that.

Yesterday I received a lovely email that included the following question:

Is it common for programmers to spend a bit of their working hours fixing/sharpening their tools?

As I wrote up my response, I asked for permission to reference their question. The sender obliged, so here’s what I wrote (with some editing to reflect a broader audience).

The Value of Fixing/Sharpening Your Tools

This is a great question and I have two trains of thought.

First, I don’t know if you’ve heard of DEV but this kind of question is one that the community loves to interact with. I work for the Forem, the maintainers of the software that powers DEV; so I’m a bit biased on sending people there.

Second, let’s look and my specific situation and understanding.

It is my assumption that in the process of using my tools, I explore those tools for further utility or “productivity” gains. This builds on the idea that I am looking for outcomes not time in seat/at keyboard. And please know that one outcome I consider absolutely enjoyable is the puttering of time simply playing with a computer. Much like idly whittling wood with a knife is quite enjoyable; and while the tangible outcome is a pointy stick, the intangible is a sense of wonder. A case in point, I spent about 2 hours writing an emacs lisp function that helped me tidy up the the blend of our pull request template and the commit message). There are (or were?) a few outcomes of taking time to do that:

First, the solution saves me about 20 seconds of time each pull request. I’ve used that function about 150 times since writing it, so I’m at 50 minutes saved with a 2 hour spend. In addition, those 20 seconds saved also included micro-decisions.

By automating a bit of those tasks, I’ve reduced one location that might cause decision fatigue.

Second, I now have a mental pathway of how to do this or what might be possible; which helped me quickly write a script to facilitate writing end of week reports (10 minutes or so?).

I now use that new function about 3 times a week. I likely saves me 30 seconds of copy paste plus the context switching of multiple tools.

This pattern repeats itself in other tasks.

I have long mapped Cmd + . to toggle between spec/test and file. That alone has helped me always think about my test as well as the predictable file system structure necessary to sustain that pathway.

I have watched other developers open their project navigation tree, and click through folders to get to the related test. That consumes decision-making resources.

I recently fiddled with installing tmr.el, a timer package for Emacs. Why? Because I can now easily set a timer within Emacs. I don’t need to grab my phone (which the vary act of doing already breaks my mental thinking).

Do I anticipate using tmr.el much? No, because it’s not often that I want a timer while I’m at my computer. But it’s there. And I practiced setting it.

A final case of working with Emacs is work I did to help with my org-roam contexts. I can select a set of tags (or a named group of tags) to auto-filter my Org-Roam commands: capture, find, insert, or CaPF.

What this has meant is I can easily write work notes and play notes in the same directory. I can then both interconnect those notes but also help me not “accidentally” inter-connect them. This helps me remove a decision-making point.

It has also helped me begin moving my blog posts from stand-alone Hugo files into my PKM system.

And I try to do all of this “exploration” of my editor when I’m working on a well understood to me problem.

When I’m trying to reason through something more complicated or vague, I avoid trying to also dive into my text editor. But I might make a quick note to tell myself “Hey you did this thing…maybe spend some time thinking about it after you’re done.”

I hope this provides some context on why you personally would want to do it, and how it’s not about asking for permission but to instead have that be part of your software development process.

Ending with a Question

Now I’m wondering, what have you learned in sharpening your tools?

Credits

Photo: Tools on wooden background with copy space by Marco Verch under Creative Commons 2.0
item.author: jeremyf

item.title: Bybit的Pybit：如何通过WebSocket订阅kline流 | Bybit's Pybit: How to subscribe to kline stream via WebSocket
item.description:
凯尔富

发表于截至撰写本文时，Pybit v2.1.0可供下载。

现在，让我们看看如何使用 Pybit Websocket API 流式传输实时 kline 数据：

 from datetime import date , datetime , timedelta import dateparser import time import hmac import json from pybit import usdt_perpetual # Apply for API keys and secrets at https://testnet.bybit.com/ api_key = "xxx" api_secret = "xxx" # Initialize web socket connection instance ws = usdt_perpetual . WebSocket ( test = True , api_key = api_key , api_secret = api_secret , ) # Initialize http connection instance session = usdt_perpetual . HTTP ( endpoint = "https://api-testnet.bybit.com" , api_key = api_key , api_secret = api_secret ) # Define a target price that you want to enter your position target_price = 40000 # handle_position is a callback that will be triggered on every new websocket event (push frequency can be 1-60s) def handle_position ( message ): data = message [ "data" ][ 0 ] if data : # check for target_price for entering position, if confirm=True, the data is the final tick for the interval. Otherwise, it is a snapshot. if data [ 'close' ] >= target_price and data [ 'confirm' ] == True : print ( "Buy Order | {} | {}" . format ( data [ 'close' ], time . strftime ( "%m/%d/%y, %H:%M:%S" , time . localtime ()))) tp = target_price + ( target_price * 0.05 ) sl = target_price - ( target_price * 0.05 ) # Buy at market price to enter Long position ret = session . place_active_order ( symbol = "BTCUSDT" , side = "Buy" , order_type = "Market" , qty = 0.001 , # amount to buy time_in_force = "GoodTillCancel" , reduce_only = False , close_on_trigger = False , take_profit = tp , tp_trigger_by = "LastPrice" , stop_loss = sl , sl_trigger_by = "LastPrice" , position_idx = 0 , ) print ( "Buy Order Id | {}" . format ( ret [ "result" ][ "order_id" ], time . strftime ( "%m/%d/%y, %H:%M:%S" , time . localtime ()))) # start kline stream here and pass in callback, symbol and desired kline interval ws . kline_stream ( callback = handle_position , symbol = "BTCUSDT" , interval = "5" )

参考官方示例： https ://github.com/bybit-exchange/pybit/blob/master/examples/websocket_example.py

Tomasz Wegrzanowski - Tomasz Wegrzanowski - Apr 29 4月29日



原文:

Kyle Foo

Posted on Apr 30

Bybit's python library has been released with a new version. As of the time of writing, v2.1.0 of Pybit is available for download.

Now, let's look at how to consume Pybit Websocket API to stream the realtime kline data:

from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta
import dateparser
import time
import hmac
import json
from pybit import usdt_perpetual # Apply for API keys and secrets at https://testnet.bybit.com/
api_key="xxx"
api_secret="xxx" # Initialize web socket connection instance
ws = usdt_perpetual.WebSocket( test=True, api_key=api_key, api_secret=api_secret,
) # Initialize http connection instance
session = usdt_perpetual.HTTP(endpoint="https://api-testnet.bybit.com", api_key=api_key, api_secret=api_secret) # Define a target price that you want to enter your position
target_price = 40000 # handle_position is a callback that will be triggered on every new websocket event (push frequency can be 1-60s)
def handle_position(message): data = message["data"][0] if data: # check for target_price for entering position, if confirm=True, the data is the final tick for the interval. Otherwise, it is a snapshot.
 if data['close'] >= target_price and data['confirm'] == True: print("Buy Order | {} | {}".format(data['close'], time.strftime("%m/%d/%y, %H:%M:%S", time.localtime()))) tp = target_price + (target_price * 0.05) sl = target_price - (target_price * 0.05) # Buy at market price to enter Long position
 ret = session.place_active_order( symbol="BTCUSDT", side="Buy", order_type="Market", qty=0.001, # amount to buy
 time_in_force="GoodTillCancel", reduce_only=False, close_on_trigger=False, take_profit=tp, tp_trigger_by="LastPrice", stop_loss=sl, sl_trigger_by="LastPrice", position_idx=0, ) print("Buy Order Id | {}".format(ret["result"]["order_id"], time.strftime("%m/%d/%y, %H:%M:%S", time.localtime()))) # start kline stream here and pass in callback, symbol and desired kline interval
ws.kline_stream(callback=handle_position, symbol="BTCUSDT", interval="5")

Reference to official example: https://github.com/bybit-exchange/pybit/blob/master/examples/websocket_example.py

Tomasz Wegrzanowski - Apr 29
item.author: kylefoo

item.title: 使用 SpriteKit 追踪 VFX | Trail VFX with SpriteKit
item.description:
视觉效果是在游戏中制作令人愉悦的效果的重要组成部分。 通常，VFX 用于子弹、弹道、魔法、传送门和无数其他事物。

前段时间我正在研究如何在 SpriteKit 中复制一些常见的 VFX 技术。 更具体地说，我希望能够制作拖尾效果来为子弹的外观增添趣味，因为玩家在游戏进行过程中升级它们。

这就是我最终得到的那种效果，只使用 SpriteKit 提供的功能。

尾随 VFX 细分

让我们从分解通常如何构建这样的效果开始，然后我们继续讨论如何使用 SpriteKit 实现这些技术。

该效果基于具有无缝轨迹纹理。 这可以很容易地在 Photoshop 中通过一些随机笔触创建，然后将边缘与偏移过滤器匹配以使其无缝。 但这超出了本文的范围。

独立移动轨迹

我们将在平面上应用此纹理，这是我们将添加到游戏世界中并在其中移动的 VFX 对象。 为了使纹理感觉动态而不是静态，我们将调整 UV 坐标，它控制纹理映射到平面的方式。

当我们在 UV 的一个边缘压缩坐标的 V 位置时，我们有效地压缩了位于另一边缘的纹理，因此我们从一个边缘到另一个边缘的纹理得到了锥形效果。

美感来了，如果我们在场景上移动这个平面，同时我们也移动纹理的 U 位置，以不同于平面使用的速度，纹理“活跃起来”，我们得到一个感觉纹理在其生命周期中不断演变和动画，而不是仅仅在场景中静态移动。

使用这样的紫外线，可能的影响可以更进一步。 我们可以使用弯曲形状而不是线性衰减来改变 UV，或者将其分段并稍微随机调整每个分段的大小以分解运动并将其移离静态纹理更远。

淡出尾随边缘

接下来，我们需要淡出轨迹的边缘，这样它就不会突然中断。 我们将在梯度图的帮助下做到这一点。

渐变图是我们用来控制效果不透明度的堆栈的顶层。 该层将保持在原位，不会像轨迹纹理那样在 UV 上移动。 通过将顶部渐变层与下面的层进行多重混合，我们最终在小径的边缘有一个很好的淡出效果。

其他调整

为了让轨迹更加耀眼和深度，我们将复制轨迹纹理两到三遍，并将它们堆叠在一起。 每个副本都会使纹理略微偏移，并以略微不同的速度在 UV 上移动。

当效果在游戏场景中移动时，这为我们提供了大量的动态变化。 在上面的 SpriteKit 效果视频中，我将轨迹纹理分层了两次，每一层以不同的速度值移动，然后混合在一起。

最后一点让它发光，用一点绽放来完成它。

这就是理论以及如何在大多数图形和游戏系统中实现这种效果。 因此，让我们看看我们如何使用 SpriteKit 来解决这个问题，并通过一些代码深入了解细节。

最大的挑战是当我们将纹理映射到 SpriteKit 中的节点时，我们无法操作 UV 坐标。 使用 SceneKit 将是一个不同的故事，但是哦，好吧。 由于游戏 VFX 通常很大程度上基于操纵 UV，我们必须想出一些变通方法来实现类似的结果。

步道设置

追踪层通常会严重依赖于操纵 UV。 由于我们无法访问 SpriteKit 中的 UV，让我们看看我们需要 UV 做什么，然后找到 SpriteKit 解决方案。

  • 使用 UV 纹理可以很容易地重复，所以当纹理在一个边缘离开表面时，它会环绕，然后从另一边缘进入。
  • 由于SKSpriteNode不具有移动和包装应用到它的纹理的功能，我们将通过自己重复纹理来解决它，将重复设置作为容器节点的父级，然后裁剪可见区域。

    我们必须自己处理包装。

    我们将需要纹理的三个重复，无论我们在什么位置观察它，这足以在每一侧都有足够的重复纹理以始终被覆盖。

    此设置将允许我们独立于父节点移动弹丸纹理。

    因此，让我们从设置路径开始。 我将它放入它自己的方法中，因此我们可以潜在地创建多个相同路径的层和堆栈以进行变化。

     func makeTrail () -> SKNode { let trailC = SKSpriteNode ( imageNamed : "trailTexture" ) let trailL = SKSpriteNode ( imageNamed : "trailTexture" ) let trailR = SKSpriteNode ( imageNamed : "trailTexture" ) trailL . position . x = - 512 trailR . position . x = 512 let trails = SKNode () trails . addChild ( trailC ) trails . addChild ( trailL ) trails . addChild ( trailR ) return trails }

    我们只需创建我们需要的纹理的三个实例。 我们有一个居中，然后我们将一个移到它的左边，一个移到它的右边。

    在上面的示例中，纹理的宽度为 512 点，因此我硬编码了值512和-512以将每个段向左和向右移动。 我们可以从纹理本身获得这个值，以使其更易于重用。

    然后，我们将所有段作为SKNode的父级并返回此层次结构。 这个轨迹对象基本上是我们需要能够独立移动和包裹轨迹纹理的东西。

    现在，只要我们需要新的轨迹来构建和分层效果，我们就可以调用makeTrail() 。

     override func didMove ( to view : SKView ) { // Get 2 wrapable trails. let trail1 = makeTrail () let trail2 = makeTrail () // Make the second trail slightly taller and change the blend mode to `add`. trail2 . zPosition = 1 trail2 . yScale = 1.25 for child in trail2 . children { if let child = child as? SKSpriteNode { child . blendMode = . add } } // ... Setup continues ... }

    在此示例中，我们创建了两个轨迹对象，因此我们可以将它们叠加在一起以获得更多变化，这将有助于将效果作为非静态纹理出售。

    通过在 y 轴上将其缩放到1.25 ，第二个轨迹对象略微变大。 第二条轨迹放置在第一条轨迹之上，因此第二条轨迹的每个子节点都将其 blendMode 更改为add 。

    移动和包裹纹理

    是时候让纹理移动了。 我们将使用SKCropNode获得一个窗口，我们可以通过该窗口观察我们的轨迹对象，然后为保存纹理的轨迹对象提供自己的移动循环。

    然后，裁剪节点可以在屏幕上移动，而轨迹纹理在其下方独立移动和包裹。

     override func didMove ( to view : SKView ) { // ... Setup continued ... // Create a huge hidden background. // This is explained further in the taper part. We need this for the warpGeometryGrid. let bg = SKSpriteNode ( color : . black , size : . init ( width : 4000 , height : 95 )) bg . zPosition = - 1 bg . isHidden = true // Mask with the crop node. let mask = SKSpriteNode ( color : . red , size : . init ( width : 400 , height : 95 )) let crop = SKCropNode () crop . maskNode = mask crop . addChild ( bg ) crop . addChild ( trail1 ) crop . addChild ( trail2 ) // ... Setup continues ... }

    我们在这里创建的巨大背景本身就是一个与 SpriteKit 的几何变形行为相关的故事。 因此，一旦我们到达锥度部分，将进一步解释。 我们在此处添加它，因为它需要是裁剪节点的子节点。

    遮罩基本上是我们的窗口，我们可以在其中看到裁剪节点内的内容，遮罩的大小成为我们效果的大小。 因此蒙版与轨迹纹理的高度相同，因此我们可以完全看到它，但宽度要小得多，因为我们将继续移动轨迹纹理并且只想水平看到它的一部分。

    因此，在设置遮罩后，我们只需继续添加我们希望通过裁剪节点看到的子节点（轨迹和背景）。

     override func didMove ( to view : SKView ) { // ... Setup continued ... // Keep moving the trails through the crop node. let move1 = SKAction . moveTo ( x : 512 , duration : 0 ) let move2 = SKAction . moveTo ( x : - 512 , duration : 2 ) trail1 . run ( . repeatForever ( . sequence ([ move1 , move2 ]))) let move3 = SKAction . moveTo ( x : 512 , duration : 0 ) let move4 = SKAction . moveTo ( x : - 512 , duration : 2.5 ) trail2 . run ( . repeatForever ( . sequence ([ move3 , move4 ]))) // ... Setup continues ... }

    然后我们需要移动轨迹纹理。 我们使用SKAction.moveTo(x:duration:)来添加纹理运动。 每条轨迹需要两个移动动作。 第一个动作的持续时间为0 ，因为它将纹理移回其起始位置。 这基本上是环绕。

    第二个移动动作使纹理移动到可见区域的另一侧。 此处的持续时间值设置速度。 我们将两个移动动作放入一个我们永远重复的序列中。

    在此示例中，我们使用了两条相互堆叠的轨迹，因此我们以不同的持续时间移动第二条轨迹以获得不同的速度，从而增加视觉变化。

    潜在地，我们还可以抵消其中一条轨迹的移动 x 值，以获得更多变化。

    我们有了它，一个可以在游戏场景中移动的父节点（裁剪节点），而纹理在其中独立移动并环绕。 此设置为我们提供了与两个 UV 项目符号中的第一个相同的行为。

    淡出边缘

    为了淡出效果的边缘，我们将完全按照上面的理论进行。 我们将采用渐变映射图像并将其与效果相乘。

     override func didMove ( to view : SKView ) { // ... Setup continued ... let opacity = SKSpriteNode ( imageNamed : "trailTexture-gradient" ) opacity . blendMode = . multiplyAlpha opacity . zPosition = 2 // ... Setup continues ... }

    我们准备一个带有渐变纹理的对象并适当地设置它的blendMode以便我们可以淡出效果的边缘。 我们将节点一起添加到效果层次结构中
    在下一步中使用锥形效果。

    我一直在考虑的另一种方法是使用SKShader ，它应该能够做同样的事情，但它会更清洁、更高效。

    锥形纹理

    是时候通过对列表中的第二个要点使用 SpriteKit 实现来处理尾迹效果的逐渐变细了，我们正在尝试复制 UV 行为。

    我最接近的是使用带有SKEffectNode的SKEffectNode。

     override func didMove ( to view : SKView ) { // ... Setup continued ... // The effect node to warp. let effect = SKEffectNode () effect . addChild ( crop ) effect . addChild ( opacity ) // Create the tapered coordinates. let destinationPositions : [ vector_float2 ] = [ vector_float2 ( 0 , 5 ), vector_float2 ( 0.5 , 0.9 ), vector_float2 ( 1 , - 2.5 ), vector_float2 ( 0 , 0.5 ), vector_float2 ( 0.5 , 0.5 ), vector_float2 ( 1 , 0.5 ), vector_float2 ( 0 , - 4 ), vector_float2 ( 0.5 , 0.1 ), vector_float2 ( 1 , 3.5 ) ] let warpGeometryGrid = SKWarpGeometryGrid ( columns : 2 , rows : 2 ) // Apply the tapered effect. effect . warpGeometry = warpGeometryGrid . replacingByDestinationPositions ( positions : destinationPositions ) // Control the quality of the distortion effect. effect . subdivisionLevels = 4 // ... Setup continues ... }

    我们使用SKWarpGeometryGrid扭曲轨迹。 基本上，我们只是创建一个坐标网格，我们可以将节点的右侧挤压在一起，所以当轨迹移动到效果节点上时，它会在右侧被压缩，而
    它在左侧展开并变宽。

    如果单独使用，这种方法效果很好，但与许多 SpriteKit 的东西一样，将部分组合在一起时会变得有点复杂。

    使用效果节点的subdivisionLevels属性来消除失真块状并获得更平滑和更清晰的外观。 较高的值确实会影响性能。

    你还记得我们之前创建巨大背景时的情况吗？

     let bg = SKSpriteNode ( color : . black , size : . init ( width : 4000 , height : 95 )) bg . isHidden = true

    现在是谈论它的时候了。 巨大的、隐藏的背景是为了确保 warpGeometryGrid 坐标保持不变。

    经纱不尊重裁剪区域。

    通过拥有比我们需要的更大的背景，经线不会四处移动。 否则，当轨迹在节点上移动时，它会根据如何计算单位坐标
    大它会看到每个帧的所有节点加在一起。 大背景抵消了这种行为并帮助它保持一致。

    这不是一个很好的解决方案，但这就是我单独使用 SpriteKit 能够做到的程度。 使用 CIFilter 作为替代选项可能值得探索，那里的转换可能比使用 warpGeometry 更一致。

    说到 CIFilter，我们已经创建了一个效果节点，为什么不继续使用它。

     override func didMove ( to view : SKView ) { // ... Setup continued ... // Add an additional bloom on top of the effect. let bloomFilter = CIFilter ( name : "CIBloom" ) ! bloomFilter . setValue ( 5 , forKey : "inputRadius" ) bloomFilter . setValue ( 1 , forKey : "inputIntensity" ) effect . filter = bloomFilter // ... Setup continues ... }

    效果节点可以在其属性中使用 CIFilter，所以让我们继续使用它来添加布隆过滤器以进一步增强效果。

    移动完整轨迹效果

    随着整个效果分层并组织在效果节点下的层次结构中，我们现在可以简单地将轨迹移动到我们需要的任何地方，以便我移动效果节点。

     override func didMove ( to view : SKView ) { // ... Setup continued ... // Move the entire projectile over the screen. effect . run ( SKAction . moveTo ( x : 700 , duration : 3 )) effect . addChild ( crop ) }

    这就是它的全部。 我们在我们希望射弹去的地方开始一个动画，然后将它添加到场景中。

    在本文中，我出于方便，只是在didMove(to:)方法中添加了设置。 在trailEffect()->SKNode方法中具有此功能会更有意义，该方法将设置跟踪并返回其节点以使其按需实例化。

    然后返回的节点可以获得分配的适当移动动作，然后添加到场景中。

    使用 SKEffectNode 和 SKCropNode 在性能方面可能会很重。 降低尺寸对效果的影响应该会对性能产生积极影响。

    下一步

    尽管该解决方案无法与您使用现代游戏引擎所做的竞争，而且该解决方案还发现了 SpriteKit 的一些缺陷（也称为错误），但它仍然可以为SpriteKit 项目。

    动态轨迹 VFX 可以是许多不同效果的构建块和基础。 在此示例中继续构建小径，我将使用动画耀斑之类的东西在小径上添加一个头部。 最重要的是，可以添加一个小粒子发射器作为最后一层，发射一些闪闪发光的纹理来帮助实现效果。



原文:

Visual Effects are an important part of making pleasing effects in games. Commonly, VFX is used for things like bullets, projectile trails, magic, portals, and countless other things.

I was doing research some time ago on how to be able to replicate some common VFX techniques in SpriteKit. More specifically, I wanted to be able to make trailing effects to spice up the look of bullets as the player upgraded them while progressing through the game.

This is the kind of effect I ended up with, using only the functionality provided by SpriteKit.

Trailing VFX Breakdown

Let's start by breaking down how an effect like this is usually constructed, and then we continue on how to implement those techniques using SpriteKit.

The effect is based on having a seamless trail texture. This can be created in Photoshop quite easily with some random strokes and then matching up the edges with the offset filter to make it seamless. But that's out of the scope of this article.

Moving the Trail Independently

We are going to apply this texture on a plane, which is the VFX object that we will add to and move around in our game world. To make the texture feel dynamic instead of static, we are going to tweak the UV coordinates, which controls how the texture maps to the plane.

When we compress the V position of the coordinates on one edge of the UV, we effectively squeeze the texture located on the opposite edge, so we get a tapered effect on the texture from one edge to the other.

And here comes the beauty, if we move this plane over the scene, and at the same time we also move the U position of the texture, at a different speed than the plane is using, the texture "comes alive" and we get a sense that the texture constantly evolves and animates during its lifetime instead of just statically move over the scene.

Working with a UV like this, the possible effects can be taken much further. We could alter the UV using a curved shape instead of a linear falloff, or segment it up and randomly adjust each segment's size slightly to break up the movement and move it even further away from a static texture.

Fading Out the Trail Edge

Next, we are going to need to fade out the edge of the trail so it doesn't cut off abruptly. We will do that with the help of a gradient map.

The gradient map is the top layer of the stack which we are using to control the opacity of the effect. This layer will remain in place and not move over the UV like the trail texture does. By doing a multiply blend of the top gradient layer with the layers below, we end up with a nice fade out at the edge of the trail.

Additional Tweaks

To give the trails a bit more flare and depth, we are going to duplicate the trail texture two or three times and stack them on top of each other. Each copy will have the texture slightly offset and move over the UV at a slightly different speed.

This gives us tons of dynamic variation as the effect moves over the game scene. In the video of my SpriteKit effect above, I've layered the trail texture two times and each layer moves with different speed values and is then blended together.

And for the final touch to make it glow, top it off with a bit of bloom.

SpriteKit Implementation

That was it for the theory and how one would achieve this kind of effect in most graphics and game systems. So let's see how we can approach this using SpriteKit and get into the nitty gritty details with some code.

The biggest challenge is that we don't have access to manipulate UV coordinates when we map textures to nodes in SpriteKit. With SceneKit it would have been a different story, but oh well. As game VFX is often heavily based on manipulating UVs, we have to come up with some workarounds to achieve similar outcomes.

The Trail Setup

The trail layer would usually rely heavily on manipulating UVs. As we don't have access to UVs in SpriteKit, let's see what we would need the UVs to do, and then find SpriteKit solutions.

  • With UVs textures can easily be repeated, so when the texture leaves the surface on one edge, it wraps around, and comes in on the opposite edge.
  • UVs allow us to compress and resize parts of the texture independently of how the displayed surface is sized.

As SKSpriteNode does not have functionality to move and wrap a texture applied to it, we'll solve it by repeating the texture ourselves, parent the repeated setup to a container node, and then crop out the visible area.

We'll have to handle the wrapping ourselves.

We will need three repetitions of the texture, which is enough no matter at what position we are observing it, to have enough repeating texture on each side to always be covered.

This setup will allow us to move the projectile texture independently of the parent node.

So let's begin with setting up the trail. I put this into it's own method so we can potentially create multiple layers of the same trail and stack for variation.

func makeTrail() -> SKNode { let trailC = SKSpriteNode(imageNamed: "trailTexture") let trailL = SKSpriteNode(imageNamed: "trailTexture") let trailR = SKSpriteNode(imageNamed: "trailTexture") trailL.position.x = -512 trailR.position.x = 512 let trails = SKNode() trails.addChild(trailC) trails.addChild(trailL) trails.addChild(trailR) return trails
}

We simply create the three instances we need of the texture. We have one centered, and then we move one to the left of it and one to the right of it.

In the example above, the texture is 512 points wide, so I've hard-coded the values 512 and -512 to move each segment to the left and right. We could have got this value from the texture itself, to make it more reusable.

We then parent all the segments to an SKNode and return this hierarchy. This trail object is basically what we need to be able to move and wrap the trail texture independently.

Now we can call makeTrail() whenever we need a new trail to build and layer up an effect.

override func didMove(to view: SKView) { // Get 2 wrapable trails. let trail1 = makeTrail() let trail2 = makeTrail() // Make the second trail slightly taller and change the blend mode to `add`. trail2.zPosition = 1 trail2.yScale = 1.25 for child in trail2.children { if let child = child as? SKSpriteNode { child.blendMode = .add } } // ... Setup continues ...
}

In this example we create two trail objects, so we can layer them on top of each other for more variation that will help sell the effect as a non-static texture.

The second trail object is made slightly larger by scaling it to 1.25 on the y-axis. The second trail is placed on top of the first, so each child of the second trail has its blendMode changed to add.

Move and Wrap the Texture

Time to get the texture moving. We will use the SKCropNode to get a window that we can observe our trail object through, and then give the trail object that holds the texture its own movement loop.

The crop node can then move around on the screen while the trail texture moves and wraps independently beneath it.

override func didMove(to view: SKView) { // ... Setup continued ... // Create a huge hidden background. // This is explained further in the taper part. We need this for the warpGeometryGrid. let bg = SKSpriteNode(color: .black, size: .init(width: 4000, height: 95)) bg.zPosition = -1 bg.isHidden = true // Mask with the crop node. let mask = SKSpriteNode(color: .red, size: .init(width: 400, height: 95)) let crop = SKCropNode() crop.maskNode = mask crop.addChild(bg) crop.addChild(trail1) crop.addChild(trail2) // ... Setup continues ...
}

The huge background we create here is a story by itself that is related to SpriteKit's geometry warping behavior. So it will be explained further once we get to the taper section. We add it here as it needs to be a child to the crop node.

SKCropNode needs a mask that defines our cropping area. The mask is basically our window where we can see what's inside the crop node, and the size of the mask becomes the size of our effect. So the mask is of the same height as the trail texture so we can fully see it, but much less wide, as we will keep moving the trail texture and only want to see a part of it horizontally.

So after setting the mask, we just keep adding children (the trails and the background) that we want to see through the crop node.

override func didMove(to view: SKView) { // ... Setup continued ... // Keep moving the trails through the crop node. let move1 = SKAction.moveTo(x: 512, duration: 0) let move2 = SKAction.moveTo(x: -512, duration: 2) trail1.run(.repeatForever(.sequence([move1, move2]))) let move3 = SKAction.moveTo(x: 512, duration: 0) let move4 = SKAction.moveTo(x: -512, duration: 2.5) trail2.run(.repeatForever(.sequence([move3, move4]))) // ... Setup continues ...
}

We then need to move the trail texture. We use SKAction.moveTo(x:duration:) to add the texture movement. Each trail needs two move actions. The first action has a duration of 0 as it moves the texture back to its starting position. This is basically the wrap around.

And the second move action animates the texture to move to the other side of the visible area. The duration value here sets the speed. We put both move actions into a sequence that we repeat forever.

In this example we use two trails stacked on top of each other, so we move the second trail with a different duration to get a different speed so it adds visual variation.

Potentially, we could also offset the move x value for one of the trails for even more variation.

And there we have it, a parent node (the crop node) that can be moved in the game scene while a texture moves independently inside it that wraps around. This setup gives us the same behavior as the first of the two UV bullet points.

Fade the Edge

To fade out the edge of the effect, we will do pretty much exactly as theorized above. We'll take a gradient map image and multiply it on top of the effect.

override func didMove(to view: SKView) { // ... Setup continued ... let opacity = SKSpriteNode(imageNamed: "trailTexture-gradient") opacity.blendMode = .multiplyAlpha opacity.zPosition = 2 // ... Setup continues ...
}

We prepare an object with the gradient texture and set its blendMode appropriately so we can fade out the edge of the effect. We will add the node to the effect hierarchy together
with the taper effect in the next step.

Another approach I've been considering is to use an SKShader that should be able to do the same thing, but which would be cleaner and more efficient.

Taper the Texture

It's time to deal with the tapering of the trail effect by using a SpriteKit implementation for the second bullet point from the list with UV behaviors we are trying to replicate.

The closest I've got is to use an SKEffectNode with warpGeometry.

override func didMove(to view: SKView) { // ... Setup continued ... // The effect node to warp. let effect = SKEffectNode() effect.addChild(crop) effect.addChild(opacity) // Create the tapered coordinates. let destinationPositions: [vector_float2] = [ vector_float2(0, 5), vector_float2(0.5, 0.9), vector_float2(1, -2.5), vector_float2(0, 0.5), vector_float2(0.5, 0.5), vector_float2(1, 0.5), vector_float2(0, -4), vector_float2(0.5, 0.1), vector_float2(1, 3.5) ] let warpGeometryGrid = SKWarpGeometryGrid(columns: 2, rows: 2) // Apply the tapered effect. effect.warpGeometry = warpGeometryGrid.replacingByDestinationPositions(positions: destinationPositions) // Control the quality of the distortion effect. effect.subdivisionLevels = 4 // ... Setup continues ...
}

We distort the trail by using SKWarpGeometryGrid. Basically, we just create a grid of coordinates where we can squeeze the right side of the node together, so when the trail moves over the effect node, it gets compressed on the right side while
it spreads out and widens on the left side.

This approach works fine if used isolated, but as with many SpriteKit things, it gets a tad more complicated when combining parts together.

Use the subdivisionLevels property of the effect node to get rid of distortion blockiness and get a much smoother and crisper look. Higher values do affect performance.

Do you remember this from before, when we created the huge background?

let bg = SKSpriteNode(color: .black, size: .init(width: 4000, height: 95))
bg.isHidden = true

Now is the time to talk about it. The huge, hidden, background is there to ensure that the warpGeometryGrid coordinates stay in put.

The warp does not respect the cropped area.

By having a larger than we need background, the warp will not move around. Otherwise, as the trails move over the node, it instead calculates the unit coordinates based on how
large it sees all nodes added together for each frame. The large background counters that behavior and helps it keep consistent.

It's not a pretty solution, but that's how far I've been able to take it with SpriteKit alone. Using a CIFilter might be worth exploring as an alternative option, a transform there might behave more consistent than using warpGeometry.

Talking about CIFilter, as we have an effect node created already, why not keep using it.

override func didMove(to view: SKView) { // ... Setup continued ... // Add an additional bloom on top of the effect. let bloomFilter = CIFilter(name: "CIBloom")! bloomFilter.setValue(5, forKey: "inputRadius") bloomFilter.setValue(1, forKey: "inputIntensity") effect.filter = bloomFilter // ... Setup continues ...
}

The effect node can take a CIFilter among its properties, so let's go ahead and use it to add a bloom filter to enhance the effect further.

Move the Full Trail Effect

With the entire effect layered up and organized in a hierarchy under the effect node, we can now simply move the trail wherever we need it to be my moving the effect node around.

override func didMove(to view: SKView) { // ... Setup continued ... // Move the entire projectile over the screen. effect.run(SKAction.moveTo(x: 700, duration: 3)) effect.addChild(crop)
}

And that's all there is to it. We start an animation where we want the projectile to go and then add it to the scene.

In this article I've out of convenience, just added the setup inside the didMove(to:) method. It would make more sense to have this functionality in a trailEffect()->SKNode method that would setup the trail and return its node to have it instantiated on demand.

The returned node can then get the appropriate movement action assigned and then added to the scene.

Performance wise using SKEffectNode and SKCropNode can be heavy. Keeping the dimensions down on the effect should have a positive effect on performance.

Next Steps

Even though the fact that this solution can not compete with what you can do with the modern game engines out there, and also that the solution uncovers a few flaws (aka bugs) with SpriteKit, it can still provide a lot of power and usefulness for SpriteKit projects.

A dynamic trail VFX can be the building block and foundation of many different effects. Continuing building on the trails in this example, I'll add a head to the trails using something like an animated flare. On top of that, a small particle emitter could be added as the final layer, emitted some sparkling texture to help bring the effect to life.
item.author: johansteen

item.title: 我用过的最好的编码键盘 - 重温 | The best keyboard I ever had for coding - Revisited
item.description:
几个月前，他们发布了一个新版本，这次使用的是 GL 开关而不是 Romer G 开关，所以我再次购买了它 - 再次使用 clicky 开关 - 以测试差异。

用法几乎相同，但据称这款新 GL 更耐用等等。

两个键盘都和第一天一样好用，所以我想我得等着看 Romer G 能用多久。

自 2020 年 2 月以来对其进行测试后，我可以确保带有 Clicky 开关的罗技 G915 / 罗技 G915 TKL 是最好的编码键盘。

他们还交换了键帽上的材料，我不确定我是否喜欢。

自从我在 2022 年 1 月 25 日购买第二个 G915 TKL 以来，已经两年多了。

我在上一篇文章中没有提到的一件事是它有两种可能的联系。 通过加密狗和蓝牙。
这对我的日常使用特别有帮助，因为我使用蓝牙将它连接到公司的笔记本电脑上工作，然后我只需按一下按钮就可以切换到自己的电脑。

我可以确认低调的东西是必须的，我的手腕不再疼痛！

这次我也拿到了一张我现在的桌面的照片😁

你用什么键盘？ 您是否注意过工作场所的人体工程学和健康状况？

另外，如果您需要有关外围设备或硬件的帮助，请随时问我！



原文:

Previous post

Some months ago they released a new version, this time with GL switches instead Romer G ones, so I bought it -again with clicky switches- to test the differences.

The usage is almost the same but this new GL are allegedly more durable and so on.

Both keyboards work as fine as the first day so I guess I'll have to wait to see how much the Romer G ones lasts.

After testing it since February 2020 I can ensure that Logitech G915 / Logitech G915 TKL with Clicky switches is the best keyboard for coding.

They also swapped the material on the key caps which is something I'm not sure if I like or not.

It's been more than two years since I bought the first G915 TKL while I bought the second one in January 25th 2022.

One thing I didn't mention in the last post is that it has two possible connections. Through the dongle and through Bluetooth.
This is specially helpful in my daily usage as I connect it using Bluetooth to the company's laptop to work and then I can switch to my own computer just by hitting a button.

I can confirm that the low profile thingy is a must, no more pain in my wrists!

This time I also got a photo of my current desktop 😁

TL;DR
After more than 2 years I still think that this is the best keyboard I ever had for coding.


What keyboard do you use? Have you watched for ergonomics and health at your workspace?

Also if you need help with peripherals or hardware ask me anything!
item.author: joelbonetr

item.title: 使用 Asgardeo 对 Express 应用程序进行 OIDC 身份验证 | OIDC Authentication for Express Apps with Asgardeo
item.description:
在我之前的文章中，我讨论了如何使用Asgardeo Node SDK为您的 Express 应用程序添加身份验证。 正如我之前提到的，在 Express 应用程序中使用 Node SDK 的过程并不简单，因为 Node SDK 是一个通用的 SDK，它兼容任何 Node JS 平台。 在本文中，我将向您展示如何使用 Asgardeo Express SDK 轻松地为您的 Express 应用程序添加身份验证。

在此处尝试官方示例应用程序。

先决条件

  • 一个 Asgardeo 组织。 基于标准的应用程序 - 在 Asgardeo 控制台中创建组织后，您可以从仪表板创建基于标准的应用程序。 项目初始化
    创建一个新目录（一个文件夹）并使用您的代码编辑器打开该目录。 打开终端并输入yarn init --y它将初始化一个新项目并创建一个package.json文件。

    下一步是安装依赖项。

     yarn add @asgardeo/auth-express cookie-parser express

    然后将 Nodemon 添加为开发依赖项以热重新加载您的更改。

    安装 Nodemon 后，修改package.json并在脚本部分添加"serve": "nodemon server.js" ，以便我们可以使用 Nodemon 运行服务器。

    创建 Express 应用程序
    创建一个新文件并将其命名为server.js 。
    然后在server.js文件中创建一个简单的快速服务器。 您可以使用下面的代码。

     const express = require ( ' express ' ); const cookieParser = require ( ' cookie-parser ' ); //Define the Port const PORT = 5000 ; //Initialize Express App const app = express (); app . use ( cookieParser ()); app . get ( " / " , ( req , res ) => { res . send ( " Hello World " ); }); //Start the app and listen on the PORT app . listen ( PORT , () => { console . log ( `Server Started at PORT ${ PORT } ` ); });

    创建服务器后，使用以下命令运行它，

    现在，如果您导航到http://localhost:5000 ，您应该会看到来自服务器的“Hello World”响应。

    为 Asgardeo 身份验证添加配置
    在目录中创建一个新文件并将其命名为config.js 。 在此文件中，您需要指定 Asgardeo Auth 的配置。

     const config = { clientID : " YOUR_CLIENT_ID " , clientSecret : " YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET " , baseUrl : " YOUR_BASE_URL " , appURL : " http://localhost:5000 " , scope : [ " openid " , " profile " ] }; module . exports = config ;

    用您从Asgardeo Console获得的值替换clientID 、 clientSecret和baseUrl 。 由于我们在http://localhost:5000中运行我们的服务器，因此我们必须将其添加为 appURL。

    笔记：
    确保将localhost:5000添加为允许的来源，并将localhost:5000/login添加为控制台中的授权重定向 URL。 稍后我们将讨论为什么要专门授权localhost:5000/login端点。

    使用 AsgardeoAuth 中间件
    打开server.js文件并从 SDK 导入中间件。

     const { AsgardeoExpressClient } = require ( " @asgardeo/auth-express " );

    然后使用我们之前创建的配置实例化 Asgardeo Express 客户端。

     AsgardeoExpressClient . getInstance ( config );

    现在您必须定义启动中间件所需的三个回调函数。

    1. 定义回调函数后，将中间件与我们创建的 Express 应用程序一起使用。

       app . use ( AsgardeoExpressClient . asgardeoExpressAuth ( onSignIn , onSignOut , onError ) );

      这就是您使用 Express SDK 实现身份验证所需要做的全部工作。 此时，您只需导航到http://localhost:5000/login并进行身份验证。 这让我解释了为什么我们添加localhost:5000/login作为授权重定向 URL。 因此，即使您不必为这两个端点实现逻辑，您仍然需要在控制台中授权端点才能正常运行。

      如果您不喜欢预先配置的登录和注销路径，您可以随时覆盖配置文件中的默认值。 您所要做的就是向配置对象添加另一个属性，如下所示。

       const config = { ... config , loginPath : " /customLogin " , logoutPath : " /customLogout " }

      如果您使用自定义登录和注销路径，请不要忘记在控制台中更改授权重定向 URL。

      实施受保护的路由
      在某些用例中，您可能希望通过登录来保护特定路由。 为此，Asgardeo Express SDK 为您提供了一个简单的中间件。 您所要做的就是将isAuthenticated中间件附加到您要保护的路由上。

       app . get ( " /protected " , isAuthenticated , async ( req , res ) => { res . status ( 200 ). send ({ message : " Hello from protected route! " }); });

      您可以在Express SDK GitHub存储库中查看它们。 与往常一样，如果您有任何问题，请随时与我联系，也欢迎您为 SDK 做出贡献。 谢谢阅读！



原文:

In my previous article, I discussed how we can use Asgardeo Node SDK to add authentication to your Express app. As I mentioned before, the process of using the Node SDK in an Express app is not straightforward because the Node SDK is a generic SDK which is compatible across any Node JS platform. In this article, I will show you how you can use the Asgardeo Express SDK to add authentication to your Express app easily.

TLDR;
Try the official sample app here.

Prerequisites

  • An Asgardeo organization. (You can create an organization upon registration at https://asgardeo.io/signup).
  • A Standard Based Application - Once you create an organization in the Asgardeo Console, you can create a Standard Based Application from the dashboard. (More info)

Projet Initialization
Create a new directory (a folder) and open the directory using your code editor. Open a terminal and type yarn init --y and it will initialize a new project and create a package.json file.

The next step is to install the dependancies.

yarn add @asgardeo/auth-express cookie-parser express

Then add Nodemon as a dev dependancy to hot reload your changes.

Once you install Nodemon, modify the package.json and add "serve": "nodemon server.js" in the scripts section so we can use Nodemon to run the server.

Create an Express app
Create a new file and name it as server.js.
Then create a simple express server inside your server.js file. You can use the code below.

const express = require('express');
const cookieParser = require('cookie-parser'); //Define the Port
const PORT = 5000; //Initialize Express App
const app = express();
app.use(cookieParser()); app.get("/", (req, res) => { res.send("Hello World");
}); //Start the app and listen on the PORT
app.listen(PORT, () => { console.log(`Server Started at PORT ${ PORT }`); });

After creating the server, run it using,

Now if you navigate to http://localhost:5000, you should see a "Hello World" response from the server.

Add configuration for Asgardeo Authentication
Create a new file in the directory and name it as config.js. Inside this file, you need to specify the configuration for the Asgardeo Auth.

const config = { clientID: "YOUR_CLIENT_ID", clientSecret: "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET", baseUrl: "YOUR_BASE_URL", appURL: "http://localhost:5000", scope: ["openid", "profile"]
};
module.exports = config;

Replace the clientID, clientSecret and baseUrl from the values you get from Asgardeo Console. Since we run our server in http://localhost:5000, we have to add it as the appURL.

Note:
Make sure you add localhost:5000 as an allowed origin and add localhost:5000/login as an authorized redirect URL in the console. We will discuss why should we authorize localhost:5000/login endpoint specifically later on.

Use the AsgardeoAuth middleware
Open the server.js file and import the middleware from the SDK.

const { AsgardeoExpressClient } = require("@asgardeo/auth-express");

Then instantiate the Asgardeo Express client with the config we created earlier.

AsgardeoExpressClient.getInstance(config);

Now you have to define three callback functions which are needed to initiate the middleware.

  1. onSignIn - Handle a successful user sign in.
  2. onSignOut - Handle a successful user sign out.
  3. onError - Handle errors that might occur in the authentication process. 
const onSignIn = (res, response) => { if (response) { res.status(200).send(response); }else{ res.status(500).send("Something went wrong"); }
}; const onSignOut = (res) => { res.status(200).send("Sign out successful");
}; const onError = (res, error) => { if(error){ res.status(400).send(error); }else{ res.status(500).send("Something went wrong"); }
};

After defining the callback functions, use the middleware with the Express app we created.

app.use( AsgardeoExpressClient.asgardeoExpressAuth(onSignIn, onSignOut, onError)
);

That is all you have to do with the Express SDK to implement authentication. At this point, you can just navigate to http://localhost:5000/login and authenticate yourself. That brings me to explain why we added localhost:5000/login as an authorized redirect URL. The SDK itself is pre-configured to set the login endpoint to /login and to set the logout endpoint to /logout. So even though you didn't have to implement the logic for those 2 endpoints, you still need to authorize the endpoints in the console in order to function properly.

In case you don't like the pre-configured login and logout paths, you can always override the defaults in the config file. All you have to do is add another property to the config object as follows.

const config = { ...config, loginPath : "/customLogin", logoutPath: "/customLogout"
}

Don't forget to change the authorized redirect URL in the console if you're using a custom login and logout paths.

Implementing Protected Routes
In some use cases, you may want to protect a specific route with a login. To do this, Asgardeo Express SDK provides you with a simple middleware. All you have to do is, attach the isAuthenticated middleware to the route you want to protect.

app.get("/protected", isAuthenticated, async (req, res) => { res.status(200).send({ message: "Hello from protected route!" });
});

The Asgardeo Express SDK has many features in addition to the basic login functions I described above. You can check them out at Express SDK GitHub repo. As always, feel free to reach me if you have any questions and you are welcome to make contributions to the SDK as well. Thanks for reading!

item.title: 从 UI/UX 的角度来看，理想的分页是什么？ | What's The Ideal Pagination From A UI/UX Perspective?
item.description:
当你用谷歌搜索某些东西时，可能会有数百万条结果。 但他们不会一次显示所有这些。

分页是 Web 开发人员用来仅显示数据集中部分结果的术语

您只显示了一些结果，然后会有一个“转到下一页”选项或类似的选项。

分页有几种变体。 我最近试图从 UI/UX 角度回答，理想的分页格式是什么？ 什么是最实用的（我敢说，“最好的”）？

分页优势

为什么要使用分页？ 我想到了两个主要好处：

  • 用户更容易消化小块信息
  • 它有助于页面加载。 加载 10 个结果比 1000 个结果快得多（想想网站显示需要几秒钟）

分页示例

这在 Web 开发人员中非常普遍，以至于 UI Web 库具有预先构建的设计模板。 例如，这是默认的 Bootstrap 设计：

谷歌有一个独特的：

我记得几年前，我做了这样的事情：

在进行此任务之前，这是我以前的标准：

如您所见，它具有：

  • 第一个/最后一个图标
  • 上一个/下一个图标
  • 您所在的页面
  • 总页数

这很简单（而且简单很好！）但我当然认为：

有没有更好的方法？

假设：搜索与浏览

你不是在重塑谷歌。 你不会有一百万个结果。

您的应用程序应该具有“搜索”功能，允许用户缩小结果范围。 可能会有 100 个甚至 1,000 个结果。 在搜索之后，用户可以对结果进行排序。 总的来说，这将是一个相当小的数据集。

如果用户不确定他们在寻找什么，那么他们正在浏览整个数据集。

对于我的用例，我在用户浏览整个数据集时使用分页。

设计理想分页的初始逻辑

使用谷歌作为灵感和大量思考，这是我的第一关：


注意事项：

  • 第一个/最后一个按钮（如果适用）
  • 上一个/下一个按钮（如果适用）
  • 数字指标

不需要第一个/最后一个按钮

执行搜索时，您希望首先看到最相关的结果。 如果结果不在页面上，您将转到下一页。 甚至可能是在那之后的那个。

一旦你在搜索结果的“中间”，你很可能不会想要回到第一页。 同样，您可能不需要跳到最后一页。 如果您有兴趣，可以按降序对结果进行排序。 因此，无需显示“第一个/最后一个”选项。

前面一个后面一个

假设结果不在第一页上，并且您实际上正在使用分页，您将需要上一个/下一个按钮。

仅在适用时显示。 例如，在查看结果的第一页时，不要显示“上一页”按钮。 或者至少，让它显示但禁用（不可点击）。

当我与 UI/UX 专家的朋友York Myers交谈时，有两条感兴趣的评论：

  • 考虑使用术语“返回”而不是“上一个”。 更少的字母将更符合“下一个”一词。
  • 如果可能，请使用图标（例如箭头）。 但请注意您的用户：根据人口统计，明确拼写“下一步”可能比使用箭头更清楚。

页码

几个明显的事情：

  • 显示用户所在的当前（“活动”）页面
  • 除了上一个（后退）/下一个按钮外，还有一个带有页码的可点击按钮。 如果您在第 1 页，则有一个“第 2 页”按钮和一个“下一步”按钮。

不那么明显：

  • 您需要多少个数字按钮？ 经过一番思考和讨论，我认为它应该不超过两个数字，在用户当前页面之前和/或之后。 这在上图中得到了证明。

我认为前面的建议中的逻辑是理想的，但是提供了另一个建议。 看看下面的组件：

它有：

  • 选择每页行的选项（此处为预选 10 行的下拉菜单）
  • 显示当前结果集（此处为 1 到 10）
  • 结果总数（此处为 14）
  • 上一个/下一个作为图标

这简化了事情，但我个人不想让用户选择要显示的结果数量。

显示更少的选项>>用户必须少思考

我意识到这就是 GMail 所做的。 另一个理想的分页设计模式变成：

我是一名拥有 20 多年经验的网络开发人员。 虽然我不是 UI/UX 专家，但这些年来我已经学会了一两件事，并且我确实为这篇文章咨询过一件事。 欢迎您的意见/意见！

这篇文章最初发表在 Medium



原文:

When you Google something, there are probably millions of results. But they don't display all of them at once.

Pagination is the term web developers use to show only some of the results within a dataset

You're showing only some results, then there will be an option for "go to the next page," or some equivalent.

There are several variations of pagination. I recently sought out to answer, from a UI/UX view, what is the ideal pagination format? What's the most practical (dare I say, "best")?

Pagination Benefits

Why use pagination? Two major benefits come to mind:

  • It's easier for users to digest small chunks of information
  • It helps with the page load. Loading 10 results is much faster than 1,000 results (think seconds it takes a website to display)

Pagination Examples

This is so common amongst web developers that UI web libraries have pre-built design templates. For example, here's the default Bootstrap design:

Google has a unique one:

I remember several years ago, I did something like this:

Prior to going on this quest, this was my previous standard:

As you can see it has:

  • First/Last icons
  • Previous/Next icons
  • Page you’re on
  • Total number of pages

That was pretty simple (and simple is good!) but of course I thought:

Was there a better way?

Assumption: Search vs Browse

You're not remaking Google. You won't have a million results.

Your app should have a "search" capability that will allow a user to narrow down the results. Maybe it’ll be 100 or even 1,000 results. After a search, the user may sort the results. Overall it’ll be a rather small dataset.

If the user isn’t sure what they're looking for, then they are browsing the entire dataset.

For my use case, I'm using pagination for when a user is browsing an entire dataset.

Initial logic in designing the ideal pagination

Using Google as an inspiration and a lot of thought, this was my first pass:


Considerations:

  • First/Last buttons (if applicable)
  • Previous/Next buttons (if applicable)
  • Numeric indicators

No Need For First/Last Buttons

When you perform a search, you expect to see the most relevant results first. If the results aren't on the page, you'll go to the next page. And maybe even the one after that.

Once you're in the "middle" of the search results, you most likely won't have a desire to go back to the first page. Similarly, you probably won't have a need to skip to the last page; if you were that interested, you’d sort the results descending. As such, no need to show "First/Last" options.

Previous/Next

Assuming the results aren't on the first page, and you're actually utilizing pagination, you'll need Previous/Next buttons.

Only show if applicable. For example, when viewing the first page of results, don’t show a "Previous" button. Or at minimum, have it displayed but disabled (unclickable).

Two comments of interest when I was talking to York Myers, a friend who's a UI/UX expert:

  • Consider using the term "Back" instead of "Previous". Fewer letters will be more consistent with the term "Next".
  • Use icons (ex. arrow) if possible. Be mindful of your users though: depending on the demographic, it may be more clear to explicitly spell-out "Next" rather than have an arrow.

Page Numbers

A couple of obvious things:

  • Show the current ("active") page that the user is on
  • In addition to Previous (Back) / Next buttons, have a clickable button that has the page number on it. If you’re on Page 1, then have a button for "Page 2" and a button for "Next".

Not as obvious:

  • How many numeric buttons do you need? After some thought and discussion, I think it should be no more than two numbers, before and/or after the current page the user is on. This is demonstrated in the image above.

(2) Final Recommendations

I think the logic in the preceding recommendation is ideal, however there was another suggestion offered. Take a look at the component below:

It has:

  • Option to select rows per page (here, a dropdown with 10 pre-selected)
  • Displaying the current set of results (here, 1 to 10)
  • Total number of results (here, 14)
  • Previous / Next as icons

That simplifies things, but personally I don't want to give the user an option to select the number of results to show.

Showing less options >> User has to think less

I realized that this is what GMail does. Another ideal pagination design pattern then becomes:

I'm a web developer with 20+ years experience. While I’m not a UI/UX expert, I’ve picked up a thing or two over the years and I did consult one for this article. Your comments/opinions are welcome!

This article was originally posted on Medium
item.author: stevesohcot

item.title: 从 Angular 8 升级到 13 | Upgrading from Angular 8 to 13
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我将报告我在将 Angular 应用程序 v8 迁移到 v13 过程中学到的知识。

此外，我将向您展示您在此过程中可能遇到的最常见错误以及它们各自的解决方案，并在适当的时候指出来源。

我花了一年半的时间在一个部门工作，该部门处理许多应用程序和一套在 Angular 8 中构建的组件。

在去年的最后 2 个月里，我们与前端工作组开会，一直在讨论 Angular 应用程序迁移的主题。
被认为是一个关键问题，尤其是当我们想起最近案例的风险时，例如 Java 中的log4j 漏洞和影响 AngularJS 中项目的faker库的不连续性，官方不支持。

然后是处理技术债务的机会。 当我们完成这项工作时，Angular 版本是 13，因为Google 最多只支持 2 个以前的版本，所以不再支持 Angular 2 到 10。

初步指南

我们借鉴了将 Angular 8 中的组件项目迁移到 13 的经验。这些是可能的迁移方法：

优点是它会自动转换代码，但是经过这么多版本的过程最终成本更高，因此在我们有5 个版本的差距的情况下不推荐使用。 这可能是未来迁移的最合适的选择。

这种方法已被用于我们的组件项目的迁移，也是我们在这种情况下推荐的方法。

迁移路线图

复制字体时，建议保留生成的新package.json以包含并按需解决各种依赖项，以便在流程结束时仅包含真正需要的字体，从而也符合最佳安全标准。 更新子项目的package.json的角度版本（如果有的话）。

更新子项目的 tsconfig.lib.json 文件（如果有）以与当前的 tsconfig.json 文件对齐

"target": "es2017", "module": "es2020", "lib": [ "es2020", "dom"

常见配置错误及解决方法

  • An unhandled exception occurred: The target entry-point "@novo-projeto" has missing dependencies: primeng/toast, primeng/button

将primeng添加到package.json中。 这将是最常见的错误，因为它会发生在尚未包含的所有各种依赖项上，例如： bootstrap 、 ng-select等。 尽量在项目中始终定义与当前 Angular 版本对应的依赖版本。

可以通过这种方式解决的其他情况是类型错误： Script file ____ does not exist. 或未An unhandled occurred exception: ENOENT: no such file or directory, lstat ______

  • "ng build" for library fails with "does not support the 'build' target

从旧项目复制angular.json文件

  • npm WARN @angular-devkit/build-ng-packagr@0.1002.0 requires a peer of ng-packagr@^10.0.0 but none is installed. You must install peer dependencies yourself.

从package.json中删除build-ng-packagr并修改angular.json中的任务，以便@angular-devkit/build-ng-packagr:build替换@angular-devkit/build-angular:ng-包装器

  • Directory import '...\node_modules\@angular\compiler-cli\ngcc' is not supported resolving ES modules imported from ...\node_modules\ng-packagr\lib\utils\ng-compiler-cli.js. Did you mean to import @angular/compiler-cli/ngcc/index.js?

通过将package.json文件中ng-packagr的版本修改为与项目中的 Angular 版本对应的版本来解决。

  • Cannot resolve type entity i5.Scrolling Module to symbol* ou *ERROR: The target entry-point "primeng/dropdown" has missing dependencies: @angular/cdk/scrolling

它通过将 @angular/cdk 添加到package.json来解决。

  • ModuleError: Module Error (from ./node_modules/postcss-loader/dist/cjs.js) ... Can't resolve 'OpenSans-Regular.eot' in '...\dist\seus-components\css'

安装chart和ng2-charts ，它是第一个的包装器，运行：

涉及代码更改的常见错误及解决方案

  • 模块错误类型： '"primeng"' has no exported member 'ConfirmDialogModule'

在早期版本的primeng导入中是这样的：

import { Checkbox, MessageService, ConfirmDialogModule } from 'primeng/primeng';

参考现在更加具体，因此需要更正：

import { Checkbox } from 'primeng/checkbox'; import { MessageService } from 'primeng/api';

从'primeng/confirmdialog'导入{ConfirmDialogModule}；

  • error TS1323: Dynamic imports are only supported when the '--module' flag is set to 'es2020'

使用observables时，可能会出现错误Error TS2554: Expected 1 arguments, but got 0

它可以通过传递一个假值来解决：

this.subject.next("");

  • Error: Module not found: Resolving to directories is not possible with the exports field (request was ./)

它是通过删除导入末尾的“/”或用单引号替换双引号来解决的。 例子：

之前： import { MsgCenterModule } from '@seu-componente/etc-client/';
之后： import { MsgCenterModule } from '@seu-componente/etc-client';
之前： import { ABCEnum, XYZEnum } from "../../tabela.constants";
之后： import { ABCEnum, XYZEnum } from '../../tabela.constants';

  • 类型Module not found: Error: Can't solve 'dayjs' / Error: Module not found: Error: Can't solve 'inputmask'

安装这个库是因为即使它不直接在应用程序中使用，但在某些情况下是必要的，因为它被使用的组件引用：

npm install dayjs --save

npm install inputmask --save

  • Type error: Object is possibly 'null'. TS2531 for window.document

因为它是以前已经工作的迁移代码，只需添加！ 代码： document.getElementById('content')!. innerHTML = ''; document.getElementById('content')!. innerHTML = '';

  • error NG8002: Can't bind to 'minWidth' since it isn't a known property of 'p-dialog'.

从 primeng 9 开始，必须在 style 中设置一些属性。 因此，在 html 中，将[width]="600" [minWidth]="200"更改为[style]="{width: '600px', minWidth: '200px'}" 。 查看括号或发生 CSS 样式的 setar 错误

  • error TS7006: Parameter 'perfis' implicitly has an 'any' type

有时需要指出的解决方案是在tsconfig.json中将“noImplicitAny”设置为false ，但不推荐这样做。

  • error TS2564: Property 'titulo' has no initializer and is not definitely assigned in the constructor

从 2.7 版开始，**typescript **编译器要求初始化属性/变量。 解决方法有多种，但我们选择添加“！” 在变量的定义中：

public container!: ViewContainerRef;

我们这样做是因为我们保证它已被初始化，因为它在 Angular 8 的前一个项目中工作，因为前一个代码在运行时运行时没有错误。

注意：在tsconfig.json中， compilerOptions可以定义为 "strictPropertyInitialization": false ，以忽略这些错误并加快这一阶段的迁移，但建议稍后再回到 true 。

  • error TS2322: Type 'string | null' is not assignable to type 'string | undefined'.

将接口属性设置为可选时很常见，用“！”解决就像之前的错误一样。

  • error TS2322: Type 'Boolean' is not assignable to type 'boolean' ... 'boolean' is a primitive, but 'Boolean' is a wrapper object. Prefer using 'boolean' when possible

较新版本的打字稿更加严格。 在这种情况下，您需要修改代码以使用布尔原始类型。

  • error NG3001: Unsupported private class MsgCenterComponent. This class is visible to consumers via SistemaClientModule -> MsgCenterComponent, but is not exported from the top-level library entrypoint.

在public_api.ts中添加与相关类对应的行，在本例中：

export * from './lib/msg-center/msg-center.component';

  • error TS2314: Generic type 'ModuleWithProviders<T>' requires 1 type argument(s)

更改代码如下，在此示例中：

static forRoot(): ModuleWithProviders<XYZClientModule>

  • error TS7030: Not all code paths return a value.

确保在相关代码中返回一个值。 它通常出现在更广泛的if子句中。

  • error TS2339: Property 'throw' does not exist on type 'typeof Observable'

安装依赖： npm i --save-dev @types/uuid

并将代码中的调用修改为：

import * as uuid from 'uuid';

  • error TS7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expression of type 'string' can't be used to index type '{}'. No index signature with a parameter of type 'string' was found on type '{}'

您需要通过修改代码来定义对象具有的索引类型：

pagesStatus: {[index: string]:any} = {}

  • error TS2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'

它可以出现在html 标签属性中。 例如，哪里有：

<p-fileUpload mode="advanced" name="demo[]" maxFileSize=1000000 multiple=true chooseLabel="Buscar arquivo" (onUpload)="onUpload($event)"></p-fileUpload>

放置括号：

<p-fileUpload mode="advanced" name="demo[]" [maxFileSize]="1000000" [multiple]="true" chooseLabel="Buscar arquivo" (onUpload)="onUpload($event)"></p-fileUpload>

迁移后

  • 从p-dialog中删除[responsive]属性。 在新版本中，此类组件是完全响应的，因此该属性已停用。



原文:

In this post I will report what I learned in the migration of Angular applications v8 to v13.

In addition, I will show you the most common errors that you may encounter in the process as well as their respective solutions, indicating the sources when appropriate.

I spent 1 year and a half allocated in a division that worked with many applications and a suite of components built in Angular 8.

In the last 2 months of the last year we had meetings with the frontend working group where the subject of angular application migration has always been talked.
Considered a critical issue, especially when we remembered the risk of recent cases such as the log4j vulnerability in Java and the discontinuity of the faker library that affected the projects in AngularJS, officially unsupported.

Then came an opportunity to work with technical debt. When we did this work the Angular version was 13 and because Google only supports up to 2 previous versions, Angular 2 through 10 were no longer supported.

Initial guidelines

We take the experience we had in migrating the component project in Angular 8 to 13. These are the possible migration approaches:

1) Run a ng update with each version because it is not possible through it to move from version 8 straight to 13, so you need to run this command to migrate from 8 to 9, from 9 to 10, and so on. The advantage is that it converts the code automatically, but the process of going through so many versions ends up being more costly and therefore not recommended in our case where there was a gap of 5 versions. It may be the most appropriate option for future migrations.

2) Create a new project in Angular 13 and copy the fonts from the old project into Angular 8. Then solve the problems as they arise. This approach has been adopted for the migration of our component project and is what we recommend in this situation.

Migration roadmap

1) Install latest version of Angular

npm install -g @angular/cli

2) Create new project

ng new novo-projeto

3) Copy fonts from old project

When copying the fonts it is recommended to keep the new package.json that was generated to include and resolve the various dependencies on demand so that at the end of the process only the ones really needed are included, thus also meeting the best safety standards. Update angular versions of the package.json of the subprojects (if any) as well.

4) Modify tsconfig.lib.json settings

Update the tsconfig.lib.json files of the subprojects (if any) to align with the current tsconfig.json files

"target": "es2017", "module": "es2020", "lib": [ "es2020", "dom"

]

Common configuration errors and solutions

  • An unhandled exception occurred: The target entry-point "@novo-projeto" has missing dependencies: primeng/toast, primeng/button

Add the primeng to the package.json. This will be the most common error because it will occur for all various dependencies that have not yet been included, such as: bootstrap, ng-select, among others. Try to always define the version of the dependency corresponding to the current version of Angular in the project.

Other situations that can be solved in this way are errors of type:Script file ____ does not exist. or An unhandled occurred exception: ENOENT: no such file or directory, lstat ______

  • "ng build" for library fails with "does not support the 'build' target

Copy angular.json file from old project

  • npm WARN @angular-devkit/build-ng-packagr@0.1002.0 requires a peer of ng-packagr@^10.0.0 but none is installed. You must install peer dependencies yourself.

The build-ng-packagr has been discontinued. Remove the build-ng-packagr from package.json and modify the tasks in angular.json, so that where there is @angular-devkit/build-ng-packagr:build replace for @angular-devkit/build-angular:ng-packagr

  • Directory import '...\node_modules\@angular\compiler-cli\ngcc' is not supported resolving ES modules imported from ...\node_modules\ng-packagr\lib\utils\ng-compiler-cli.js. Did you mean to import @angular/compiler-cli/ngcc/index.js?

Resolves by modifying the version of ng-packagr in the package.json file, to one corresponding to the Angular version in the project.

  • Cannot resolve type entity i5.Scrolling Module to symbol* ou *ERROR: The target entry-point "primeng/dropdown" has missing dependencies: @angular/cdk/scrolling

It is solved by adding the @angular/cdk to package.json.

  • ModuleError: Module Error (from ./node_modules/postcss-loader/dist/cjs.js) ... Can't resolve 'OpenSans-Regular.eot' in '...\dist\seus-components\css'

Install the postcss and postcss-cli:

npm install postcss postcss-cli

  • Error: Module not found: Error: Can't resolve 'chart.js/auto'

Install chart and ng2-charts, which is a wrapper of the first, running:

npm install --save ng2-charts and npm install --save chart.js

Common errors involving code change and solutions

  • Module errors of type: '"primeng"' has no exported member 'ConfirmDialogModule'

In earlier versions of primeng imports were like this:

import { Checkbox, MessageService, ConfirmDialogModule } from 'primeng/primeng';

References are now more specific, so they need to be corrected:

import { Checkbox } from 'primeng/checkbox'; import { MessageService } from 'primeng/api';

import { ConfirmDialogModule } from 'primeng/confirmdialog';

  • error TS1323: Dynamic imports are only supported when the '--module' flag is set to 'es2020'

In order to support dynamic imports, add the line to tsconfig.json:

"module": "esnext",

  • When working with observables, error Error TS2554: Expected 1 arguments, but got 0 may arise

Occurs after migration to rxjs 7. It can be solved by passing a fake value:

this.subject.next("");

  • Error: Module not found: Resolving to directories is not possible with the exports field (request was ./)

It usually occurs after migration to Angular 12. It is solved by removing the "/" at the end of imports or replacement of double quotes by single. Example:

Before: import { MsgCenterModule } from '@seu-componente/etc-client/';
After: import { MsgCenterModule } from '@seu-componente/etc-client';
Before: import { ABCEnum, XYZEnum } from "../../tabela.constants";
After: import { ABCEnum, XYZEnum } from '../../tabela.constants';

  • Error of the type: Module not found: Error: Can't solve 'dayjs' / Error: Module not found: Error: Can't solve 'inputmask'

Installing this library because even though it is not directly used in the application, it is necessary in some cases because it is referenced by the component used:

npm install dayjs --save

npm install inputmask --save

  • Type error: Object is possibly 'null'. TS2531 for window.document

The typescript compiler is indicating that window.document.getElementById('content') may return NULL. Because it is a migrated code that already worked previously just add the ! to code: document.getElementById('content')!. innerHTML = '';

  • error NG8002: Can't bind to 'minWidth' since it isn't a known property of 'p-dialog'.

From primeng 9 some properties must be set in style. Therefore, in html, where it has [width]="600" [minWidth]="200" change to [style]="{width: '600px', minWidth: '200px'}". Look at the brackets or a CSS-style setar error occurs

  • error TS7006: Parameter 'perfis' implicitly has an 'any' type

This is because variables now need to have some type defined, so it is necessary at all points in the code to set them to any or for a more specific type. A sometimes pointed solution is to set "noImplicitAny" to false in the tsconfig.json, but it is not recommended.

  • error TS2564: Property 'titulo' has no initializer and is not definitely assigned in the constructor

Starting with version 2.7 the **typescript **compiler requires that the property/variable be initialized. To solve there are several approaches but we chose to add the "!" in the definition of the variable:

public container!: ViewContainerRef;

We did this because we have assurances that this is initialized because it worked in the previous project in Angular 8, since the previous code ran without errors at runtime.

Note: In tsconfig.json, compilerOptions can be defined as "strictPropertyInitialization": false, to ignore these errors and speed up this phase of migration, but it is recommended to go back to true later.

  • error TS2322: Type 'string | null' is not assignable to type 'string | undefined'.

Very common when setting an interface property as optional, solved with "!" just like the previous error.

  • error TS2322: Type 'Boolean' is not assignable to type 'boolean' ... 'boolean' is a primitive, but 'Boolean' is a wrapper object. Prefer using 'boolean' when possible

Newer versions of typescript are more rigid. In this case you will need to modify the code to use the boolean primitive type.

  • error NG3001: Unsupported private class MsgCenterComponent. This class is visible to consumers via SistemaClientModule -> MsgCenterComponent, but is not exported from the top-level library entrypoint.

It usually happens from version 9 of Angular. Add to the public_api.ts the line corresponding to the class in question, in this example:

export * from './lib/msg-center/msg-center.component';

  • error TS2314: Generic type 'ModuleWithProviders<T>' requires 1 type argument(s)

Change the code as follows, in this example:

static forRoot(): ModuleWithProviders<XYZClientModule>

  • error TS7030: Not all code paths return a value.

Ensure that a value is returned in the code in question. It usually occurs in more extensive if clauses.

  • error TS2339: Property 'throw' does not exist on type 'typeof Observable'

Change the call to:

return throwError(() => response);

  • error TS1192: Module '".../node_modules/@types/uuid/index"' has no default export.

Install dependency: npm i --save-dev @types/uuid

And modify the call in the code to:

import * as uuid from 'uuid';

  • error TS7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expression of type 'string' can't be used to index type '{}'. No index signature with a parameter of type 'string' was found on type '{}'

You need to define the type of index that the object has by modifying the code to:

pagesStatus: {[index: string]:any} = {}

  • error TS2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'number'

It can occur in html tag attributes. For example, where there were:

<p-fileUpload mode="advanced" name="demo[]" maxFileSize=1000000 multiple=true chooseLabel="Buscar arquivo" (onUpload)="onUpload($event)"></p-fileUpload>

Place the brackets:

<p-fileUpload mode="advanced" name="demo[]" [maxFileSize]="1000000" [multiple]="true" chooseLabel="Buscar arquivo" (onUpload)="onUpload($event)"></p-fileUpload>

After migration

  • Code entryComponents Removal: From Angular 13 on, the use of entryComponents is no longer required

  • Remove [responsive] attributes from p-dialog. In the new version such components are fully responsive and so the attribute has been discontinued.
item.author: windmateus

item.title: [已解决] 找不到 CMAKE CXX 编译器 | [Solved] No CMAKE CXX COMPILER could be found
item.description:
尝试运行cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RelWithDebInfo时遇到以下错误：

 -- The CXX compiler identification is unknown CMake Error at CMakeLists.txt:3 (project): No CMAKE CXX COMPILER could be found. Tell CMake where to find the compiler by setting either the environment variable "CXX" or the CMake cache entry CMAKE CXX COMPILER to the full path to the compiler, or to the compiler name if it is in the PATH.

解决方案：

此错误通常是由未安装build-essential软件包引起的。 如果您使用的是 Ubuntu，请运行以下命令来安装此软件包：

 sudo apt update sudo apt install build-essential

安装此软件包后，再次尝试运行cmake命令，此错误应该得到解决。

结论

感谢您阅读这篇博文！

如果您有任何问题或疑虑，请随时在此帖子中发表评论，我会在找到时间时回复您。

如果您觉得这篇文章有帮助，请分享并确保在Twitter和GitHub 上关注我，在LinkedIn上与我联系并订阅我的YouTube 频道。



原文:

Running into the following error when trying to run cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RelWithDebInfo:

-- The CXX compiler identification is unknown CMake Error at CMakeLists.txt:3 (project): No CMAKE CXX COMPILER could be found.
Tell CMake where to find the compiler by setting either the environment variable "CXX" or the CMake cache entry CMAKE CXX COMPILER to the full path to the compiler, or to the compiler name if it is in the PATH.

Solution:

This error is usually caused by the build-essential package not being installed. If you're on Ubuntu run the following commands to install this package:

sudo apt update
sudo apt install build-essential

After installing this package, try running the cmake command again and this error should be resolved.

Conclusion

Thanks for reading this blog post!

If you have any questions or concerns please feel free to post a comment in this post and I will get back to you when I find the time.

If you found this article helpful please share it and make sure to follow me on Twitter and GitHub, connect with me on LinkedIn and subscribe to my YouTube channel.
item.author: shehanat
